Â 

9. The EUâs role in supporting the recent peace efforts for Gaza and a two-state solution (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is Council and Commission statements on the EUâs role in supporting the recent peace efforts for Gaza and a two-state solution (2025/2899(RSP)).

It's not an easy issue, so colleagues, please quieten down. You are worried about what's going on in Gaza, but you are not showing any respect for the debate that we are going to have about it.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, today, on 7Â October, we mark two years since Hamas' terrible terrorist attack against Israel.

We remember the 1Â 200 innocent civilians who lost their lives.Â We also remember the 251 hostages taken on that day, 7Â OctoberÂ 2023. Some have since been brought back, but with traumas for life. Others have sadly been killed and some remain in cruel captivity for two years now. Our thoughts are with all the hostages and their loved ones. Some of you family members are here with us today. I have no words for your pain. Thank you for being with us in Strasbourg.

The EU welcomes the US President's commitment to ending the war in Gaza and we are hopeful for the negotiations currently taking place in Sharm el-Sheikh in Egypt. Finally, there is a renewed hope for having the hostages released and putting an end to the tremendous suffering of the civilians in Gaza. We strongly urge all parties to seize the opportunity, not least Hamas.

We desperately need a ceasefire, the unconditional release of all hostages and unhindered humanitarian access to Gaza. Once conditions are in place, the enormous task of reconstruction lies ahead. The EU stands ready to play an active role due to our consistent commitment, including as the largest humanitarian donor in Gaza and biggest provider of assistance to the Palestinian Authority.

We must also build on the political momentum to find a credible pathway towards a twoâstate solution where two states, Israel and Palestine, can live side by side in peace and security.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dubravka Å uica, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, dear Members of the European Parliament, as you heard, on this day two years ago, we woke up to the terrible news of an attack that caused at least 1Â 195 victims and led to 251 hostages that have been held captive by Hamas. I want to welcome the members of the families of the hostages who are present today in Parliament, maybe not in this chamber.

In these two years, pain and suffering have been the reality of Israelis and Palestinians. Heartbreaking images are spreading all over the world. In these two years, violence and war have continued, causing more than 65Â 000 reported Palestinian victims and instability in the broader Middle East region. In these two years, the humanitarian situation in Gaza has become unbearable.

President von der Leyen recalled during her State of the Union address, one month ago, that what is happening in Gaza has shaken the conscience of the world. The European Union has continuously called for a ceasefire, for the release of all remaining hostages and the resumption of humanitarian aid to the Gaza Strip, working tirelessly with regional and international partners.

Honourable Members, I would like to once again reiterate that the European Union has been, and is, the largest humanitarian donor in Gaza. Two weeks ago, the Commission announced an additional EURÂ 50Â million in emergency humanitarian aid to deliver lifeâsaving assistance both in Gaza and the West Bank. This will bring the total humanitarian allocation to Palestine to almost EURÂ 220Â million this year.

Returning from an exceptionally important UN General Assembly week, it is clear for me that we need to be unequivocal in our support of the multilateral system. In this context, we believe that only with the United Nations and its agencies in a central role can the badly needed humanitarian surge work. In this regard, the Commission takes good note of your plenary resolution of 11Â September, where you called for the mandate and funding of UNRWA to be reinstated in full, with strong oversight and accountability.

Guaranteeing immediate support in the short term is essential, but we need to keep the longerâterm political perspective in mind. Europe's goal has always been the same: real security for Israel and a safe present and future for all Palestinians. We are now at the key moment of the decision of whether this terrible war will finally come to an end.

The European Union has welcomed the 20âpoint plan on Gaza presented by the United States. We remain a steadfast proponent of peace in the Middle East. The plan presents a credible framework in this direction. The European Union stands behind the plan's critical elements. First, no role for Hamas â no Hamas â, no displacement, no annexation â including of the West Bank â, no threats emanating from Gaza to its neighbours and no military operations.Â A number of elements are now being negotiated in detail but the European Union will play its part in the implementation of the plan. We are encouraged by Israel's positive reaction to the peace plan and call on its leadership to work towards its implementation in good faith.

We also note Hamas's stated readiness to release all hostages and end the conflict, while urging it to engage credibly and constructively in the process, including through disarmament, and the renunciation of any claim to post-war governance. We also welcome the joint statement by Arab and Muslim foreign ministers. Regional leadership will be a critical enabler in every step of the implementation of this plan.

It is important, however, to emphasise that transitional arrangements should pave the way for lasting and sustainable peace. A credible pathway towards Palestinian statehood and the twoâstate solution will be key, based on the principles endorsed by a large majority of UN member states. The European Union supports a clear role for the Palestinians in the future of Gaza. To this end, the European Union will continue to support the Palestinian Authority to implement the needed reforms as they have been proposed by the Palestinians themselves. The Palestine Donor Group, announced by President von der Leyen in this House, will serve as a forum to check progress on this endeavour.

Let me recall that the European Union is the biggest provider of external assistance to the Palestinians. We presented a programme worth EURÂ 1.6Â billion for 2025 to 2027, with the aim to support the Palestinian Authority and the Palestinian population. The emergency funding package, announced in the margins of the UN General Assembly, is an important step for stabilising the fiscal situation of the Palestinian Authority, and it can be dispersed through the European Union's robust funding system, 'PEGASE'. In New York, we signed a EURÂ 400Â million loan from the European Investment Bank to the Palestine Monetary Authority. This loan will support small and medium enterprises in the West Bank that are the backbone of the economy.

The European Union is also ready to support the Palestinianâled recovery and reconstruction of Gaza through dedicated instruments, as announced by President von der Leyen. This needs to be coordinated and discussed with all stakeholders in the context of the economic development plans proposed by the United States President. The Palestine Donor Group, which will hold its first meeting soon, by the end of the year, including regional partners, will be crucial in bringing together European initiatives and coordination efforts.

Beyond supporting governance agreements, the European Union can make concrete contributions to the implementation of the plan via various instruments at its disposal. Achieving demilitarisation and effective transitional governance and establishing an international stabilisation force are vital. This all will require careful coordination and stakeholder engagement. We have experience in this respect and we can bring it to the table. First, the European Union could support the proposed interfaith dialogue and the platform via existing peacebuilding programmes. And the European Union is also well positioned to continue supporting regional integration in the Middle East.

To end, dear colleagues, dear Members, the tragedy in Gaza must end. Now is the time to begin the process of building a better future for Israelis, for Palestinians and for the entire region.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hildegard Bentele, on behalf of the PPE Group. â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Commissioner, dear colleagues, now or never. A genuine diplomatic breakthrough is on the table, shaped by President Trump and broadly backed by the Arab and Muslim world, including those with the most influence on Hamas.

The plan demands something from both sides, even if it is hard to accept that terrorists sit at the table at all. The plan puts strong emphasis on what matters most to us: better humanitarian aid delivery. Every day counts for civilians in Gaza and the hostages. Therefore, both sides must be pressed to agree quickly. I welcome those European leaders, including Germany, who are engaging to help finalise the plan.

For the EU, the plan is also a wake-up call. While Brussels talks about sanctions and cutting trade, the plan creates economic incentives, a special economic zone, lower tariffs and investment opportunities â for everybody.

And what has become the pet project of many here â a Palestinian state under PA rule â is mentioned only as a potential result of a long process. The plan, rather, establishes a board of independent Palestinian experts and, in general, international oversight over Gaza.

High Representative Kallas often likes to state that there is no military solution. But if Hamas refuses to lay down arms â what then? Should Israel live under permanent threat?

And in conclusion â especially today, on the second anniversary of 7Â October â I really wish that our European institutions showed the same empathy for the traumatised Israeli people and the unsettled Jewish communities worldwide as they show for the Palestinian cause.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Iratxe GarcÃ­a PÃ©rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±or presidente, hoy se cumplen dos aÃ±os del ataque terrorista de HamÃ¡s, que dejÃ³ a 1Â 195 personas asesinadas y 251 rehenes. Dos aÃ±os desde aquella jornada de horror que cambiÃ³ la vida para siempre en Israel y en toda la regiÃ³n. Quiero comenzar recordando a las vÃ­ctimas, a sus familias, a quienes hemos abrazado aquÃ­, en Estrasburgo, y en Israel, cuando visitÃ© el horror de los kibutz y del festival de mÃºsica donde la barbarie se desatÃ³. Nunca olvidaremos su dolor ni dejaremos de exigir la liberaciÃ³n de todos los rehenes.

Pero el terrorismo no puede combatirse con un genocidio. No se derrota el terror asesinando a mÃ¡s de 67Â 000 palestinos, entre ellos 20Â 000Â niÃ±os y niÃ±as; ni convirtiendo a Gaza en una tierra arrasada donde dos millones de personas sobreviven sin agua, sin medicamentos, sin luz, sin alimentos y sin esperanza. El terrorismo se derrota desde el Estado de derecho. Europa lo sabe. Derrotamos el terrorismo en todas sus formas. Lo derrotamos con justicia, no con venganza.

Por eso, ante el clamor de millones de ciudadanos que se manifiestan en las calles de nuestras ciudades exigiendo el final del genocidio, la UniÃ³n Europea no puede permanecer inmÃ³vil. Y ante la valentÃ­a de hombres y mujeres que a bordo de la flotilla humanitaria han intentado romper el cerco de Gaza y llevar ayuda a quienes agonizan, no podemos quedarnos callados mientras son cobardemente detenidos por el Gobierno de Israel.

La ComisiÃ³n ha dado pasos, pero el Consejo debe actuar ya suspendiendo el acuerdo de asociaciÃ³n con Israel, imponiendo sanciones al Gobierno de Netanyahu y declarando un embargo de armas. Y debe hacerlo junto con un mecanismo para Gaza con el fin de reconstruir lo destruido y devolver la dignidad al pueblo palestino.

El plan de Trump no puede servir Ãºnicamente para parar la guerra y devolver los rehenes. Cualquier plan de paz debe servir tambiÃ©n para abrir de una vez por todas el camino hacia la creaciÃ³n del Estado palestino, porque sin ese horizonte no habrÃ¡ paz posible. Y el principal obstÃ¡culo para la paz es Netanyahu. Porque Netanyahu ha reconocido haber financiado a HamÃ¡s para hacer imposible la soluciÃ³n de los dos Estados.

Europa debe elegir complicidad o coraje. Y el coraje consiste en defender la Ãºnica soluciÃ³n posible: dos pueblos, dos Estados, que vivan en paz y en igualdad.

Paremos el genocidio. Que hable la humanidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AntÃ³nio TÃ¢nger CorrÃªa, em nome do Grupo PfE. â Senhor Presidente, Senhora Ministra, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, meus caros colegas, aleluia!Â Fez-se luz.

HÃ¡ anos que venho a defender uma administraÃ§Ã£o internacional para Gaza e, em princÃ­pio, para toda a Autoridade Palestiniana. E agora que o plano dos americanos veio Ã  liÃ§a, tornou-se incontornÃ¡vel essa administraÃ§Ã£o, que penso ser extremamente positiva, pois sem ela nÃ£o haverÃ¡ paz, nÃ£o haverÃ¡ estabilidade. Penso que Ã© esse o caminho que devemos seguir, nÃ£o sÃ³ para a Faixa de Gaza, mas tambÃ©m para os territÃ³rios da CisjordÃ¢nia.

A UniÃ£o Europeia tem tido uma conduta lamentÃ¡vel relativamente ao MÃ©dio Oriente: tem dado dinheiro, tem perdido o controlo do dinheiro; constroem-se tÃºneis com o dinheiro da UniÃ£o Europeia, e a ajuda nÃ£o chega Ã s populaÃ§Ãµes; as populaÃ§Ãµes tÃªm fome e sÃ£o as menos defendidas de todas.

Esta ajuda, que a senhora comissÃ¡ria veio anunciar, vai ser feita atravÃ©s de quem? Das NaÃ§Ãµes Unidas? Do Hamas? Porque, nÃ£o se esqueÃ§am que existe uma coisa chamada sÃ­ndrome de Estocolmo ââ e podem ter a certeza de que grande parte da populaÃ§Ã£o de Gaza e grande parte da CisjordÃ¢nia, da Autoridade Palestiniana, estÃ£o cada vez maisÂ ao lado do Hamas. Acho bem que nÃ£o se esqueÃ§am disso.

Quanto Ã  soluÃ§Ã£o dos dois Estados, vamos reconhecer o quÃª? SÃ³ poderÃ¡ haver um Estado Palestiniano se esse Estado reconhecer o Estado de Israel. Foi por isso que falharam as cinco tentativas no passado e Ã© por isso que falharÃ¡ esta, caso os palestinianos nÃ£o reconheÃ§am o Estado de Israel.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. â Voorzitter, collegaâs, wie echt vrede wil voor Jood en Palestijn âÂ en dat willen we toch allemaalÂ â die moet Trumps vredesplan wel omarmen. Alle ingrediÃ«nten om tot een duurzame vrede te kunnen komen, zitten er immers in. IsraÃ«l gaat al akkoord, nu Hamas nog. Zijn zij nu eindelijk bereid de gijzelaars daadwerkelijk vrij te laten? Alles staat of valt daarmee. Gaat deze deur niet open, dan blijft de weg naar vrede gesloten, ben ik bang. In plaats van IsraÃ«l te sanctioneren, moet daarom alles erop gericht zijn dat Hamas de gijzelaars laat gaan.

Het vervolg zal vast een weg worden boordevol kuilen en nieuwe obstakels. De traumaâs aan beide kanten zijn immers immens. De kans die het nu voorliggende plan biedt om weer iets van een vredesproces op gang te brengen en een nieuwe 7Â oktober te voorkomen, mogen wij echter niet laten passeren.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bernard Guetta, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, la paix, les deux Ãtats... Non, pas encore. Mais si c'Ã©tait au moins l'arrÃªt des bombardements, un cessez-le-feu et la libÃ©ration des otages, quel bonheur ce serait! Croisons les doigts. EspÃ©rons.

Mais cherchons aussi Ã  comprendre: pourquoi Donald Trump a-t-il soudain tordu le bras Ã  Benjamin Netanyahu? Pourquoi a-t-il redonnÃ© aux Ãtats-Unis ce rÃ´le d'arbitre auquel il avait renoncÃ© sous sa prÃ©sidence? C'est simple. Les pays du Golfe lui avaient dit: Â«Si IsraÃ«l annexe la Cisjordanie, ce sera la fin des accords d'AbrahamÂ», le seul succÃ¨s qu'ait jamais enregistrÃ© Donald Trump. Â«Si IsraÃ«l ne s'excuse pas d'avoir bombardÃ© Doha, avaient-ils ajoutÃ©, ce sera la fin du contrat de confiance entre le Golfe et la Maison-Blanche.Â»

Puis les EuropÃ©ens, France et Grande-Bretagne en tÃªte, ont Ã©galement modifiÃ© la donne en amplifiant le mouvement de reconnaissance de la Palestine. HumiliÃ© par Vladimir Poutine en Ukraine, Donald Trump risquait de perdre pied au Proche-Orient. Soudain isolÃ©, il a dÃ», comme tout un chacun, tenir compte d'un rapport de force.

Nous autres, EuropÃ©ens, devons en tirer la leÃ§on. Face Ã  des hommes qui nous veulent si peu de bien, face Ã  Donald Trump et Vladimir Poutine, nous avons alors montrÃ© nos muscles et notre force en nous trouvant de nouveaux alliÃ©s et en ne permettant plus, plus une seule fois, la moindre incursion dans notre espace aÃ©rien.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Villy SÃ¸vndal, for Verts/ALE-Gruppen. â Hr. formand! I dag for to Ã¥r siden udfÃ¸rte Hamas et brutalt terrorangreb. Endnu et traume er blevet tilfÃ¸jet den jÃ¸diske historie. Mine tanker gÃ¥r til de ramte familier og gidslerne, og der er ingen undskyldninger for at drÃ¦be uskyldige civile. Israels svar var tonstunge bomber, der har Ã¸delagt alt. Et uhyrligt antal af drÃ¦bte, sÃ¥rede og traumatiserede. Og Israel har blokeret for nÃ¸dhjÃ¦lp. Der er ingen undskyldninger for drab af uskyldige civile. Vi ser en menneskeskabt hungersnÃ¸d og et handlingslammet EU. I dette menneskeretsvakuum er europÃ¦ere i mange lande gÃ¥et pÃ¥ gaden med krav om sanktioner, med krav om konsekvenser. Flere hundrede, heriblandt nogle af vores kolleger, har forsÃ¸gt at bryde blokaden og bringe nÃ¸dhjÃ¦lp med skib til Gaza. OgsÃ¥ her brÃ¸d Israel den internationale lov. Der er brug for permanent vÃ¥benhvile og nÃ¸dhjÃ¦lp. Der er brug for, at alle anerkender, at palÃ¦stinenserne har ret til at leve i en bÃ¦redygtig stat. BesÃ¦ttelse og krigsforbrydelser skal have konsekvenser. Og israelerne og palÃ¦stinenserne skal kunne se hinanden som medmennesker og naboer. Trumps mulige fred er bedre end bomber, men og det er afgÃ¸rende: Freden skal sikre palÃ¦stinensernes ret til at forblive i Gaza. Den totale mangel pÃ¥ europÃ¦isk handling i den her sammenhÃ¦ng er en skamfuld historie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lynn Boylan, on behalf of The Left Group. â A UachtarÃ¡in, the legacy of the Palestinian genocide, the systematic failure of political leadership and, in some cases, the EU's act of complicity will have ramifications for decades. First and foremost, the profound trauma of mass displacement, of the loss of multiple generations of families, of tens of thousands of orphans, of forced starvation will be catastrophic.

Palestinians may never be able to forgive the Western and Arab world, but it is not just Palestinians. Across Europe, young and old have taken to the streets in ever increasing numbers. They have been beaten and criminalised for standing up for international law. More than 100 civilians, EU civilians and three Members of this House were illegally abducted by Israel for trying to do what their governments should have been doing, which was to end the illegal blockade, and neither the Council nor the Commission thought to mention them today.

How is this that a small, oppressive foreign regime built on racism and violent ideology has been able to infiltrate our institutions and dominate our foreign and domestic policy to create a situation where the freedoms, interests and wishes of our own citizens have been trampled on?

We absolutely need the killing to stop, but this peace plan is colonialism. Gaza does not need a viceroy, and certainly not one who prosecuted an illegal war in Iraq. Gaza needs to be free from occupation, and Palestinians need to be treated with respect and given the autonomy for their own future. International law is the only framework for peace. Palestinians need more than EU money. They need justice.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Donald Trump hat einen Friedensplan fÃ¼r den Nahen Osten vorgelegt, und Friedrich Merz zÃ¼ckt schon mal das Scheckbuch. Der Wiederaufbau von Gaza soll von deutschen Steuerzahlern bezahlt werden. Es ist dasselbe Prinzip wie in Syrien, Afghanistan oder in der Ukraine â die Amerikaner machen Deals, Deutschland zahlt. 12Â Milliarden Euro Wiederaufbauhilfe haben wir an Syrien Ã¼berwiesen, 17Â Milliarden an Afghanistan, 500Â Milliarden soll der Wiederaufbau der Ukraine kosten.

Seit den Zeiten von Helmut Kohl bedient sich die deutsche AuÃenpolitik der Scheckbuchdiplomatie. Deutschland hat nichts zu melden, dafÃ¼r zahlen wir aber alles und jeden. Problem nur: Das Scheckbuch von Friedrich Merz ist nicht mehr gedeckt. Im Bundeshaushalt klafft eine gigantische LÃ¼cke. 170Â Milliarden Euro fehlen, trotz 1Â 000Â Milliarden Euro neuer Schulden. Der Bundeskanzler will deshalb, dass die Deutschen erst mit 70 in Rente gehen, er will die Steuern erhÃ¶hen und pflegebedÃ¼rftigen Senioren die Hilfe streichen, nur damit er weiter Geld im Ausland verteilen kann. Das wird nicht lÃ¤nger funktionieren. Wir werden die Geldgeschenke an Gaza stoppen und die Interessen der Deutschen wieder zur StaatsrÃ¤son machen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Antonio LÃ³pez-IstÃºriz White (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear Commissioner, two years after the horrible terrorist attacks of 7Â October, we honour the memory of the victims and we say that we want the hostages back home in Israel.

While European politicians were picking sides, using the pain of the Palestinian and the Israeli people for their political games, on the other side of the Atlantic, President Trump was engaged in serious talks, trying to negotiate the terms of a peace plan that could bring a new era of progress and prosperity for the Middle East. That same person who, in his first mandate â while many in Europe were criminally silent about it â was working for the normalisation of relations between the Arab countries and Israel through the Abraham Accords.

This peace plan brings to the table the possibility for a stable ceasefire, which will put an end to the suffering of millions of civilians in Gaza, including the muchâneeded access to humanitarian aid. It will also allow the return of hostages to their families. It will create a solid foundation for peace and implementation of the twoâstate solution by removing the terrorist organisation Hamas from the equation.

I know it is difficult for Greta Thunberg and the Spanish 'Barbie of Gaza' on those boats, screaming and partying in front of the cameras. So it's over. No more excuses for socially accepted antisemitic behaviour. The Arab countries in the region are very much aware that further confrontation is not the solution. Normalisation, dialogue and trade is. They seek peace and progress. Let us Europeans do the same.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Annalisa Corrado (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, mentre discutiamo in quest'Aula, a Gaza continua a consumarsi un disastro umanitario che non Ã¨ una fatalitÃ , ma il frutto di precise scelte politiche disumane e criminali: bombardamenti, assedi, deportazioni, senza che esista un luogo sicuro, fame usata come arma di guerra.

Questo Parlamento e queste istituzioni non possono fingere che non esista un genocidio in corso, denunciato dalle Nazioni Unite. Non possono non ascoltare i milioni di cittadine e cittadini europei scesi in piazza in queste settimane. Le responsabilitÃ  del governo israeliano non possono essere coperte da un silenzio complice.

In queste ore siamo appesi a un filo sottile con la discussione del piano di pace promosso dagli Stati Uniti. Ogni spiraglio va esplorato e ogni sforzo diplomatico va posto in campo, ma non illudiamoci, perchÃ© la pace non si costruisce imponendo condizioni dall'alto, negando la voce e la dignitÃ  del popolo palestinese.

Senza il riconoscimento della Palestina, senza un processo politico che ponga fine alle occupazioni illegali, ogni accordo resterÃ  fragile e non si estirperanno le radici dell'odio. Devono tacere le armi, deve cessare il massacro del popolo palestinese e gli ostaggi israeliani devono essere liberati subito. Nessuno creda che esista un altro modo accettabile di procedere.

La distribuzione degli aiuti deve essere garantita da canali sicuri e istituzionali credibili, nel solco del diritto internazionale. Era questo, esattamente questo, l'obiettivo della Global Sumud Flotilla, che Ã¨ stata fermata illegalmente in acque internazionali con centinaia di civili europei e attivisti deportati, torturati, maltrattati.

L'Europa deve smettere di oscillare tra ipocrisie e connivenze. Bisogna fermare il genocidio, bisogna far tacere le armi, sospendere gli accordi di associazione con Israele, liberare gli ostaggi e dare corpo alla prospettiva di due popoli e due Stati in pace e in sicurezza, costruendo quella pace duratura che Ã¨ il modo migliore per onorare e ricordare tanto le 65Â 000 vittime palestinesi quanto le vittime dei vili attacchi terroristici contro inermi civili compiuti da Hamas in questa data due anni fa.

Â

Â Â Alberico Gambino (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, il piano di pace proposto dagli Stati Uniti rappresenta un tentativo concreto di garantire stabilitÃ  e sviluppo nella Striscia di Gaza. L'Unione europea deve sostenerlo con pragmatismo e unitÃ  di intenti. Come ricordato dalla Presidente von der Leyen, l'Europa Ã¨ pronta a contribuire con risorse politiche, economiche e umanitarie. Questo impegno deve rafforzare la prospettiva di una coesistenza pacifica tra due Stati, fondata appunto sul rifiuto della violenza e sul reciproco riconoscimento.

Il governo italiano, tra l'altro, ha indicato una linea chiara: Hamas non puÃ² ricoprire nessun ruolo nel futuro governo di Gaza, ma Ã¨ altrettanto importante evitare che la sofferenza del popolo palestinese non venga strumentalizzata per fini ideologici.

Il cammino verso la pace a Gaza resta difficile, ma oggi si intravede uno spiraglio di luce e di speranza: la possibilitÃ  di restituire ai civili un futuro di sicurezza e di dignitÃ .

Â Â Barry Andrews (Renew). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, colleagues, 7Â October must be repeatedly and unambiguously condemned. I wish to share my sympathy with the bereaved families, with the injured and those taken hostage.

The response that followed, aimed at wiping out Hamas, soon warped into a policy of regional vandalism, bombing and occupying neighbours, thrilling in the awesome power of it all, seemingly possessed of a drunken selfâconfidence.

The sickening policy that sees children being killed by AI drones, by IDF snipers and been starved to death, as acknowledged by PresidentÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen, is not his strategy. Rather, it is an aimless display of military prowess. So much of the current Israeli leadership are so in awe of this that statesmanship, vision and a genuine desire for peace are not valued.

So let us genuinely hope that this peace process can lead to an end to the violence for peace and security for Palestinians and Israelis alike, and that the EU can play its role in ensuring that there is a pathway to selfâdetermination for the people of Palestine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi, 7 ottobre, Ã¨ l'anniversario di un attacco terribile e sanguinoso, che condanno senza remore. Ma allora perchÃ© voi non condannate l'8, il 9, il 10 e l'11 ottobre? PerchÃ©, dopo due anni di genocidio palestinese e pulizia etnica, ancora non facciamo niente per fermare Israele? PerchÃ© vi siete rifiutati di parlare della Flotilla se non avete a cuore i diritti degli europei che rappresentate, delle vostre colleghe? Allora come quest'Aula potrÃ  fare qualcosa per il popolo palestinese?

Presidente von der Leyen, ieri ha detto che l'Europa deve rimanere unita per rimanere viva. Io Le dico che l'Europa sta morendo nel silenzio e nella complicitÃ  di queste istituzioni. Ma c'Ã¨ un'altra Europa, viva nelle milioni di persone che sono nelle nostre strade, che gridano "non nel nostro nome". Un'Europa che crede nella giustizia, nel diritto, nella pace, che non vuole ripetere gli errori del passato e che si oppone davvero al genocidio.

Noi stiamo con quell'Europa, con il popolo palestinese oppresso. Ora Ã¨ il momento di mettere in pratica i valori che diciamo di difendere, altrimenti rimanete nella vostra ipocrisia, ma noi saremo in quelle strade, su quelle barche, noi saremo con l'umanitÃ , perchÃ© lÃ¬ l'umanitÃ  vive, qui l'umanitÃ  muore.

Â Â Marc Botenga (The Left). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Madame la Commissaire, dans votre discours, vous avez oubliÃ© l'expression Â«sanctionner IsraÃ«lÂ». Pourquoi ne sanctionnez-vous toujours pas IsraÃ«l, deuxÂ ans aprÃ¨s la demande explicite de la Cour internationale de justice? Celle-ci avait pourtant indiquÃ© que des Ãtats tiers se faisaient complices en continuant Ã  financer IsraÃ«l.

Aujourd'hui, l'Union europÃ©enne finance IsraÃ«l par l'intermÃ©diaire de fonds de recherche, de fonds militaires, de la Banque europÃ©enne d'investissement ou encore de son programme spatial. Mais vous ne sanctionnez pas IsraÃ«l. J'ai entendu MmeÂ KajaÂ Kallas dÃ©clarer que l'Union europÃ©enne devait avoir sa place dans l'autoritÃ© transitoire pour Gaza, aux cÃ´tÃ©s de criminels de guerre comme TonyÂ Blair et DonaldÂ Trump.

C'est cela, le colonialisme. Ce que l'on voit aujourd'hui dans votre Commission, c'est une intÃ©riorisation de la logique coloniale, oÃ¹ vous pensez que ce n'est pas aux Palestiniens, mais aux EuropÃ©ens, aux AmÃ©ricains, voire aux Britanniques, de gouverner Gaza.

Je voudrais que vous rompiez avec cela et que vous fassiez une seule chose: que vous reveniez ici pour dire: Â«Oui, nous allons proposer des sanctions contre IsraÃ«l.Â» IsraÃ«l doit Ãªtre sanctionnÃ© aujourd'hui, parce que, sinon, nous sommes complices des pires crimes de guerre.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR), blue-card question. â Mr Botenga, your colleague Boylan, in her speech mentioned the necessity to provide aid, humanitarian aid. We know there is a legal reason for the sea blockade, for obvious reasons. And, let alone, last week there were almost 1Â 500 aid trucks sent into Gaza. But could you please elaborate? And have you asked your colleagues who apparently were detained by the Israel Defence Forces what the reason was for MEP Hassan and others to toss their mobile phones overboard into the sea? What was so incriminating in their phones that they had to do such a thing?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marc Botenga (The Left), blue-card answer. â Yes, first of all, please go back and study international law before you mention it. There's a right of free passage, especially for humanitarian aid, so go study and don't come and waste our time with these kind of ridiculous questions.

Secondly, in regard to phones, you have one state that has been developing software to spy on Europeans that is committing a genocide in Gaza. Would you hand over all of your private data to them? No, you wouldn't. No one would.

So, rather than accuse the humanitarians, as you generally do, start accusing the genocidal maniacs that are today in Israel and are killing Palestinians.Â But no, you want to shoot on the boats with humanitarian aid. Go shame yourself.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

ÎÎ±Î¼Î¹Î¬ ÏÏÎ½ÎµÏÎ³Î±ÏÎ¯Î± Î¼Îµ ÏÎ¿ ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿Ï ÎºÎ±ÏÎ±ÎºÏÎ·ÏÎ® ÎÏÏÎ±Î®Î»! ÎÎµÏÏÎµÏÎ·, Î±Î½ÎµÎ¾Î¬ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ· Î Î±Î»Î±Î¹ÏÏÎ¯Î½Î· ÏÏÏÎ¯Ï ÎºÎ±ÏÎ¿ÏÎ® ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÎµÏÎ¿Î¹ÎºÎ¹ÏÎ¼Î¿ÏÏ, Î¼Îµ ÎµÎ´Î±ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® ÎµÎ½ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ±, ÏÏÎ± ÏÏÎ½Î¿ÏÎ± ÏÏÎ¹Î½ ÏÎ¿Î½ ÎÎ¿ÏÎ½Î¹Î¿ ÏÎ¿Ï 1967, Î¼Îµ ÏÏÏÏÎµÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ± ÏÎ·Î½ ÎÎ½Î±ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÎºÎ® ÎÎµÏÎ¿ÏÏÎ±Î»Î®Î¼.

Â Â Michael Gahler (PPE). â Mr President, colleagues, on the second anniversary of Hamas's terrorist attack on Israel, we strongly support the ongoing efforts to put an end to this war, to liberate the remaining hostages, to finally end the grip of Hamas on Gaza for all future time to come and to stop the suffering of the innocent civilian population in the Gaza Strip. We need immediate access for all available humanitarian assistance by all those organisations that have proven that they can deliver on the ground. Disarming Hamas, dismantling its structures will secure that humanitarian aid will reach those in need.

But our EU role must go beyond providing humanitarian assistance. We must, on the one hand, be prepared to agree to participate in an international administration that organises security and reconstruction in Gaza. I cannot suggest to Israel to continue its military presence inside Gaza, but suggesting that also requires a robust international presence and mandate, including us.

In parallel, we need to push for an inclusive peace process that ultimately leads to a twoâstate solution, a secure state of Israel and a viable Palestinian state. On the way, Israel must stop its settlement policy in the West Bank, and I hope that also in this regard Donald Trump and the European Council will find proper arguments to convince the Israeli Government that this is also in the longâterm interests of Israel. I also wish the Palestinian population to get the chance to elect a legitimate leadership, after the last elections dating from 2005 and 2006. Together, they can find a solution, hopefully.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â MrÂ Gahler, you have a blue card from MrÂ SellÂ from ESN, who has already taken the floor in this debate. Do you accept it?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael Gahler (PPE). â (start of speech off mic) ... floor cannot ask a blue card.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â No, the rules allow them to ask to take a blue card. But it's up to you.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael Gahler (PPE). â To our German extremists I don't give extra speaking time.

(The speaker declined to take a blue-card question from Alexander Sell)

ÎÏÏ ÏÎ·Î½ ÏÏÏÏÎ· ÏÏÎ¹Î³Î¼Î® Î±Î½Î±Î³Î½ÏÏÎ¯ÏÎ±Î¼Îµ ÏÎ¿ Î´Î¹ÎºÎ±Î¯ÏÎ¼Î± ÏÎ¿Ï ÎÏÏÎ±Î®Î» Î½Î± ÏÏÎµÏÎ±ÏÏÎ¯Î¶ÎµÏÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿Î½ ÎµÎ±ÏÏÏ ÏÎ¿Ï, Î±Î»Î»Î¬ Î±ÏÏÏ Î´ÎµÎ½ ÏÎ¿Ï Î´Î¯Î½ÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ¿ Î´Î¹ÎºÎ±Î¯ÏÎ¼Î± Î½Î± ÎºÎ±ÏÎ±ÏÎ±ÏÎ¬ ÏÎ¿ Î´Î¹ÎµÎ¸Î½Î­Ï Î´Î¯ÎºÎ±Î¹Î¿, Î±ÏÎ¿ÎºÎ»ÎµÎ¯Î¿Î½ÏÎ±Ï ÏÎ· ÎÎ¬Î¶Î±, ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ¹Î¼Î¿ÏÎ¿Î¹ÏÎ½ÏÎ±Ï ÏÎ·Î½ ÏÎµÎ¯Î½Î± ÏÏ ÏÏÎ»Î¿ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î´Î¹Î±ÏÏÎ¬ÏÏÎ¿Î½ÏÎ±Ï âÏÏÎ¼ÏÏÎ½Î± Î¼Îµ ÏÎ± ÎÎ½ÏÎ¼Î­Î½Î± ÎÎ¸Î½Î·â ÏÏÎ¬Î¾ÎµÎ¹Ï Î³ÎµÎ½Î¿ÎºÏÎ¿Î½Î¯Î±Ï.

ÎÎ¹ ÏÏÎ½Î¿Î¼Î¹Î»Î¯ÎµÏ ÏÎ¿Ï Î¾ÎµÎºÎ¯Î½Î·ÏÎ±Î½ ÏÎ·Î½ ÎÏÏÎ¹Î±ÎºÎ® ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÎÎ¯Î³ÏÏÏÎ¿ Î±Î½Î¿Î¯Î³Î¿ÏÎ½ Î­Î½Î± ÏÎµÏÎ¹Î¸ÏÏÎ¹Î¿ ÎµÎ»ÏÎ¯Î´Î±Ï Î³Î¹Î± ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ®Î½ÎµÏÏÎ· ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÏÎµÏÎ¹Î¿ÏÎ®: Î¼Î¹Î± ÏÏÎ¼ÏÏÎ½Î¯Î± ÏÎ¿Ï Î¸Î± Î¿Î´Î·Î³Î®ÏÎµÎ¹ ÏÎµ Î¬ÏÏÎ· ÏÎ¿Ï Î±ÏÎ¿ÎºÎ»ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ¼Î¿Ï ÏÎ·Ï ÎÎ¬Î¶Î±Ï, ÎºÎ±ÏÎ¬ÏÎ±ÏÏÎ· ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÏÏÏ, Î±ÏÎµÎ»ÎµÏÎ¸Î­ÏÏÏÎ· ÏÎ»ÏÎ½ ÏÏÎ½ Î¿Î¼Î®ÏÏÎ½, ÎµÎ½Ï Î¸Î± Î±Î½Î¿Î¯Î¾ÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ¿Î½ Î´ÏÏÎ¼Î¿ Î³Î¹Î± Î¼Î¹Î± Î´Î¹Î±ÏÎºÎ® ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ®Î½Î·, ÏÏÎ· Î²Î¬ÏÎ· ÏÎ·Ï Î»ÏÏÎ·Ï ÏÏÎ½ Î´ÏÎ¿ ÎºÏÎ±ÏÏÎ½: Î­Î½Î± ÎÏÏÎ±Î®Î» ÎµÎ»ÎµÏÎ¸ÎµÏÎ¿ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î±ÏÏÎ±Î»Î­Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î­Î½Î± ÏÎ±Î»Î±Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î½Î¹Î±ÎºÏ ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿Ï Î²Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î¼Î¿ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î´Î·Î¼Î¿ÎºÏÎ±ÏÎ¹ÎºÏ, ÏÏÏÏ ÏÏÎ¿Î²Î»Î­ÏÎ¿ÏÎ½ ÏÎ± ÏÏÎ½Î¿ÏÎ± ÏÎ¿Ï 1967. ÎÏÏÏÏÏÏÏ, Î· ÎºÏÎ²Î­ÏÎ½Î·ÏÎ· ÎÎµÏÎ±Î½Î¹Î¬ÏÎ¿Ï Î­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î´Î·Î»ÏÏÎµÎ¹ Î±Î½Î¿Î¹ÏÏÎ¬ ÏÏÎ¹ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î±Î½ÏÎ¯Î¸ÎµÏÎ· ÏÏÎ·Î½ ÏÏÎ¿Î¿ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® ÎµÎ½ÏÏ Î±Î½ÎµÎ¾Î¬ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎ±Î»Î±Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î½Î¹Î±ÎºÎ¿Ï ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿ÏÏ. Î ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ¸Î·ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ½ ÏÎ±ÏÎ¬Î½Î¿Î¼ÏÎ½ ÎµÏÎ¿Î¹ÎºÎ¹ÏÎ¼ÏÎ½ âÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¹Î´Î¹Î±Î¯ÏÎµÏÎ± ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÎµÎ´Î¯Î¿Ï Î1â Î­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î±ÎºÏÎ¹Î²ÏÏ Î±ÏÏÏÎ½ ÏÎ¿Î½ ÏÏÏÏÎ¿: ÏÎ·Î½ Î±ÎºÏÏÏÏÎ· ÎµÏÎ¯ ÏÎ¿Ï ÏÎµÎ´Î¯Î¿Ï ÎµÎ½ÏÏ Î²Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î¼Î¿Ï ÏÎ±Î»Î±Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î½Î¹Î±ÎºÎ¿Ï ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿ÏÏ.

ÎÎ¹' Î±ÏÏÏ, ÎºÎ±Î»Î¿ÏÎ¼Îµ ÏÎ»Î± ÏÎ± ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ· Î¼Î­Î»Î· Î½Î± ÏÏÎ¿ÏÏÏÎ®ÏÎ¿ÏÎ½ ÏÎµ Î±ÏÏÎ® ÏÎ·Î½ ÏÏÎ¬Î¾Î· ÎµÏÎ¸ÏÎ½Î·Ï. Â Â Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, alors qu'aujourd'hui marque avec tristesse les deux ans de l'attaque duÂ 7Â octobreÂ 2023 perpÃ©trÃ©e par les terroristes du Hamas et d'autres factions armÃ©es, et acclamÃ©e par certains civils de Gaza, il est essentiel de rappeler les faits.

Ã Gaza, la vÃ©ritÃ© est brouillÃ©e par les mots. On parle de gÃ©nocide, mais ce que nous voyons, c'est un peuple pris en otage par le Hamas, une organisation terroriste qui refuse chaque trÃªve et transforme la souffrance en arme politique. Dans ce chaos, l'Union europÃ©enne se rÃ©fugie derriÃ¨re des dÃ©clarations creuses et des gestes symboliques. Elle reste incapable de dire clairement qui menace la paix.

Car la vÃ©ritÃ© est simple: il n'y aura pas de paix tant que le Hamas choisira la guerre plutÃ´t que la vie. L'Europe doit cesser de masquer son inaction et affirmer une vÃ©ritÃ© claire: la sÃ©curitÃ© des peuples et la protection des civils ne seront jamais garanties tant que le terrorisme dictera sa loi au Proche-Orient.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rihards Kols (ECR). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, you don't have to be pro-Israeli or pro-Palestinian, but you should be pro-fact.

We must not confuse immediate conflict management with long-term political solutions. The peace plan is a response to war. It outlines tactical steps: ceasefire, humanitarian aid, hostage release.

A two-state solution may sound noble, but what is being built? A state with no functioning institutions, no elections in nearly two decades and systematic corruption. This plan does not create a Palestinian state. It explicitly rules out Hamas â as it must. No serious state can emerge under the shadow of a terrorist organisation.

Rebuilding Gaza will take at least a decade. The question is not only 'who pays?', but 'who leads and guarantees the process?', because it certainly cannot â and should not â be the European Union. Regional countries â Egypt, Jordan, the Gulf â must take responsibility. Peace cannot be outsourced, nor can it be financed endlessly by Europe.

The EU's role must be principled, realistic and conditional â tied to regional ownership and general reform. Anything else is wishful thinking.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â ÐÐ»ÑÐ°Ð½ ÐÑÑÑÐº (Renew). â Ð-Ð½ ÐÑÐµÐ´ÑÐµÐ´Ð°ÑÐµÐ», Ð´Ð½ÐµÑ Ð½Ðµ Ð¼Ð¾Ð¶Ðµ Ð´Ð° ÑÐ¸ Ð¿Ð¾Ð·Ð²Ð¾Ð»Ð¸Ð¼ Ð´Ð° Ð³Ð¾Ð²Ð¾ÑÐ¸Ð¼ Ð·Ð° ÐÐ°Ð·Ð° ÑÑÑÐ¸ÑÐµ Ð¿ÑÐ°Ð·Ð½Ð¸ Ð´ÑÐ¼Ð¸, Ñ ÐºÐ¾Ð¸ÑÐ¾ ÑÐ²ÐµÑÑÑ ÑÐµ Ð¾Ð¿Ð¸ÑÐ²Ð° Ð´Ð° Ð¾Ð±Ð»ÐµÐºÑÐ¸ ÑÑÐ²ÐµÑÑÑÐ° ÑÐ¸ Ð²ÐµÑÐµ Ð´ÐµÑÐµÑÐ¸Ð»ÐµÑÐ¸Ñ. Ð¢ÐµÐ·Ð¸ Ð´ÐµÐ¹ÑÑÐ²Ð¸Ñ ÑÐ° Ð¿ÑÐ¸Ð¼ÐµÑ, Ð½Ð¾ ÐºÑÐ¼ ÑÐ¾Ð·Ð¸ Ð¼Ð¾Ð¼ÐµÐ½Ñ ÑÐ° ÑÐ°Ð¼Ð¾ Ð¿Ð¾ÑÐµÐ´Ð¸ÑÐ° Ð¾Ñ Ð¸Ð·Ð¾Ð»Ð¸ÑÐ°Ð½Ð¸ Ð¸Ð½Ð¸ÑÐ¸Ð°ÑÐ¸Ð²Ð¸. ÐÐ²ÑÐ¾Ð¿ÐµÐ¹ÑÐºÐ¸ÑÑ ÑÑÑÐ· ÑÑÑÐ±Ð²Ð° Ð´Ð° Ð¾ÑÑÐ°Ð½Ðµ ÑÐ²ÑÑÐ´Ð¾ Ð°Ð½Ð³Ð°Ð¶Ð¸ÑÐ°Ð½ Ñ Ð¿Ð¾ÑÑÐ¸Ð³Ð°Ð½ÐµÑÐ¾ Ð½Ð° ÑÑÐ°ÐµÐ½ Ð¸ ÑÐ¿ÑÐ°Ð²ÐµÐ´Ð»Ð¸Ð² Ð¼Ð¸Ñ, Ð¾ÑÐ½Ð¾Ð²Ð°Ð½ Ð½Ð° ÑÐµÑÐµÐ½Ð¸ÐµÑÐ¾ Ð·Ð° Ð´Ð²Ðµ Ð´ÑÑÐ¶Ð°Ð²Ð¸. Ð©Ðµ Ð¿ÑÐ¾Ð´ÑÐ»Ð¶Ð¸Ð¼ Ð´Ð° ÑÐ°Ð±Ð¾ÑÐ¸Ð¼ Ð·Ð°ÐµÐ´Ð½Ð¾ Ñ ÑÐµÐ³Ð¸Ð¾Ð½Ð°Ð»Ð½Ð¸ÑÐµ Ð¿Ð°ÑÑÐ½ÑÐ¾ÑÐ¸ Ð·Ð° Ð¿Ð¾ÑÑÐ¸Ð³Ð°Ð½ÐµÑÐ¾ Ð½Ð° ÑÑÑÐ¾Ð¹ÑÐ¸Ð² Ð¼Ð¸Ñ, ÐºÐ¾Ð¹ÑÐ¾ ÑÐµ Ð´Ð°Ð´Ðµ Ð²ÑÐ·Ð¼Ð¾Ð¶Ð½Ð¾ÑÑ Ð½Ð° Ð²ÑÐ¸ÑÐºÐ¸ Ð´Ð° Ð¶Ð¸Ð²ÐµÑÑ Ð² Ð¿ÑÐ¾ÑÐ¿ÐµÑÐ¸ÑÐµÑ Ð¸ ÑÐ²Ð¾Ð±Ð¾Ð´Ð¾Ð»ÑÐ±Ð¸Ð² Ð´ÑÑ.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannah Neumann (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, as we speak, families in Israel mourn the victims of 7 October while hostages remain in captivity, enduring hell.

As we speak, families in Gaza cannot grieve. More bombs are falling and settlers terrorise those in the West Bank.

As we speak, Jews in Europe face anti-Semitic attacks, racism against Muslims is rising and Palestinians worldwide wonder on which they their victims will be mourned.

Dear colleagues, we all hope for a deal to end this suffering. But negotiations do not feed the starving. We need to increase pressure for more humanitarian aid to reach Gaza. And even if an agreement comes, our work will not be over. Rebuilding will need resources. Peace will need protection. Healing will need specialists. And justice will need accountability.

Europe was too divided to pressure ceasefire. Let us at least be united and lead to protect and nurture whatever fragile hope may soon emerge.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Irene Montero (The Left). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora vicepresidenta, Â¿quÃ© tal su dÃ­a? Â¿QuÃ© tiene mÃ¡s importante en agenda que romper ya las relaciones con el Estado terrorista de Israel? El ejÃ©rcito israelÃ­ asalta y secuestra barcos europeos en aguas internacionales y aquÃ­ no pasa nada. Â¿QuÃ© hubiera pasado si es Rusia quien realiza este ataque directo contra paÃ­ses de la OTAN? Y si esto se lo hacen a Europa con impunidad, Â¿quÃ© no hacen con los palestinos? Impedir la entrada de comida y medicinas en Gaza es terrorismo de Estado. 680Â 000Â asesinados, niÃ±os quemados vivos, Gaza destruida por bombas europeas... y aquÃ­ no pasa nada. MÃ¡s de 10Â 000Â presos palestinos sometidos a ahogamientos simulados, ataques con perros, privaciÃ³n de comida, de sueÃ±o y de agua, violencia sexual... y aquÃ­ no pasa nada.

Lo que han presentado Trump y Netanyahu es un plan de colonizaciÃ³n y negocio para ser los dueÃ±os de Palestina, y es un plan ilegal porque no reconoce a los palestinos los derechos de autodeterminaciÃ³n y retorno a las tierras robadas por Israel. Rompan ya las relaciones con Israel âmilitares, econÃ³micas, comercialesâ. Rechacen el plan ilegal de Trump. Cumplan la legalidad internacional, que van a pasar ustedes a la historia como cÃ³mplices de un genocidio, y eso les convierte en criminales, vicepresidenta.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomasz Froelich (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Gaza ist die HÃ¶lle auf Erden: zehntausende Zivilisten tot, Kinder verhungern, die Stadt plattgebombt, die humanitÃ¤re Lage katastrophal. Das ist die brutale Antwort Israels auf den Terror der Hamas, der absolut unentschuldbar ist. Ãber 1Â 000Â Menschen massakriert, Geiseln leiden bis heute unvorstellbare Qualen, purer TerrorÂ â TÃ¤ter und Terror auf beiden Seiten. Trumps Plan kÃ¶nnte jetzt Frieden bringen: Waffenstillstand, Freilassung aller Geiseln, Entmachtung der Hamas, keine Vertreibung der PalÃ¤stinenser, sondern Aussicht auf eine ZweistaatenlÃ¶sung. Richtig so!

FÃ¼r uns EuropÃ¤er zÃ¤hlt vor allem eines: StabilitÃ¤t im Nahen Osten. Denn wir sind es, die die Folgen fremder Kriege immer wieder ausbaden mÃ¼ssen â Flucht, Massenmigration, Terror. Wir wollen das nicht. Deshalb dÃ¼rfen die PalÃ¤stinenser nicht vertrieben werden. Wir fordern ein Recht auf Heimat fÃ¼r unser Volk. In diesem Sinne stehen wir auch zum Recht auf Heimat fÃ¼r die PalÃ¤stinenser, und zwar in PalÃ¤stina, nicht in Europa. Das fordert auch Trumps Friedensplan. Setzen Sie sich dafÃ¼r ein, dass er realisiert wird. Mehr kann ein geopolitisch impotentes Europa leider nicht tun.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Massimiliano Salini (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il male seminato da Hamas e dal terrorismo dal 1987, quindi ben prima del 7 ottobre, e culminato nell'attacco del 7 ottobre, meritava e merita una reazione. Ma quella reazione non puÃ² essere un nuovo progetto di annessione o di occupazione della Striscia di Gaza, perchÃ© un progetto di annessione rappresenta terreno fertile per la rinascita di nuovi semi di male.

Quindi ben venga, assolutamente sÃ¬, il progetto di accordo di pace proposto dall'attuale amministrazione americana. Tuttavia, non basta quella proposta. Come giustamente - e la ringrazio - segnalava la Commissaria Å uica, noi abbiamo un ulteriore compito. Quell'accordo genererÃ  sicuramente, anzi probabilmente, un blocco delle armi e della guerra, ma non la pace. Per la pace ci vuole di piÃ¹, ci vuole la politica, ci vuole il dialogo, ci vuole il dialogo tra le culture, tra le religioni, tra i paesi.

Ã utile, a mio parere, che recuperiamo le parole pronunciate dal cardinale Pizzaballa in una recente intervista, dove parla della necessitÃ  che l'uomo riconosca l'altro uomo diverso da sÃ©, la sua cultura, il suo dolore. Questo Ã¨ il punto della politica. La pace si genera nel momento in cui il soggetto non ritiene di essere l'unica vittima, ma dentro il proprio dolore accetta di guardare anche il dolore dell'altro. E qui si gioca il futuro anche del compito politico dell'Unione europea, se vorrÃ  tornare ad essere quello per cui Ã¨ nata.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hana Jalloul Muro (S&D). â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Commissioner,Â today marks two years' of the Hamas terrorist attack that took the lives of 1Â 200 people and condemned the families of 250 hostages to despair. I want to convey our solidarity with the families of the hostages that are with us today. Believe us, we want more than anything for the hostages to be released and for there to be peace and security within a two-state solution for Israel and Palestine.

In this already-declared genocide by the United Nations, we are not only seeing the far-right government of Netanyahu killing more thanÂ 67Â 000 Palestinians, but destroying the last resort of democracy in Israel. Israelis are protesting in the streets; the world is protesting to end the suffering and impunity in Gaza.

Believe me, we need to end the occupation of Palestine, the occupation in the West Bank. We need to have access for the Palestinians to humanitarian aid. We need a democratic transition in a Palestinian state where Palestinians have an active role. We are not against Israel. We are not against Israel â I repeat this many times â because we are calling and defending the right of Palestinians to have their own state.

Peace is not merely the absence of war, it is the presence of justice. Let us avoid double standards, as we have always been reflecting in this Parliament, and fight for what is right. We need the implementation of international law and international humanitarian law, and if we are not on the right side of history, we will regret it all our lives.

Every day that passes, many Palestinians are being killed, and we have an uncertainty about the future of each one of the hostages that are still being held by Hamas. So I call here for peace, and for the implementation of an international humanitarian law that brings peace for both states. But we need to be clear and reaffirm and state our position with what is right: justice and security for each one of them; not for one side, but for both.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

IN THE CHAIR: CHRISTEL SCHALDEMOSE
Vice-President

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jorge MartÃ­n FrÃ­as (PfE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, el 7 de octubre de 2023, 1Â 200Â inocentes fueron asesinados por HamÃ¡s, hubo personas que fueron mutiladas, niÃ±os que vieron cÃ³mo ejecutaban a sus padres antes de enfrentarse ellos al mismo destino, mujeres violadas y exhibidas como trofeos, 250Â inocentes secuestrados... El mundo entero fue testigo de uno de los mayores atentados terroristas. El mundo entero fue testigo de cÃ³mo en algunas de nuestras plazas pÃºblicas se celebraba esa muerte. Y el mundo entero tambiÃ©n ha sido testigo de hasta quÃ© punto se ha retorcido y manipulado la verdad, presentando a las vÃ­ctimas como verdugos y a los verdugos como vÃ­ctimas.

La ComisiÃ³n, el Parlamento y algunos Estados miembros no han sido ajenos a esta gran mascarada. Prueba de ello es la Ãºltima resoluciÃ³n que votamos aquÃ­ y que obviaba, entre tanta palabra, la Ãºnica verdad: HamÃ¡s empezÃ³ la guerra. HamÃ¡s es responsable de las muertes en Gaza âque todos lamentamosâ. Y HamÃ¡s puede poner fin a la guerra si libera a los rehenes.

Si hoy estamos cerca de la paz, es gracias a la AdministraciÃ³nÂ Trump y a la generosidad ârepito, generosidadâ de Israel, que estÃ¡ dispuesto, una vez mÃ¡s, a buscar una paz que se le ha negado continuamente.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marco Squarta (ECR). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il piano proposto dal presidente Trump Ã¨ oggi l'unica luce nelle tenebre di un conflitto che, dal 7 ottobre, ha seminato dolore e distruzione. Ã un piano concreto che l'Italia, e con la forza di Giorgia Meloni, sostiene perchÃ© rappresenta l'unica via per una pace duratura e per porre fine a questa guerra assurda.

Noi non possiamo ignorare che la tragedia Ã¨ iniziata con la violenza di Hamas, ma non possiamo chiudere gli occhi davanti a risposte di Israele che si sono dimostrate sproporzionate e inaccettabili.

PerciÃ² oggi l'Europa ha un dovere: sostenere con coraggio il piano di pace e non lasciarsi distrarre da iniziative pericolose e strumentali, come flottiglie o manifestazioni varie, che non aiutano affatto il popolo palestinese, ma ne sfruttano la sofferenza per guadagnare visibilitÃ  e recuperare consenso che non hanno piÃ¹ in Italia e in Europa.

L'Europa non puÃ² scegliere la propaganda, deve scegliere la responsabilitÃ . Scegliamo la pace, non lo sfruttamento del dolore.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). â Madam President, today we are thinking about the victims of the terrorist attack on 7 October and their families. Peace is needed and it has been needed for the last 70 years.

So let's talk about the peace plan then, and here, I really need the Commission to listen, because this plan comes at a time when several international organisations and bodies of authority and human rights organisations have concluded that after two years of violence, the atrocities in Gaza meet the threshold for genocide.

What are the risks with this plan then?

First, it risks locking in impunity, because how can two parties that are deemed by the International Court of Justice being war criminals, actually will lead to responsibility?

Secondly, it risks to rewrite the responsibility of the attack, of what is going on.

Thirdly, the outcome will be for the powerful ones and not for the victims.

Fourth, the gains that are won on the battlefield will be permanent political gains. We all know that there cannot be peace without justice because it is very much needed...

(The President cut off the speaker)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, deux ans aprÃ¨s le 7Â octobre, nous rendons hommage aux victimes des massacres, aux otages encore retenus et Ã  leurs familles qui attendent toujours. Notre engagement pour leur libÃ©ration reste total. Mais celui du Premier ministre Netanyahu, lui, a failli. Il a instrumentalisÃ© la dÃ©tresse des familles pour justifier une guerre dÃ©vastatrice, abandonnant ainsi les siens pour poursuivre un objectif d'Ã©radication: celui du peuple palestinien Ã  Gaza.

Et pendant que nous dÃ©battons ici, l'Union europÃ©enne se fÃ©licite d'un soi-disant plan de paix faÃ§onnÃ© sans les Palestiniens, qui entÃ©rine l'occupation et organise la colonisation sous tutelle. Mais comment pouvez-vous parler de paix alors que le droit international est piÃ©tinÃ©, alors qu'une armÃ©e mÃ¨ne sous nos yeux un gÃ©nocide documentÃ©, revendiquÃ©, indÃ©niable?

Une vie vaut une vie. Oublier cette vÃ©ritÃ©, c'est renoncer Ã  notre humanitÃ©. C'est trahir les valeurs que nous dÃ©fendons depuis toujours. L'Europe ne peut pas rester immobile et complaisante; elle ne doit pas laisser faire. Ne lÃ¢chons ni le droit ni les sanctions et n'oublions aucune des victimes, israÃ©liennes comme palestiniennes.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Martins (The Left). â Senhora Presidente, testado em cenÃ¡rio real, Ã© a marca do armamento israelita. Um negÃ³cio que cresce como nunca.

A Europa Ã© o principal comprador, mas tambÃ©m hÃ¡ paÃ­ses Ã¡rabes a comprar. Morrem palestinianos. Gaza Ã© o terreno de ensaio da indÃºstria de morte. Um plano de paz Ã  medida deste negÃ³cio nÃ£o Ã© um plano de paz.

No plano de Trump e Netanyahu os palestinianos nÃ£o sÃ£o sequer ouvidos. O criminoso de guerra Tony Blair Ã© o capataz de serviÃ§o e Israel mantÃ©m nÃ£o sÃ³ todo o poder como toda a impunidade. Ã um plano de guerra e Ã© o fim do direito internacional e humanitÃ¡rio.

A Europa finge condenar, mas continua a armar o genocÃ­dio. Pelo meu paÃ­s passaram F-35 para Israel com autorizaÃ§Ã£o do governo portuguÃªs. O mÃ­nimo que se exige Ã  UniÃ£o Europeia Ã© menos cinismo e mais sanÃ§Ãµes efetivas.

Seguir o exemplo corajoso das flotilhas e mandar as suas frotas entregar ajuda humanitÃ¡ria a Gaza, quebrar o cerco, parar o genocÃ­dio, comprometer-se com a responsabilizaÃ§Ã£o dos criminosos de guerra. AÃ­ sim, poderemos ter um plano de paz com alguma credibilidade.

(A oradora aceita responder a uma pergunta Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ»)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE), Pergunta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Senhora Deputada, considera que a flotilha Sumud por Gaza foi defendida pela UniÃ£o Europeia, diplomÃ¡tica e tambÃ©m humanitariamente?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Martins (The Left), Resposta segundo o procedimento Â«cartÃ£o azulÂ». â Tenho a certeza de que a UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o fez o que devia. A UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o fez o que devia para defender a flotilha humanitÃ¡ria, porque eu nÃ£o esqueÃ§o que o MediterrÃ¢neo estÃ¡ cheio de barcos da Frontex que podiam ter acompanhado os barcos da flotilha, quebrado o cerco e levado ajuda humanitÃ¡ria a quem precisa.

A UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o fez o que precisava de fazer para defender a flotilha, porque aceitaria de algum paÃ­s, como aceitou de Israel, uma detenÃ§Ã£o criminosa em Ã¡guas internacionais e o tratamento desumano dado Ã queles detidos, que nem sequer o apoio de advogados, muitos deles, conseguiram ter?

E sei que a UniÃ£o Europeia nÃ£o fez o que devia fazer porque os Estados-Membros (Â«...Â»)

(a Presidente retira a palavra Ã  oradora)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Fidias Panayiotou (NI). â Madam President, finally, we have a peace plan for Gaza after millions of citizens across the world took to the streets and put pressure to their governments.

Even though this is overall good news, we still need to be very careful. This is because we shouldn't forget that the creators of this peace plan are Trump and Netanyahu. These are the same people who are still committing the biggest genocide of the 21st century against the Palestinians. The same people who are absolutely opposed to a two-state solution. The same people that might be ready to start a new colonial time in Gaza if this peace plan is not implemented properly.

So, our role as the European Union should be to keep pressuring the governments of Israel and the United States to respect all the points of the peace plan and make sure that the final result is the long-awaited two-state solution.

I love you all.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alice Teodorescu MÃ¥we (PPE). â Fru talman! Den 7 oktober tog aldrig slut. Inte fÃ¶r de som mÃ¶rdades, de som togs som gisslan eller de som hÃ¥ller andan i vÃ¤ntan pÃ¥ en liksÃ¤ck eller en spillra av en Ã¤lskad mÃ¤nniska som kanske snart fÃ¥r Ã¥tervÃ¤nda.Â Den fredsplan som president Trump presenterat och som Israel â liksom arabstaterna och Turkiet â genast stÃ¤llde sig bakom, Ã¤r vÃ¥r tids enda mÃ¶jlighet till fred. Gisslan fÃ¥r Ã¥tervÃ¤nda, Hamas avvÃ¤pnas, ingen ockupation och en ny sÃ¤kerhetsstyrka fÃ¶r Gaza, uppbackad av arabvÃ¤rlden.

EU bÃ¶r stÃ¥ bakom varje lÃ¶sning som innebÃ¤r att bÃ¤gge folkens rÃ¤tt att existera upprÃ¤tthÃ¥lls, samtidigt som den ideologiska grogrunden fÃ¶r terrorism, martyrskap och utplÃ¥ning av Israel en gÃ¥ng fÃ¶r alla elimineras. Gaza behÃ¶ver genomgÃ¥ samma process som Tyskland efter nazisterna. Annars Ã¤r det bara en tidsfrÃ¥ga innan andra radikala krafter Ã¥tertar makten.

De som motsÃ¤tter sig detta avtal fÃ¶r att de vÃ¤grar att rikta sina krav mot Hamas vÃ¤rnade aldrig om Gazas barn. Deras hyckleri Ã¤r en bekrÃ¤ftelse pÃ¥ att fred endast Ã¤r mÃ¶jlig nÃ¤r palestinierna Ã¤lskar sina barn mer Ã¤n vad de hatar ...

(Talmannen tog ifrÃ¥n talaren ordet)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â Madam President, I am at a loss for words to express my outrage at the passivity of the EU, to put an end to the genocide in Gaza, and to bring home the Israeli hostages.

Two years have passed. Passivity is complicity. By refusing to suspend the EUâIsraeli Association Agreement and rejecting calls for a weapon embargo, the EU is choosing complicity over conscience.

Now regarding the soâcalled peace efforts, all peace efforts must be welcomed. But there is no genuine peace effort when those most affected are excluded. The extremist Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and his far right ministers have made it clear that they will not allow the Palestinian Authority to have a role in Gaza. True peace cannot be achieved by excluding the Palestinians, while Hamas must not be part of the governance in Gaza.Â The Palestinian Authority must be if the peace efforts are to be genuine and credible.

Let us also remember the old saying, peace is not the absence of conflict, but the ability to cope with conflict by peaceful means. The people of the West Bank understand this deeply. They try to pursue peace through non-violent means, even when an ethnic cleansing is unfolding before our eyes in the West Bank, with the Israeli military and settlers terrorising Palestinians day and night.

The EU must take a stand for real peace resolution. That means ceasefire now. That means humanitarian aid into Gaza now. That means the immediate release of all hostages, both Israeli and Palestinian. That means an immediate end to the siege of Gaza. That means an end to the occupation of all Palestinian territories. That means recognition of Palestine. That means full respect for international law.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Irena Joveva (Renew). â MadamÂ President, now or never. Not tomorrow, not next week. Now or never. This is the moment for everyone who still defends the atrocities committed by the State of Israel in Palestine, to prove that there remains somewhere inside â I hope â a spark of humanity.

Sure, I welcome Trump's ceasefire plan, but only barely and only because it is less grotesque than the soâcalled Middle Eastern Riviera plan that he had. But where in this plan are the mechanisms for protection of Palestinian people and provisions safeguarding their further displacement? Instead of unification of the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, there is de facto separation of the two.

Also, let's be honest, plans alone do not bring justice. Words do not stop bombs. Speeches do not feed starving children. The international community has failed to act when it mattered most, so it must not fail now with holding Israel accountable.

So, I say again, colleagues, now or never. Let us prove that we are not just commentators or witnesses to tragedy, to a genocide. Now or never to stand on the side of humanity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tineke Strik (Verts/ALE). â Madam President, Council, Commissioner, across Europe record numbers of people have taken to the streets: Rome 1 million, Madrid 400Â 000, Amsterdam 250Â 000, Berlin and Barcelona 100Â 000.

Hundreds of vessels sailed to Gaza to break the unlawful humanitarian blockade. Evident opinion polls express people's frustration.

So all over Europe, we see an unprecedented disconnect between the people and politicians because they simply reject that their leaders and the EU remain silent and even facilitate genocide in Gaza and the ethnic cleansing in the West Bank.

This is in no way compliant with international law, in no way leading to peace, in no way what European citizens want. Our citizens do not allow you any longer to cowardly hide behind Trump or even ongoing negotiations. We need to go all out to stop the genocide, to enforce international law and include the Palestinians in any decision about their own land.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Danilo Della Valle (The Left). â Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, "vogliamo aiutare la Palestina. La Palestina sta dando una scossa alle nostre coscienze": queste sono le parole di un'anziana manifestante che ho incontrato a Roma durante l'imponente corteo di sabato. Un milione di persone scese in piazza che vorrei ringraziare una a una, come ringrazio i 400 attivisti della flottiglia, tra cui alcuni miei colleghi, e chiunque qui in Europa sta manifestando e scioperando a sostegno del popolo palestinese, mentre voi continuate ad essere complici dello Stato terrorista di Israele, restando in silenzio, continuando a intrattenere relazioni commerciali e militari con il criminale internazionale Netanyahu e adesso appoggiando il piano Trump per Gaza.

Un piano che sembra scritto da un notabile di fine Ottocento, dove si torna alle colonie e ai mandati, senza citare nemmeno minimamente il diritto dei palestinesi a decidere della propria terra e del proprio futuro, ancora una volta considerati esseri umani di serie B.

A differenza vostra, chi scende in piazza e chi manifesta il proprio dissenso sta salvando l'onore dell'Occidente. Un giorno a tutti voi verrÃ  chiesto di rendere conto di dove eravamo e cosa abbiamo fatto nei tempi in cui si consumava il genocidio a Gaza. E voi sarete sempre ricordati come i complici del genocidio. Palestina libera!

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). â Voorzitter, commissaris, Raad, exact twee jaar zijn we nu getuige van de gruwel die zich afspeelt in Palestina en in IsraÃ«l en van een ondraaglijke, menselijke tol van het extremisme van Hamas en de IsraÃ«lische regering. Inderdaad, er wordt vandaag opnieuw gesproken en dat moet ons hoopvol stemmen, maar tegelijkertijd moeten we realistisch zijn. Vrede is nog erg veraf, laat staan de oprichting van een Palestijnse staat. Elke dag sterven er nog mensen in Gaza en de Westelijke Jordaanoever.

Het mag niet zo zijn dat de Commissie nu zou stoppen met actie te ondernemen. De associatieovereenkomst moet nog steeds worden opgezegd en er moeten nog steeds sancties genomen worden tegen de extreemrechtse regering van Netanyahu. Tegelijkertijd moeten we uiteraard zorgen dat we meewerken aan dit vredesproces, met een duidelijk doel voor ogen: niet alleen een duurzame vrede, maar ook de tweestatenoplossing, met dus een Palestijnse staat en de erkenning van het recht van de Palestijnen voor de toekomst.

Opnieuw zijn massaal veel Europeanen, onder meer in Amsterdam, op straat gekomen. De Commissie moet luisteren naar haar eigen bevolking. Actie ondernemen dus.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Billy Kelleher (Renew). â Madam President, on the anniversary of 7Â October, we must remember the 1Â 200 people killed, the 251 hostages taken by Hamas, a terrorist organisation. We now need a ceasefire, a release of all hostages and humanitarian aid to flow into Gaza. And we need to support a two-state solution and a peace plan.

But let us be under no illusions. This conflict did not start in the last number of years. This has been ongoing for decades. The continual undermining and encroaching of the Palestinian territories by Israel has been continually allowed by this institution and many others in the European Union.

As I have said previously, Israel acts with impunity because we have granted it immunity. It is absolved from all its obligations under international law, under basic decency and humanity, and it continues to flout and we continue to bow our head and pretend we don't see. It is a shameful, shameful stain on this Parliament and on the institutions that make up the European Union. We have to act and stand in solidarity and support a two-state solution, and insist that Israel is held accountable...

(The President cut off the speaker)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, Â¿escucha la UniÃ³n Europea el clamor y la dignidad de las calles que desde Santiago de Compostela a Ãmsterdam piden que se acabe con el genocidio de Israel en Gaza? No. Â¿QuÃ© hizo la UniÃ³n Europea por defender la valentÃ­a de las personas que fueron en la flotilla humanitaria arriesgando su vida para denunciar el genocidio? Nada.

El tema que estÃ© moviendo al mundo es el genocidio en Gaza, pues lo que pase en Palestina condicionarÃ¡ las relaciones internacionales y el futuro de los derechos humanos. La pasividad de la presidenta Von der Leyen, su hipocresÃ­a e inacciÃ³n en estos dos aÃ±os demuestran la complicidad con el genocidio que comete Netanyahu.

Lo que estamos diciendo en las calles de toda Europa es que nos da vergÃ¼enza la posiciÃ³n de la UniÃ³n Europea. Sesenta mil personas asesinadas, bebÃ©s sin comida, hospitales destruidos, edificios bombardeados. De verdad, Â¿no tienen humanidad ni sensibilidad? No podemos esperar mÃ¡s: ruptura de relaciones diplomÃ¡ticas y comerciales, embargo de armas, suspensiÃ³n del Acuerdo de AsociaciÃ³n, sanciones al Estado de Israel y no al plan de Trump.

No podemos seguir permitiendo esta violaciÃ³n de derechos humanos, la ocupaciÃ³n y elÂ apartheid. Frente al odio, las mentiras y las burlas de la derecha y la extrema derecha: nuestra solidaridad. Stop...

(la presidenta retira la palabra a la oradora)

Â Â Li Andersson (The Left). â Arvoisa puhemies, kaksi vuotta kansanmurhaa Gazassa ja nyt rauhasta keskustellaan sellaisen suunnitelman pohjalta, joka toistaa historialliset vÃ¤Ã¤ryydet ohittamalla tÃ¤ysin palestiinalaiset ja heidÃ¤n itsemÃ¤Ã¤rÃ¤Ã¤misoikeutensa.

Â Â Francisco Assis (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora Ministra, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, neste dia 7 de outubro a minha primeira palavra Ã© para os sobreviventes do pogrom levado a cabo pelos impiedosos terroristas do Hamas hÃ¡ exatamente dois anos, para os feridos e para aqueles que continuam sequestrados, nÃ£o se sabe bem em que condiÃ§Ãµes.

A minha solidariedade vai tambÃ©m para as famÃ­lias israelitas em luto ou que vivem, desde entÃ£o, na angÃºstia quanto Ã  sorte dos seus entes queridos.

HÃ¡, contudo, hoje um vastÃ­ssimo consenso quanto Ã  desproporÃ§Ã£o gritante da resposta dada por Israel.

ApÃ³s dois anos de carnificina na Faixa de Gaza, parece surgir, finalmente, alguma luz ao fundo do tÃºnel. Parece haver um plano. TerÃ¡ para isso de haver abertura de ambos os lados para dar passos para concretizÃ¡-lo.

Notemos, de resto, que este avanÃ§o nÃ£o se deve Ã  UniÃ£o Europeia, que continua a desempenhar um papel de mero figurante num palco onde tinha obrigaÃ§Ã£o de ser um dos atores principais.

Talvez nÃ£o houvesse alternativa, ou nÃ£o parece haver alternativa, a esse plano, mas nÃ£o Ã© menos verdade â e nÃ£o podemos deixar de o dizer aqui â que este plano pode transportar consigo traÃ§os neocoloniais de muitÃ­ssimo mÃ¡ memÃ³ria. NÃ£o podemos, pois, aderir a este plano, em nenhuma circunstÃ¢ncia, de forma acrÃ­tica.

Â Â Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). â Voorzitter, collegaâs, vandaag staan we stil bij de gitzwarte dag van 7Â oktober 2023. Een dag die zonnig en vrolijk begon, maar die eindigde met de dood van 1Â 200Â onschuldige IsraÃ«liÃ«rs. Sinds die dag is het al twee jaar oorlog, met vele tienduizenden, voornamelijk onschuldige Palestijnse doden tot gevolg. Hoewel ik het recht op zelfverdediging van IsraÃ«l onderschrijf, is de reactie van Netanyahu volstrekt buitenproportioneel en onrechtmatig.

Nu ligt er een plan voor vrede. Een plan met heel veel haken en ogen. Het geeft echter wel, voor het eerst in twee jaar, een sprankje hoop. Een sprankje hoop dat de bommenregen stopt. Een sprankje hoop dat de overgebleven gijzelaars eindelijk vrijgelaten worden.

Europa is nauwelijks zichtbaar betrokken geweest, maar moet er nu wel op aandringen dat een akkoord mÃ©t de Palestijnen en niet alleen Ã³ver de Palestijnen gesloten wordt. Geen enkele vrede kan gesloten worden zonder actieve deelname van de Palestijnen. Alleen dan zal er uitzicht zijn op een tweestatenoplossing.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Saskia Bricmont (Verts/ALE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, un plan colonial en vingtÂ points, ou tout autre plan de paix, n'exonÃ¨re pas nos gouvernements ou l'Union europÃ©enne d'agir pour briser le blocus humanitaire imposÃ© par IsraÃ«l et mettre fin au gÃ©nocide. L'obligation morale et lÃ©gale d'agir est engagÃ©e. C'est sur cette obligation, sur la reconnaissance de la Palestine et sur l'adoption de sanctions contre le gouvernement Netanyahu que les membres des flottilles attirent l'attention par leurs actions.

Des millions de citoyens Ã  travers le monde se lÃ¨vent pour exiger d'une seule voix la fin du gÃ©nocide et de l'occupation. L'attaque terroriste du Hamas duÂ 7Â octobreÂ 2023 restera Ã  jamais gravÃ©e dans nos mÃ©moires. Mes pensÃ©es vont aux familles des victimes et aux otages, que nous voulons voir libÃ©rÃ©s sans dÃ©lai.

Mais deuxÂ ans plus tard, ce sont les Palestiniens qui en font les frais: 65Â 000Â morts, deuxÂ millions de vies palestiniennes en danger, des vies que nous avons le pouvoir et le devoir de protÃ©ger. Le Parlement europÃ©en et la Commission europÃ©enne doivent faire en sorte que la paix soit assurÃ©e Ã  tous.

Â Â Hanna Gedin (The Left). â Fru talman! Jag vill bÃ¶rja med att tacka. Tack till alla pÃ¥ flotillan. Tack till alla miljontals mÃ¤nniskor vÃ¤rlden Ã¶ver som intar gatorna och demonstrerar. Â Â Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, prvo Å¾elim danas izraziti solidarnost s obiteljima Å¾rtava i talaca koje Hamas joÅ¡ drÅ¾i u zatoÄeniÅ¡tvu. Danas, 7. listopada, moramo se joÅ¡ jednom prisjetiti da je Hamas svojim teroristiÄkim napadom na izraelske civile zapoÄeo ovo novo tragiÄno poglavlje rata na Bliskom istoku.

Hamas zbog toga ne smije biti nagraÄen. Hamas mora nestati iz Gaze kako bi Palestinci mogli biti doista slobodni, a Izraelci sigurni. Europa mora podrÅ¾ati mirovni plan Bijele kuÄe, pregovore koje se trenutno vode u Egiptu i u sluÄaju uspjeha u njegovoj implementaciji. DvodrÅ¾avno rjeÅ¡enje neÄe se postiÄi govorima i rezolucijama koje su viÅ¡e namijenjene domaÄoj javnosti nego o stvarnom postizanju mira.

Stoga treba podrÅ¾ati plan Bijele kuÄe. To je trenutno najbolja Å¡ansa za mir, za osloboÄenje talaca, za prestanak izraelskih napada na Gazu i patnju civila, za sigurnost Izraela i za ostvarivanje prava Palestinaca da budu svoji na svome.

Â Â MatjaÅ¾ Nemec (S&D). â SpoÅ¡tovani visoki zbor! Posledice, ki jih je prinesel sedmi oktober pred dvema letoma, bodo v zgodovini ÄloveÅ¡tva zapisane kot eno najtemnejÅ¡ih in najokrutnejÅ¡ih obdobij, ki jih je storila in dopustila naÅ¡a generacija. Hamasov napad je vreden vse obsodbe, o tem ni dvoma, a nesorazmerni odgovor Izraela nanj z nedvoumnim in neumornim brisanjem Å¾ivljenja v Gazi, kar Å¡e traja danes, je popolnoma sprevrÅ¾eno dejanje in hud zloÄin proti ÄloveÅ¡tvu.

Te dni znova potekajo mirovna pogajanja, da bi konÄno ustavili morijo v Gazi in vrnili izraelske talce svojcem, in ta si zasluÅ¾ijo vso naÅ¡o podporo.

A moje sporoÄilo ostaja jasno - moralna dolÅ¾nost Unije je priznati in ukrepati. Holokavst ni opraviÄilo za desetletja trajajoÄ apartheid nad palestinskim ljudstvom in sedmi oktober ni opraviÄilo za genocid v Gazi.

Â Â Nela Riehl (Verts/ALE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Wie kommt es, dass wir so viel Ã¼ber und so wenig mit den PalÃ¤stinenserinnen und PalÃ¤stinensern sprechen? Sie merken, mir fehlen fast die Worte, heute, am 7.Â Oktober, wenn ich an das Leid des Massakers am 7.Â Oktober denke und wenn ich an das Leid des Genozids, der daraufhin begann, denke. Wie kommt es, dass in deutschen Medien seit zwei Jahren ein Bild von dem israelischen MilitÃ¤r quasi unkritisch Ã¼bernommen wirdÂ â dieses Narrativ von Bild bis TagesschauÂ â und palÃ¤stinensische Stimmen selbst kaum zu Wort kommen?

Und jetzt der Friedensdeal von Trump: Nicht etwa PalÃ¤stinenserinnen und PalÃ¤stinenser sollen von Anfang an selbst Ã¼ber ihr Land bestimmen, sondern ein sogenannter Aufsichtsrat unter dem US-PrÃ¤sidenten und Tony Blair. Das hat koloniale UntertÃ¶ne, die nur schwer zu Ã¼berhÃ¶ren sind. Ich frage mich: Warum ist freiheitliche Selbstbestimmung nicht das Grundprinzip, wenn wir Ã¼ber die Zukunft PalÃ¤stinas und Israels diskutieren? Die EU steht fÃ¼r Freiheit und Frieden. Jetzt muss bewiesen werden, dass das fÃ¼r alle Menschen gilt. Ja, die Verhandlungen sind ein Hoffnungsschimmer. Aber es braucht den Druck und die UnterstÃ¼tzung der EU. Es kann keine Freiheit ohne Selbstbestimmung geben. PalÃ¤stinenserinnen und PalÃ¤stinenser haben das Recht auf ihren eigenen Staat, ihr eigenes Land und eine unabhÃ¤ngige Regierung.

Â Â NicolÃ¡s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±or comisario, hace dos aÃ±os se produjo una matanza de israelÃ­es cruel y salvaje. Y despuÃ©s hemos tenido dos aÃ±os de guerra y de sufrimiento en Gaza. Pero justo ahora tenemos un rayo de esperanza en el plan de paz presentado por los Estados Unidos que la UniÃ³n Europea y los Estados miembros deberÃ­amos respaldar sin reservas porque es la Ãºnica iniciativa seria, viable y factible para traer la paz y acabar con el sufrimiento de los palestinos.

Es un plan muy complejo que tiene varias fases y que estÃ¡ condicionado a muchos factores. Pero hemos de apoyarlo desde ya. El Gobierno de Israel lo apoya, lo apoyan los paÃ­ses musulmanes y los paÃ­ses Ã¡rabes de la regiÃ³n, lo apoya el secretario general de las Naciones Unidas y por tanto, como digo, es la Ãºnica esperanza.

Esto demuestra que los conflictos internacionales se resuelven y se vehiculan a travÃ©s del trabajo diplomÃ¡tico, de los esfuerzos diplomÃ¡ticos, del trabajo duro de lÃ­deres que estÃ¡n dispuestos a conseguir compromisos polÃ­ticos y no de activismos estÃ©riles, de aspavientos, de posicionamientos radicales y de otras manifestaciones totalmente inÃºtiles.

Â Â Jaume Asens LlodrÃ  (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, esto no es un plan de paz, esto es un plan de negocios de Trump, de su yerno, de sus amigos... para convertir Gaza despuÃ©s de un genocidio en lo que aparecÃ­a en su vÃ­deo macabro: en un parque temÃ¡tico, en un resort de lujo. DetrÃ¡s de todo exterminio, siempre hay un plan de negocios; lo de HamÃ¡s es una excusa. Y para que sea viable este plan hace falta mano de obra barata âesclava, si es posibleâ. Y de ahÃ­ el ultimÃ¡tum a los supervivientes del gueto de Gaza: Â«u obedecÃ©is o serÃ©is borrados del mapaÂ». Ese es el mismo lenguaje de los imperios coloniales o de los regÃ­menes totalitarios como el nazismo.

Pero nosotros, seÃ±orÃ­as del Partido Socialista, tenemos que defender la ley, los tribunales de justicia, las Naciones Unidas. NingÃºn plan es legÃ­timo si nace de la impunidad de la ocupaciÃ³n y del exterminio. Ninguna justicia es legÃ­tima si no hay derechos, justicia ni reparaciÃ³n, y eso empieza por ejecutar la orden de detenciÃ³n internacional contra Netanyahu. Â¿Lo otro? Volver a nuestro pasado neocolonial y repartirnos el botÃ­n, que es lo que ustedes proponen. Como, claro, hemos puesto la mitad de las bombas, hemos participado de este genocidio... pues ahora reclaman su parte. Esto es una vergÃ¼enza.

Î ÎºÏÏÎ¹Î¿Ï Î¤ÏÎ±Î¼Ï Î¼Î±ÏÎ¬ÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ± Î»ÏÎ³Î¹Î± ÏÎ¿Ï. ÎÏÎµÎ¯Ï ÏÎ¹ Î»Î­ÏÎµ;

Â Â

Â Â Å½eljana Zovko (PPE). â Madam President, Commissioner, exactly two years ago, hundreds of innocent Israelis were brutally and tragically killed, raped and taken as hostages.

This horrific massacre was not a random act. It was a meticulously planned terrorist strategy aimed at erasing any possibility of coexistence between communities in the region. Hundreds of hostages were taken, in acts reminiscent of medieval barbarism, shamefully broadcast online, with the intent to spread hatred and destroy any hope for peace in the Middle East.

Those who call for the disappearance of Israel and those who reject the two-state solution are the only ones who benefit from such heinous crimes.

Fortunately, President Trump has presented a peace plan which constitutes a crucial step towards building lasting peace and stability in the Middle East. This is our last real opportunity to end this conflict and to secure the safe release of the hostages.

The European Union must take an active role in supporting the success of this plan, and in laying the foundation for a viable two-state solution. I wish you good luck, Commissioner.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â ChloÃ© Ridel (S&D). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, il y a deuxÂ ans, les attaques terroristes du Hamas leÂ 7Â octobre nous plongeaient dans l'effroi. Aujourd'hui, je pense aux 1Â 219Â morts, aux 251Â otages israÃ©liens et Ã  leurs familles. Je pense aussi Ã  tous les militants israÃ©liens pour la justice et pour la paix, qui tentent de sortir leur pays d'une spirale de vengeance.

Depuis deuxÂ ans, l'Union europÃ©enne a Ã©tÃ© impuissante Ã  exercer une pression suffisante sur un gouvernement israÃ©lien gÃ©nocidaire. Aujourd'hui, elle observe en spectatrice DonaldÂ Trump imposer son Â«plan de paixÂ»Â â utilisons des guillemets car de la paix, nous en sommes encore loin. Si ce plan permet un cessez-le-feu avec la libÃ©ration des otages israÃ©liens, la fin de la famine Ã  Gaza par l'acheminement de l'aide humanitaire et l'engagement d'IsraÃ«l Ã  ne pas occuper Gaza, alors oui, nous devons le soutenir.

Mais ce cessez-le-feu, aussi nÃ©cessaire soit-il pour allÃ©ger les atroces souffrances humaines des Gazaouis et des otages israÃ©liens, n'apportera la paix que s'il est suivi par des politiques de justice pour les Palestiniens. Pour y arriver, l'Europe devra enfin choisir entre la complicitÃ© et le courage. Tous les Ãtats membres de l'Union europÃ©enne doivent maintenant reconnaÃ®tre l'Ãtat palestinien et refuser que la bande de Gaza soit administrÃ©e par des technocrates nonÂ palestiniens envoyÃ©s par Trump.

Nous devons aussi valider la suspension de l'accord d'association entre l'Union europÃ©enne et IsraÃ«l, les sanctions contre les ministres israÃ©liens, l'embargo sur les armes et la punition des criminels de guerre...

(La PrÃ©sidente retire la parole Ã  l'oratrice)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rudi Kennes (The Left). â Voorzitter, commissaris, wat was de rol van de EU bij de ondersteuning van de vredesinspanningen voor Gaza? Nihil, nul, noppes, nada.

Europese mogendheden herhalen vandaag de dag het meest weerzinwekkende koloniale racisme waarvan we dachten dat het eindelijk tot het verleden zou behoren. Niet alleen hertekenen Europese leiders vandaag opnieuw de grenzen van Palestina zonder de Palestijnen, maar blijven ze ook gewoon toekijken hoe vrouwen en kinderen worden afgeslacht, zonder iets te ondernemen. Babyâs die in couveuses worden achtergelaten om te sterven, vormen geen rode lijn. Mensen in het Midden-Oosten worden behandeld als muggen, zonder recht op bestaan of verdediging. Hun strijd om te overleven is slechts een hinderlijke stok in het wiel van onze wapenverkoop.

Deze twee jaar zullen dan ook worden herinnerd als een tijdperk waarin deze Commissie en onze regeringen stilzwijgend toestemming gaven om een genocide Ã©n een etnische zuivering op de Westelijke Jordaanoever te organiseren. Zij zouden zich moeten schamen. De geschiedenis zal hen niet vrijspreken en ik hoop dat de rechters in Den Haag dat ook niet zullen doen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â David Casa (PPE). â Sinjura President, Kummissarju, it-traÄ¡edja umana li hemm gÄ§addejja bÄ§alissa f'GaÅ¼a hija naturalment intollerabbli. AÄ§na lkoll xhud ta' ksur sfaÄÄat tal-liÄ¡i internazzjonali, ta' krudeltÃ  mingÄ§ajr preÄedent. U dan kollu bÄ§ala reazzjoni gÄ§all-att terroristiku tas-7 ta' Ottubru. Dakinhar Ä¡ew massakrati aktar minn elf persuna innoÄenti u hemm ostaÄ¡Ä¡i li sal-lum gÄ§adhom priÄ¡unieri ta' Ä¦amas. Ä¦bieb tiegÄ§i, iÅ¼-Å¼ejjed kollu Å¼ejjed. PerÃ² hemm xaqq ta' dawl: hemm pjan gÄ§all-paÄi li ppropona l-Gvern Amerikan, pjan biex jeÄ§ilsu l-ostaÄ¡Ä¡i, pjan biex jitwaqqaf l-attakk fuq GaÅ¼a u titfassal triq gÄ§al soluzzjoni ta' Å¼ewÄ¡ stati biex il-Palestinjani u l-IÅ¼raeljani jkunu jistgÄ§u jgÄ§ixu fil-paÄi fir-reÄ¡jun. Â 
Â Â
