Â Â Thijs Reuten (S&D). â Madam President, Commission, Council, colleagues, the Middle East and the innocent people of Israel and Palestine cannot endure yet another broken promise of peace, sabotaged by extremists from either side once again. And frankly, it saddens me deeply that the Trump plan is the closest to a peace proposal we have seen in years because of our own passivity. And close is not enough.

Yes, Hamas must go. Hostages must be freed. But true peace requires ending Hamas's grip. And all those who say that as well in this room must realise that that means strengthening the Palestinian Authority and taking Palestinian self-determination seriously â not about Palestinians without Palestinians. A real twoâstate solution, with the West Bank. Not a distant dream, but a decision, now. An immediate ceasefire, humanitarian aid, fair reconstruction, and truth and accountability to heal and restore dignity.

The cycle of terror, injustice and inhumanity must end, and Europe can help, but only if we have one voice.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pernando Barrena Arza (The Left). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, el plan del presidente Trump para parar la agresiÃ³n israelÃ­ en Palestina llega 67Â 000 muertos tarde. Desde el plano polÃ­tico, no es sino un compendio de restricciones y humillaciones para la parte palestina, exigencias de desapariciÃ³n y amenazas de todo tipo que solo se hacen a una de las partes, la palestina, mientras se extiende la alfombra roja de la impunidad a Israel.

Esto como plan de paz no presagia nada bueno. No serÃ­a aceptable en ningÃºn otro conflicto ni en ninguna otra parte del mundo. En cualquier caso, nosotros no vamos a ser mÃ¡s papistas que el papa y no vamos a enmendar la plana a nadie. Si finalmente es un plan que sirve a la parte de Palestina, que es la agredida, a nosotros tambiÃ©n nos valdrÃ¡ y estaremos de acuerdo en que, si al menos sirve para detener el genocidio contra el pueblo palestino, que se acuerde y se firme cuanto antes.

Seguimos perplejos por la falta de iniciativa y acciÃ³n de la alta representante Kallas en esta materia. Somos conscientes de que los tratados no dan capacidad de acciÃ³n ejecutiva en relaciones internacionales, pero su gestiÃ³n destaca por una inacciÃ³n brutal que ha convertido a la UniÃ³n en cÃ³mplice de la mayor matanza de civiles que hemos conocido en los Ãºltimos decenios.

Gracias a quienes desean acabar con la capacidad de Europa para ser un actor global, Europa ni estÃ¡ ni se la espera en Palestina...

(la presidenta retira la palabra al orador)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you very much, dear colleague. I have closed the microphone, so people can't hear you. The thing is that we have a lot of speakers, also for catch-the-eye, and I want to take as many as possible. The more you stick to your speaking time, the easier it is for me to give more colleagues the floor.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Wouter Beke (PPE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, twee jaar geleden voerde Hamas een vreselijke aanslag uit op onschuldige IsraÃ«lische burgers. 1Â 200Â doden, afgeslacht in hun huizen, op straat en op een festival. Tientallen gijzelaars zitten nog steeds vast. Onmenselijk. We moeten er alles aan doen om hen vrij te krijgen en alle initiatieven ervoor ondersteunen.

De ene misdaad rechtvaardigt echter nog altijd de andere niet. Wat IsraÃ«l sindsdien in Gaza heeft ontketend, is niet alleen disproportioneel, maar minstens even onmenselijk. Tienduizenden doden. Hele families zijn weggebombardeerd. Ook dat moeten we even krachtig blijven veroordelen.

We hopen dat de huidige gesprekken een kiem zijn voor vrede. Elke stap om deze waanzin te stoppen, is een stap vooruit. Maar wat dan? Een staakt-het-vuren is nog altijd geen vrede. Duurzame vrede kan er alleen komen met een tweestatenoplossing âÂ waarbij IsraÃ«l stopt met illegale nederzettingenÂ â en met wederzijdse erkenning in de regio en een echt zelfbestuur voor Palestina. De missie van de Europese Unie is om hieraan mee te werken.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tonino Picula (S&D). â PoÅ¡tovana predsjedavajuÄa, povjerenice, podrÅ¾avam uspostavu mira u uniÅ¡tenoj Gazi jer je to put kojim treba krenuti bez oklijevanja. Humanost mora nadjaÄati transakcijski cinizam koji dominira globalnom politikom u ovo doba. NaÅ¾alost, Å¾ivimo u razdoblju kada su ekstremisti izrazito ojaÄali, dok sporazum iz Osla i lideri tog vremena djeluju kao fusnota iz daleke proÅ¡losti.

Osobito je vaÅ¾no oslobaÄanje svih talaca i dogovaranje trajnog i odrÅ¾ivog mira. Hamas je teroristiÄka organizacija i ne smije imati nikakvu ulogu u buduÄem ureÄenju odnosa unutar Palestine. MeÄutim, izraelska vlada sastavljena od politiÄkih ekstremista ne Å¾eli ni Äuti za dogovor koji bi oÄuvao palestinsku samoupravu, a kamoli doveo do stvaranja samostalne palestinske drÅ¾ave.

Å½alim Å¡to Europska unija nije pokazala vjerodostojnost na ovom prevaÅ¾nom ispitu. NaÅ¡e nejedinstvo opet je u praksi izguralo one vrijednosti na koje se tako Äesto pozivamo. Å teta, jer programirano stradanje civilnog stanovniÅ¡tva u Gazi je do kraja zaoÅ¡trilo politiÄku odgovornost svake vlade drÅ¾ave Älanice i Europske unije u cjelini.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â SeÃ¡n Kelly (PPE). â A UachtarÃ¡in, today marks two years since the massive, violent and brutal attacks on Israeli citizens and the taking of 251 hostages. We will remember the victims and their families, as well as the innocent civilians of Gaza, who continue to suffer as collateral in this conflict. Yesterday's talks in Egypt on President Trump's Gaza peace plan offered a glimmer of hope. The plan centres around urgency and immediate results. No doubt â there is urgency, but enforced starvation, especially of little children, must never be used as a bargaining tool. Any plan must ensure a sustainable future in Gaza.

I welcome the commitment to Gaza's redevelopment and Israel's withdrawal from the strip â a vital step towards a ceasefire and a two-state solution. But we also need guarantees to stop annexation and settler violence in the West Bank, to protect Palestinians from further harm and demand accountability for violations on both sides. True peace will only come when the hostages are released, both peoples are treated with equal dignity, equal security and equal rights. The sooner, the better.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AodhÃ¡n Ã RÃ­ordÃ¡in (S&D). â MadamÂ President, Europe, the world's greatest peace project, must not stand by as Trump and Netanyahu design a toxic plan for Palestine. Europe must take inspiration from the humanity of the Sumud flotilla, not the cowering submission of PresidentÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen.Â Her failure of leadership is why I will support Thursday's motion of censure from the Left.

We must return to the vision of a twoâstate solution, where the national aspirations of Palestinians and Israelis can coexist and never again fall to the depths of Hamas depravity of 7Â October, or the monstrous actions of the Israeli apartheid regime over generations and the genocide of the last two years.

Israel rejects the concept of a Palestinian state, which is why we must stand for it and lead international isolation of Israel until that happens on every level: diplomatic, cultural, economic and sporting. FIFA must ban Israel. Europe must sanction Israel now.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Daniel Attard (S&D). â Dear colleagues, the very title of this debate exposes the sorry state of our Union; reduced to an observer on the global stage. We were first in line for the photo, but once again we are last when it comes to global policy. We even heard colleagues today shamelessly attacking humanitarian efforts, ridiculing the Sumud Flotilla, parroting Netanyahu instead of defending our shared European values.

Yes â we welcome every peace effort. Yes â we condemn the terrorist attacks by Hamas. And, yes â we demand the immediate and unconditional release of all hostages. But colleagues, no agreement will erase the war crimes committed in Gaza. No agreement will bring back a future to the thousands of murdered innocent children. Of course we want peace, but equally, we want justice because there is no lasting peace without justice.

The EU must stop reducing itself to an observer, and we must stand up for our own values, insist on compliance with international law, enforce sanctions and enforce our own treaties...

(The President cut off the speaker)

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Ricordo che non sono stati portati aiuti umanitari e che quelli che c'erano non riempivano manco mezzo camion. Ã chiaro quindi a tutti che non ci fosse nessun intento di aiuti umanitari, era semplicemente un'operazione mediatica. Nessun bambino di Gaza Ã¨ stato aiutato da quella missione.

Io ricordo anche sommessamente che molti dei partecipanti a quella missione sono contrari persino al piano di pace di Trump, quello che praticamente tutto il mondo riconosce favorevolmente. La Commissione ha appoggiato chi evidentemente ha posizioni anche piÃ¹ estreme di Hamas, gente che dice "Palestina libera dal fiume al mare", il che vuol dire Israele cancellato.

Â Â Oihane Agirregoitia MartÃ­nez (Renew). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, hoy estamos aquÃ­ y unos ponen el foco en el atroz acto de HamÃ¡s de hace dos aÃ±os y otros en la absolutamente desproporcionada respuesta de Netanyahu. Las muertes son muertes y nada justifica el uso de la violencia, la violaciÃ³n de derechos humanos y mucho menos un genocidio. El pueblo palestino merece un proceso de paz que sea justo y duradero.

Y hablamos del fin de los bombardeos, de la entrega de la ayuda humanitaria, de la liberaciÃ³n de los rehenes, de la entrega de las armas, del reconocimiento de dos Estados y de la reconstrucciÃ³n y necesaria convivencia. Â Â Cynthia NÃ­ MhurchÃº (Renew). â MadamÂ President, Commissioner, Europe and this Parliament prides itself on complying with international law, championing the rule of law.

A few questions, therefore, for the Commissioner, PresidentÂ vonÂ derÂ Leyen and HighÂ RepresentativeÂ Kallas: is the continuation of the EUâIsrael trade agreement compatible with EU law? No. Is Israel interfering with EU aid and aid infrastructure? Yes. What are the EU Commission doing about protecting taxpayers' funds? Nothing. Is the arms trading with Israel by certain countries in the EU compatible with EU law? No. So, why are we waiting?

You need to play your part, Commission. Suspend the EUâIsrael trade agreement forthwith. Halt the sale of arms to Israel by certain EU states. Stop prioritising dialogue over action in the face of abject genocide. Do your job. Do it right. Do it right now.

Î£Î®Î¼ÎµÏÎ±, Î­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î±ÏÎ¿Î¼ÎµÎ¯Î½ÎµÎ¹ ÏÎ¿ 12% ÏÏÏ ÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÏ Î±ÏÎ¿ÎºÎ»ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ¼Ï. ÎÏÎµÏÎµ Î½Î± Î¼Î±Ï ÏÎµÎ¯ÏÎµ ÎºÎ¬ÏÎ¹ Î³Î¹' Î±ÏÏÏ;

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dubravka Å uica, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, thank you dear members for your remarks â I was listening carefully. Just to conclude â the European Union remains, as you know, a steadfast proponent of the peace in the Middle East. We are committed to the two-state solution. But let me reply to some of your questions which were repeated.

To Mr Botenga on sanctions, also some others such as Ms Montero: as you know, sanctions on the extremist ministers and on violent settlers and further sanctions against Hamas members â this was adopted by the Commission and proposed to the Council on 17Â September. Also, there is a partial suspension of trade-related provisions of the Association Agreement. We are aware that it will be difficult to find majorities. I am talking about the Council, and I know that any action will be too much for some and too little for others. This is what we are saying â to be adopted, this Commission's proposals require EU Member State support by unanimity and qualified majority, respectively. We are not putting on hold â I have to mention this â support to fight antisemitism and, among others, for Yad Vashem. We are not touching support for both Israeli and Palestinian civil society.

To reply on genocide â as you know, the Commission will respect all rulings by the multilateral system based on international law and the universality of human rights. On the flotilla, because the flotilla was mentioned several times â of course, as you know, the Commission has remained in regular contact with Member States and with Israel regarding the humanitarian situation in Gaza. We reiterate their consistent call for rapid and unimpeded delivery of aid. We respect the humanitarian commitment of those that were aboard the flotilla. In the European Union, everyone, as all of us know, has the right to exercise such activism and freedom of assembly. This is a core pillar of our values. Freedom of navigation under international law must be upheld and no attacks or any use of force against the flotilla, including drone strikes or seizure, are acceptable. To end this reply, all consular authorities have been in charge and ensured consular protection to their citizens. This is the reply to some of your questions.

Just to conclude, the path to a sustainable peace should be built on inclusive dialogue, as you know, and respect for international law. We need to learn from the lessons of the past, and we need to build on the momentum of the New York conference, which was co-led by France and Saudi Arabia. I have to mention the Global Alliance for the Implementation of the Two-State Solution which is supported by the European Union and is best placed to sustain the political horizon and credible pathway to Palestinian statehood, which includes both Gaza and the West Bank, with the Palestinian Authority as the only viable partner. We want to achieve demilitarisation, effective transitional governance and establishing the International Stabilisation Force. These three elements are vital for sustainable peace and they will require careful coordination and stakeholder engagement.

The European Union has a key role to play as the longest standing supporter of the Palestinian Authority and its reform agenda. Since someone asked now 'what has the European Union done?' and someone said 'nothing, nada' â I just wanted to reiterate we are the biggest donor and supporter of the Palestinian Authority, because, if we are speaking about dialogue, about negotiations, we need both of them to sit at the table. So we need a strong Palestinian Authority to sit at the table and no Hamas â once again, I have to mention this. So this is our position.

We also reiterate the indispensable role of the United Nations, also UNRWA, which continues to be an important partner in delivering assistance. In close coordination with the international partners and with the United Nations World Bank, the European Union will play a key role in the recovery and reconstruction process through the Palestinian donor group. This donor group can now be the main forum to bring together like-minded partners, including all EU Member States, regional stakeholders and signatories of the New York Declaration. This is the moment to invite all of them to participate on board this donor group. This effort will revolve around the recovery and reconstruction strategy, which can be led by a reformed Palestinian Authority. This will safeguard Palestinian ownership, set parameters and offer an umbrella and credibility to individual initiatives. The dedicated instrument for Gaza and the European Union which is being developed will considerably back up this convening role. As I already said, the Palestinian Authority should be stronger and there is no place for Hamas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, thank you for today's debate. It showed again that we are all committed to addressing the ongoing crisis in Gaza.Â We will remain dedicated to a peaceful resolution of the conflict and we will contribute to the new momentum towards peace.

In the Council, Member States are currently discussing the measures that the Commission proposed in September.Â Based on our continuous commitment to the Palestinian and Israeli people and our support to regional integration, the European Union is well positioned to offer valuable contribution.

We will work jointly with our longâstanding partners in the region and with our partners through the Global Alliance. The twoâstate solution remains our commitment.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

10. Common agricultural policy (joint debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the joint debate on:

â the report by AndrÃ© Rodrigues, on behalf of the Committee on Agriculture and Rural Development, on amending certain CAP regulations as regards the conditionality system, types of intervention in the form of direct payment, types of intervention in certain sectors and rural development and annual performance reports, data and interoperability governance, suspensions of payments annual performance clearance and controls and penalties (COM(2025)0236 â C10-0086/2025 â 2025/0236(COD)) (A10-0164/2025); and.

â the report by CÃ©line Imart, on behalf of the Committee on Agriculture and Rural Development, on strengthening the position of farmers in the food supply chain (COM(2024)0577 â C10-0209/2024 â 2024/0319(COD)) (A10-0161/2025).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â CÃ©line Imart, rapporteure. â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, ce rapport est le fruit de longs mois de travail avec un seul objectif: renforcer la place de nos agriculteurs dans la chaÃ®ne de valeur et garantir leur revenu, l'une de leurs principales revendications lors des manifestations deÂ 2024. Monsieur le Commissaire, vous avez dÃ©fendu cette premiÃ¨re proposition lÃ©gislative du mandat avec beaucoup de courage. Nous ne sommes pas d'accord sur tout, mais s'il y a bien un combat que nous partageons profondÃ©ment, c'est plus de revenus et moins de normes.

Le cÅur de cette rÃ©forme est simple: sÃ©curiser l'agriculteur avec un contrat conclu avec son premier acheteur. Il faut mettre fin Ã  la prÃ©caritÃ© de relations commerciales trop souvent dÃ©sÃ©quilibrÃ©es et garantir une rÃ©munÃ©ration juste pour ceux qui nous nourrissent, Ã  travers la prise en compte des coÃ»ts de production. Cet objectif est toutefois abordÃ© avec pragmatisme: un contrat peut Ãªtre un simple courriel ou un engagement sur une application mobile, et des dÃ©rogations sectorielles Ã  l'Ã©chelle nationale sont prÃ©vues pour les filiÃ¨res qui en feront la demande.

La premiÃ¨re levÃ©e de boucliers face Ã  ce texte, pourtant Ã©quilibrÃ©, est venue des coopÃ©ratives. Beaucoup d'entre elles m'ont fait un procÃ¨s d'intention, m'accusant d'Ãªtre leur ennemie dÃ©clarÃ©e, ce qui est faux. Elles sont importantes, mais elles ne peuvent pas pour autant se soustraire Ã  toute rÃ¨gle sur le seul fondement qu'elles sont composÃ©es d'agriculteurs coopÃ©rateurs. Elles doivent aux agriculteurs une information claire, en particulier sur les prix. Il ne s'agit pas de dÃ©molir un modÃ¨le, ni de demander des Ã©crits Ã  tout-va, mais simplement d'avoir plus de transparence.

Imaginez que vous arriviez dans un magasin de chaussures et qu'au moment de passer Ã  la caisse, vous puissiez redÃ©finir l'ensemble des prix affichÃ©s au motif que le noir d'une chaussure n'est pas assez noir pour vous ou que le modÃ¨le n'est pas rigoureusement le mÃªme que celui dÃ©crit dans le catalogue. On vous prendrait pour un fou. Pourtant, c'est la rÃ©alitÃ© Ã  laquelle les agriculteurs sont parfois confrontÃ©s. Je le dis donc: le Copa-Cogeca, pour certains, ne dÃ©fend plus les agriculteurs, et je m'en dÃ©sole. Ã l'inverse, d'autres syndicats agricoles de plusieurs pays europÃ©ens, notamment la France, l'Italie et l'Espagne, ont fait entendre une voix dissonante en appelant Ã  soutenir les compromis du texte, et je m'en rÃ©jouis.

Alors que le vote approche, je veux Ã©galement insister sur deux autres points essentiels de mon rapport. Le premier concerne la protection des dÃ©nominations liÃ©es Ã  la viande. Je l'assume: un steak, une escalope ou une saucisse sont des produits issus de nos Ã©levages. Point. Pas d'ersatz de laboratoire, pas de produit vÃ©gÃ©tal. Il s'agit de transparence et de clartÃ© pour le consommateur et de reconnaissance pour le travail de nos Ã©leveurs. Car l'Ã©levage, au-delÃ  d'Ãªtre une activitÃ© Ã©conomique, a un impact social et environnemental essentiel dans des territoires parfois isolÃ©s, oÃ¹ il prÃ©serve aussi les paysages et la biodiversitÃ©. Il n'est aucunement question d'interdire les alternatives vÃ©gÃ©tales, mais je suis attachÃ© Ã  la valorisation des termes, Ã  leur sens vÃ©ritable, Ã  leur portÃ©e. La deuxiÃ¨me levÃ©e de boucliers sur cette disposition est venue de la grande distribution, notamment allemande, mais sa critique ne reflÃ¨te pas la rÃ©alitÃ© du texte. On ne peut pas, sous couvert de prÃ©tendre dÃ©fendre le prix le plus bas pour le consommateur, piÃ©tiner un texte qui renforce la position des agriculteurs et la valorisation de leur production.

Le second point concerne la prÃ©fÃ©rence europÃ©enne. Le Made in Europe est sur toutes les lÃ¨vres, parce qu'aujourd'hui, nous importons 20Â % des denrÃ©es alimentaires que nous consommons, et ce chiffre est alarmant. Dans un monde instable, une Europe forte doit avant tout garder et conquÃ©rir sa souverainetÃ© alimentaire.

Je vous appelle donc, en conclusion, Ã  voter demain en faveur de ce texte essentiel pour nos agriculteurs. DerriÃ¨re chaque exploitation, il y a des visages, des vies, des familles qui travaillent sans relÃ¢che, parfois sept jours sur sept, pour nous nourrir. Le revenu agricole n'est pas qu'une statistique ou un chiffre abstrait; c'est une question de justice, de dignitÃ© et parfois mÃªme de survie. Je me battrai jusqu'au bout pour que ce texte soit adoptÃ© et je remercie tous les dÃ©putÃ©s de tous les groupes qui l'ont soutenu. Quand je pense Ã  ceux qui produisent notre nourriture, qui sont les sentinelles de nos paysages et de notre identitÃ©, quand je pense Ã  leur combat difficile et Ã  leur engagement quotidien, je vous le dis: ce texte, c'est le minimum que nous leur devons.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ© Rodrigues, Relator. â Senhora Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, caros colegas, ao longo dos Ãºltimos anos, os agricultores fizeram-se ouvir nas estradas da Europa, nas praÃ§as das nossas cidades, no exterior e no interior deste Parlamento.

E os agricultores europeus tÃªm sido muito claros: querem produzir e querem menos papelada; menos duplicaÃ§Ãµes; mais tempo no campo, com regras estÃ¡veis.

Ã isso que estÃ¡ em causa hoje: devolver previsibilidade a quem produz os alimentos que chegam Ã s nossas mesas, a quem cuida do nosso territÃ³rio e garante o futuro do mundo rural.

A proposta que trazemos em resposta, aliÃ¡s, Ã  proposta da ComissÃ£o Europeia, nÃ£o pretende reescrever a PAC, mas sim tornÃ¡-la mais simples e mais acessÃ­vel. NÃ£o Ã© perfeita, mas perante a realidade do terreno, antes feito do que perfeito. O que aqui propomos Ã© uma correÃ§Ã£o de rumo contra a inÃ©rcia burocrÃ¡tica europeia. Cortar custos de contexto sem cortar direitos. Ganhar eficiÃªncia sem perder rigor. Acelerar pagamentos sem abdicar dos controlos.

Fizemos este trabalho tambÃ©m com o propÃ³sito de ajudar a administraÃ§Ã£o pÃºblica a fazer o que deve: servir de ponte de acesso Ãºnico e nÃ£o como acesso a portas de um labirinto infinito, para que a informaÃ§Ã£o circule entre as entidades e nÃ£o se peÃ§a o mesmo documento duas, trÃªs, quatro vezes ao mesmo agricultor.

Menos fricÃ§Ã£o nos procedimentos significa menos erros, inspeÃ§Ãµes mais focadas e pagamentos mais atempados. E assim, tambÃ©m, se reconstrÃ³i a confianÃ§a.

TambÃ©m por isso, damos um sinal a quem mais precisa. As pequenas e mÃ©dias exploraÃ§Ãµes terÃ£o um regime mais simples e estÃ¡vel Ã  escala da sua realidade, bem como um maior apoio. Quem anda todos os dias no terreno nÃ£o quer labirintos administrativos, mas sim apoios previsÃ­veis para poder investir, planear e continuar.

Ao mesmo tempo, deixamos bem claro: simplificar nÃ£o significa desregular. NÃ£o abdicamos das salvaguardas essenciais nem do compromisso, desde logo, com a dignidade no trabalho agrÃ­cola. Receber dinheiro pÃºblico exige respeito por normas laborais e transparÃªncia no uso dos fundos. E de forma serena, de forma serena mas simples, deixem-me tambÃ©m dizer-vos: nÃ£o hÃ¡ futuro para a agricultura sem ambiente e nÃ£o hÃ¡ ambiente protegido sem agricultores com rendimentos.

Igualmente, permitam-me destacar que adaptamos as propostas da ComissÃ£o ao nÃ­vel dos instrumentos de gestÃ£o de risco, ajustando o limiar de perdas e permitindo metodologias adaptadas Ã s culturas permanentes, aos jovens e aos novos agricultores. ResiliÃªncia sim, mas com responsabilidade e transparÃªncia.

Por fim, introduzimos mecanismos mais Ã¡geis para os Estados-Membros alterarem os seus planos estratÃ©gicos, permitindo ajustes rÃ¡pidos, sem abrir discussÃµes estruturais em cada revisÃ£o. Assim, garantimos previsibilidade e rapidez. Duas palavras que dificilmente estavam habituadas a serem conjugadas com a PAC.

Senhora Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, caros colegas, a PolÃ­tica AgrÃ­cola Comum precisa de uma coisa muito simples: de voltar a falar a lÃ­ngua de quem trabalha a terra. FaÃ§amos a polÃ­tica funcionar onde realmente conta, no terreno, junto dos mais de 9 milhÃµes de agricultores europeus, da ilha das Flores, nos AÃ§ores, no coraÃ§Ã£o do AtlÃ¢ntico, ao extremo oriental da RomÃ©nia, do norte gelado, da FinlÃ¢ndia ao sol ensolarado da Andaluzia.

PeÃ§o o vosso apoio para dar um passo concreto, que devolva tempo, que devolva rendimento e que dÃª previsibilidade Ã  agricultura europeia.

A histÃ³ria nÃ£o se escreve com promessas, escreve-se com decisÃµes. E a decisÃ£o certa hoje Ã© aprovar este relatÃ³rio e tornar concretas as suas medidas, tornar concreta a realidade.

Por Ãºltimo, quero agradecer a todos aqueles, nomeadamente aos meus colegas relatores-sombra, pelo trabalho que tambÃ©m deram para que este relatÃ³rio fosse realidade.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christophe Hansen, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, first of all, I would like to thank you for the opportunity to exchange on these two important files that I have put very early on already in my mandate. I especially would like to thank the two rapporteurs, CÃ©lineÂ Imart and AndrÃ©Â Rodrigues, for their work, as well as their shadow rapporteurs, to have delivered so quickly on these important files.

Time is of the essence for these two proposals to also start having an effect on the ground because our farmers need to feel that something is changing. Our farmers are eagerly awaiting European solutions to address some of their most pressing needs: a supply chain that works for farmers and simpler operating rules. The CMO proposal is about giving farmers a stronger position in the food supply chain. We must create rules that increase their bargaining power and ensure fair opportunities, and this is precisely what the proposal does.

I am pleased to see that many of your amendments align with this goal. Some might be going beyond our initial proposal and some anticipate also proposals the Commission made in the frame of the future CFP proposals, but I am open to explore them constructively, such as those on origin labelling, for example.

One of the crucial elements of our proposals are the written contracts. Written agreements are not about imposing prices or adding red tape, as I hear way too often. They are essential for certainty and trust, and a key tool to fight against unfair trading practices. MsÂ Imart has used the example of buying shoes, I would like to use the example of buying a car. When you buy a car or sign up for an energy provision for your home, for example, we naturally contract in writing because we expect clarity about what we will get and a certain security about our rights as well. There, I think my question would be: should farmers be entitled to any less? I believe that the answer is clearly no.

Let me now move on to the simplification package. Simplification and the administrative burden, I think that is something I heard from our farming community in all the Member States I have travelled to so far. Simplification is also to be seen as part of our competitiveness agenda and I have therefore put forward a package of simplification that I consider is balanced and pragmatic, but preserving the targeted nature of this proposal is, of course, crucial. Our proposal strikes the right balance between the urgent need to further simplify certain aspects of our policy, while at the same time ensuring that we avoid disruptions in the last two years of the implementation of the current CAP. Equally, we must maintain the ability of the CAP to achieve its objectives.

So, the proposal is targeted in a way that it is better adapted to the situation of, for example, organic young farmers or farmers affected by natural disasters. It encourages the use of simplified payments for small farmers, a single control on farms and less data submission for our farmers. You made a number of proposals that I can welcome there, like reinforcing data interoperability or increasing the coverage of lump sum payments for small farmers.

On the other hand, I also see certain amendments that raise some concerns. For example, extending rules on the decommitment or weakening risk management changes that deviate considerably from our proposal. These are particularly worrying, but I must express certain reservations on the changes which cover a number of important good agriculture and environmental conditions. These changes would cumulatively undermine the attainment of the CAP's climate and environmental objectives, and can also delay the agreement on this file, given the Council's position. Such amendments seriously put at risk the timely adoption â I would just like to remind you that we have to deliver on this file still this year, preferably in the month of October already, in order for our farmers to feel this simplification during the next calendar year.

So, this is our common challenge to deliver on that and find solutions that are quickly applicable. We owe this to our farmers. I am looking forward to the constructive work on that with you in the trilogues that we can hopefully start soon.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRÃSIDENCE: YOUNOUS OMARJEE
Vice-PrÃ©sident

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan-Åtefan Motreanu, Ã®n numele grupului PPE. â Domnule preÈedinte, domnule comisar, stimaÈi colegi, Ã®ncep prin a mulÈumi Comisiei Europene pentru propunerea de simplificare a politicii agricole comune Èi colegilor din Comisia AGRI pentru contribuÈiile lor. Am reuÈit sÄ aducem mai multÄ flexibilitate Ã®n aplicarea regulilor de mediu, sÄ includem bolile animalelor Ã®n lista situaÈiilor eligibile pentru plÄÈi de crizÄ, sÄ reducem pragul pierderilor de la 20Â % la 15Â % Èi, foarte important, sÄ creÈtem sprijinul pentru micii fermieri pÃ¢nÄ la 5Â 000Â EUR Èi o platÄ unicÄ de 75Â 000Â EUR pentru dezvoltarea afacerii.

Aceste mÄsuri sunt fÄrÄ Ã®ndoialÄ binevenite, Ã®nsÄ agricultura europeanÄ are nevoie pe termen lung de reguli clare, flexibile Èi echitabile. Cu toate acestea, Ã®n perspectiva urmÄtorului buget al Uniunii Europene, asistÄm la un alt tip de simplificare propus de Comisia EuropeanÄ, respectiv o reducere bugetarÄ de 20-30Â % Ã®n termeni reali, dupÄ ajustarea la inflaÈie Èi dispariÈia pilonuluiÂ II prin comasarea acestuia cu fondurile de coeziune.

AceastÄ propunere a stÃ¢rnit reacÈii puternice Ã®n rÃ¢ndul fermierilor, al autoritÄÈilor locale Èi al comunitÄÈilor rurale, ale cÄror nemulÈumiri riscÄ sÄ fie exploatate de forÈele populiste atÃ¢t la nivel naÈional, cÃ¢t Èi european. Fermierii cer simplificare realÄ, sprijin concret, venituri echitabile Èi predictibilitate, iar cetÄÈenii aÈteaptÄ hranÄ de calitate Èi preÈuri corecte. De aceea, avem nevoie de o politicÄ agricolÄ cu adevÄrat comunÄ, simplificatÄ Èi corect finanÈatÄ, care sÄ asigure demnitatea fermierilor Èi sÄ protejeze identitatea satelor, tradiÈiile Èi hrana de calitate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Cristina Maestre, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, bienvenido de nuevo. Estamos ante una reforma exprÃ©s del Reglamento de la OCM Ãºnica que deberÃ­a, sin duda, servir para reforzar la posiciÃ³n de los agricultores en la cadena de valor âes muy fÃ¡cil decirlo, pero yo creo que tambiÃ©n hay que demostrarlo con las propuestas y tambiÃ©n con el sentido del votoâ. Por tanto, a mÃ­ la pregunta que me gustarÃ­a hacer es si todos los miembros del Partido Popular Europeo estÃ¡n en esa misma disposiciÃ³n, porque me resulta muy muy extraÃ±o âademÃ¡s, incomprensibleâ que a estas alturas de la pelÃ­cula ây permÃ­tanme la expresiÃ³nâ todavÃ­a haya diputados que estÃ©n en contra de la formalizaciÃ³n de contratos por escrito, algo que, querido comisario, me parece que es esencial. Lo ha dicho muy bien en su intervenciÃ³n tambiÃ©n nuestra ponente, CÃ©line Imart, que hablaba sobre cÃ³mo de generalizado estÃ¡ el hecho de que todos adquirimos relaciones contractuales.

La semana pasada alquilÃ© un vehÃ­culo y tenÃ­a seis o sieteÂ pÃ¡ginas de contrato, y eso solo un dÃ­a. Â¿QuÃ© serÃ¡ de la cosecha entera y del trabajo y el esfuerzo de tantos agricultores que tienen que poner de forma oral, sin ningÃºn tipo de garantÃ­a, su cosecha en manos de terceras personas? Por lo tanto, esto es esencial para nosotros.

Miren, en mi paÃ­s tenemos desde el aÃ±o 2013 la formulaciÃ³n de los contratos por escrito. De forma natural, ha sido aceptada por los agricultores y, ademÃ¡s, lo utilizan con toda normalidad. No es la panacea, pero es un avance claro para poder fortalecerlos. SeÃ±or comisario, la uniÃ³n es la fuerza âdesde luego que tenemos que estar unidosâ, y la uniÃ³n de los productores en cooperativas, organizaciones de productores y sus asociaciones es la forma mÃ¡s eficaz para lograr un retorno econÃ³mico justo dentro de la cadena.

Rechazamos cualquier intento de diluir su fuerza de concentraciÃ³n en la oferta y asÃ­ lo reflejaremos con nuestros votos. Este es un informe que, en general, ha conseguido un buen equilibrio y, por ello, felicito a la ponente, CÃ©line Imart, a quien pido un Ãºltimo esfuerzo en la lÃ­nea de lo que he dicho antes: conseguir el apoyo mayoritario de sus compaÃ±eros en los acuerdos por escrito.

Y, por Ãºltimo, tambiÃ©n querÃ­a resaltar todo lo que tiene que ver con el etiquetado de origen, algo que usted, comisario, ha pospuesto en la reforma de la OCM para dentro de dos aÃ±os y que podrÃ­a ser efectivo si ya lo aprobamos aquÃ­. Por Ãºltimo, seÃ±or presidente, termino dando las gracias a los ponentes y tambiÃ©n felicitando a mi compaÃ±ero AndrÃ© Rodrigues por su excelente trabajo en el paquete de simplificaciÃ³n, que es muy importante y con el que, junto con la reforma de la OCM, enviamos un mensaje claro a los agricultores: escuchamos y respondemos.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raffaele Stancanelli, a nome del gruppo PfE. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario,Â domani voteremo due dossier di grande rilievo: quello sulla semplificazione e quello sul rafforzamento della posizione degli agricoltori nella filiera agroalimentare. Parliamo della vita quotidiana di chi lavora la terra, della sopravvivenza delle nostre aziende agricole e della sicurezza alimentare dei cittadini europei.

Rafforzare la posizione degli agricoltori significa difendere il valore delle nostre produzioni. Ã fondamentale accogliere ciÃ² che garantisce un reddito giusto alle aziende agricole, dando dignitÃ  e prospettiva futura.

Lo abbiamo sempre sostenuto con chiarezza: la burocrazia Ã¨ un macigno sulle spalle degli agricoltori. Eppure dobbiamo avere l'onestÃ  di dirlo: il problema non si esaurisce con la riduzione degli oneri amministrativi.

Il punto centrale Ã¨ che la nuova PAC, cosÃ¬ come Ã¨ stata concepita, produrrÃ  effetti devastanti. Non si tratta piÃ¹ soltanto di un problema tecnico-amministrativo, Ã¨ diventato un problema politico. Noi siamo stati scelti dai cittadini e siamo qui a rappresentare la loro voce e la voce del settore agricolo oggi Ã¨ chiara: questa PAC Ã¨ una vergogna, una politica cieca che non valorizza le eccellenze, che non tiene conto delle specificitÃ  territoriali, che penalizza chi produce invece di sostenerlo.

Tutti i gruppi politici, pur con differenze, riconoscono la gravitÃ  della situazione. Questo immobilismo non Ã¨ piÃ¹ tollerabile. Pretendiamo una svolta. Questa riforma deve essere ritirata.Â Se davvero vogliamo difendere i nostri agricoltori, signor Commissario, dobbiamo avere il coraggio politico di cambiare rotta. Solo cosÃ¬ restituiremo dignitÃ  a chi lavora la terra e costruiremo un futuro solido per l'agricoltura europea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, vandaag bespreken we twee heel interessante onderwerpen: de vereenvoudiging van het gemeenschappelijk landbouwbeleid en aanpassingen in de marktordening. Beide voorstellen zijn belangrijk om de rentabiliteit van de landbouwbedrijven te kunnen versterken. Het is goed om te zien dat er hiervoor onder de nieuwe Commissie meer aandacht is gekomen.

Er zijn echter twee kanttekeningen. KanttekeningÂ 1: de vereenvoudiging van het gemeenschappelijk landbouwbeleid is goed, maar we moeten absoluut breder kijken. Ook in de sfeer van de milieu- en natuurwetgeving hebben we te maken met onnodig knellende regelgeving. Ik denk aan de recente oproep van het Europees Parlement om de nitraatrichtlijn daadwerkelijk te herzien. We moeten landbouwers veel meer ruimte geven om de maatschappelijke doelen te realiseren naar eigen inzicht op basis van verantwoord vakmanschap. Dat vraagt om ruimte voor doelsturing als alternatief voor te gedetailleerde middelsturing.

KanttekeningÂ 2: het is inderdaad belangrijk om de positie van de landbouwer in de keten te versterken. Schriftelijke contracten kunnen daarbij zeker helpen. Dat alles mag echter niet ten koste gaan van een gezonde marktwerking. We moeten daarom niet alles wettelijk willen dichtregelen. Voor je het weet, ga je goede praktijken ondermijnen, bijvoorbeeld overeenkomsten binnen coÃ¶peraties of andere waardeketens. Daar is echt niemand mee gediend. Vandaar mijn pleidooi voor passende uitzonderingsmogelijkheden. Het is zaak daar in de trialoog goed oog voor te houden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christine Singer, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Landwirte brauchen keine Schlagworte. Sie brauchen spÃ¼rbare Entlastung. Genau dafÃ¼r haben wir im GAP-Vereinfachungspaket gekÃ¤mpft: fÃ¼r weniger BÃ¼rokratie und fÃ¼r mehr PraxisnÃ¤he. Ein zentraler Durchbruch ist die neue Stichtagsregelung fÃ¼r DauergrÃ¼nland. Damit wÃ¼rden wir den absurden Pflugzwang alle fÃ¼nf Jahre oder nun alle sieben Jahre beenden. Wir wÃ¼rden mehr Rechtssicherheit schaffen, unnÃ¶tige Emissionen verhindern und den Landwirten die Freiheit geben, so zu wirtschaften, wie es Ã¶kologisch und betriebswirtschaftlich sinnvoll ist.

Das ist Klimaschutz durch gesunden Menschenverstand und ein Gewinn fÃ¼r alle Betriebe â ob groÃ, ob klein, ob bio oder ob konventionell. Wir schlagen zudem vor, dass auch Biobetriebe in Umstellung wie Biobetriebe kÃ¼nftig als GLÃZ-konform gelten sollen. Damit entlasten wir noch einen Schwung Biobetriebe und Landwirte mehr. Mit unserem Transparenzmechanismus fÃ¼r Krisenzahlungen wollen wir sicherstellen, dass die UnterstÃ¼tzung durch dieses neue Instrument fair und nachvollziehbar verteilt wird â ohne Wettbewerbsverzerrungen.

Das sind keine kosmetischen Korrekturen, sondern Schritte, die in der FlÃ¤che ankommen. Darum appelliere ich an Sie, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen: UnterstÃ¼tzen Sie dieses Paket! FÃ¼r Vertrauen statt Misstrauen, fÃ¼r Vereinfachung statt BÃ¼rokratie und fÃ¼r die Landwirte, die endlich wieder gestalten wollen, anstatt Formulare auszufÃ¼llen. Dann liegt es an Rat und Kommission, unsere Linie mitzutragen und die Landwirte nicht zu enttÃ¤uschen. Nutzen wir diese Chance!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thomas Waitz, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Wir verlieren immer noch 800Â Betriebe jeden Tag in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union. Und ich bedanke mich ausdrÃ¼cklich fÃ¼r den Vorschlag von Kommissar Hansen, die Berichtspflichten fÃ¼r Landwirtschaften unter zehn Hektar massiv zu vereinfachen und auch die MÃ¶glichkeiten auszubauen, hier mit Pauschalzahlungen Leistungen abzugelten. Das wird unsere kleinstrukturierte Landwirtschaft unterstÃ¼tzen.

Ich bedanke mich auch ausdrÃ¼cklich dafÃ¼r und begrÃ¼Ãe, dass biologische Betriebe kÃ¼nftig alsÂ green per definition, also grÃ¼n qua Biozertifikat anerkannt werden. Das ist eine Anerkennung der Arbeit von Biobetrieben. Biobetriebe sind die am besten kontrollierten Betriebe in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union. Und sie erfÃ¼llen alle diese Kriterien.

Doch dann kommen wir schon zu den kritischen Teilen: Wir schaffen weiterhin grÃ¼ne MaÃnahmen innerhalb der GAP ab. Die Fruchtfolge haben wir schon abgeschafft, also weiterhin Monokultur nach Monokultur nach Monokultur. Die Pufferstreifen schaffen wir ab, also Pestizide bis an den Bach, bis an das GewÃ¤sser. Bei Glyphosat heiÃt das: Wenn das in das GewÃ¤sser kommt, ist das GewÃ¤sser tot. Ja, wir brechen mehr GrÃ¼nland um, obwohl GrÃ¼nland die klimafreundlichste Methode ist, um Landwirtschaft zu betreiben. Und so weiter und so fort.

Die EuropÃ¤ische Volkspartei in ihrer NaturzerstÃ¶rungskoalition mit ihren neuen Alliierten â rechts und rechtsextrem und noch extremer rechtsextrem â schafft eine UmweltmaÃnahme nach der anderen ab. Aber wenn es dann um die Bienen geht, dann lieben wir alle die Bienen. BloÃ die MaÃnahmen, die wollen wir nicht setzen. Und obendrauf noch Natura-2000-Gebiete als sowieso bio zu erklÃ¤ren, das ist einfach fachlich falsch.

Und zum Zweiten: Es ist wichtig, die BÃ¤uerinnen und Bauern in der WertschÃ¶pfungskette zu stÃ¤rken â mit Vertragspflicht, absolut. Aber mit diesem unnÃ¶tigen Veggie-Burger-Ãnderungsantrag streuen Sie nur Sand in die Augen. Glauben Sie denn, die Leute sind dumm, wenn sie in den Supermarkt gehen, und sie erkennen nicht, was ein vegetarisches Produkt ist und was nicht? HÃ¶ren Sie auf mit diesem Fleischfetischismus! Jeder ErnÃ¤hrungswissenschaftler wird Ihnen sagen: Wir mÃ¼ssen weniger Fleisch und Milch essen. BegrÃ¼Ãen wir das und hÃ¶ren wir auf, die veganen und vegetarischen, auch bÃ¤uerlichen Produkte hier zu boykottieren. Ich hoffe, Sie stimmen mit uns gegen diesen Veggie-Burger-Ãnderungsantrag.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Luke Ming Flanagan, on behalf of The Left Group. â Mr President, Commissioner, we meet again. This time I'm agreeing with you. Everyone should get fair remuneration for their work. It's fundamental. It's a human right. And as things stand, many, many farmers do not get fair remuneration. This proposed improved CMO Regulation goes some way to improving the lot of farmers. It attempts to enhance the existing provision on contracts â written contracts. It shouldn't be that amazing. Some people seem to think that's amazing. Two, to reinforce the bargaining powers of producer organisations. Three, to simplify their recognition and many other areas.

Unfortunately, though, we have MEPs, who claim to represent farmers, that want to water all of this down. We have amendments that allow Member States to do nothing, and it is my experience in Ireland that if we may do something, we will never, ever do it. It will be the case here if they are allowed to make their choice. We also see amendments which would see exemptions given to Member States for the milk sector, and I quote: 'at the request of [â¦] an organisation deemed largely representative'. That is wide open to abuse. For example, in Ireland, the IFA potentially could get such a power, an organisation that has worked against this improved regulation.

So I'll finish by saying none of those amendments should be passed. I would like to congratulate the rapporteur, CÃ©line Imart, on this. I have to say I was sceptical about her at the beginning, but I have to say I really admire her determination on this. And most of all, I admire her calling out Copa-Cogeca,Â long overdue.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Herbert Dorfmann (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Herr Kommissar! Die Verordnung Ã¼ber die Vereinfachung der GAP greift eine Ã¼berbordende BÃ¼rokratie auf, die wir in den letzten Jahren erlebt haben und leider in diesem Haus auch selbst geschaffen haben. Aber Vereinfachung bedeutet nicht Deregulierung. Vereinfachung bedeutet eben, BÃ¼rokratie dort abzubauen, wo sie nur mehr zur Last wird, nur mehr Kosten und keinen Mehrwert erzeugt. Landwirtschaftliche Betriebe haben halt im Regelfall keine BÃ¼rokraft, die den ganzen Tag dafÃ¼r sorgt, dass die Papiere in Ordnung sind. Das gilt besonders fÃ¼r die kleinen Betriebe. Deswegen ist es gut, wenn wir dort jetzt ansetzen, und das gilt auch fÃ¼r die Biobetriebe. Ich glaube, es ist richtig, dass wir dort vereinfachen, auch fÃ¼r jene Betriebe, die auf dem Weg zum Biobetrieb sind. Wieso sollte dort KonditionalitÃ¤t kontrolliert werden, wenn sie schon von der Zertifizierungsstelle kontrolliert wird?

Ich glaube auch, dass die FlexibilitÃ¤t bei mehr GrÃ¼nland sinnvoll ist, nicht weil wir weniger GrÃ¼nland wollen, sondern weil mit der bisherigen Regelung wohl kaum jemand einen Acker in DauergrÃ¼nland umwidmet, wissend, dass der Weg zurÃ¼ck nicht mehr mÃ¶glich ist. BÃ¤uerinnen und Bauern sind in der Regel gut ausgebildete Menschen. Wir mÃ¼ssen ihnen nicht bei jedem Arbeitsschritt sagen, was sie zu tun haben.

Wir unterstÃ¼tzen auch die Ãnderungen bei der Gemeinsamen Marktordnung. Jede Regelung, die letztendlich die Position des Bauern in der WertschÃ¶pfungskette unterstÃ¼tzt, ist meiner Meinung nach unterstÃ¼tzenswert. Wenn sich Bauern in Genossenschaften zusammenschlieÃen, dann sollten sie zusÃ¤tzliche MÃ¶glichkeiten haben. Deswegen glaube ich: Beide Verordnungen werden das Leben unserer BÃ¤uerinnen und Bauern ein bisschen einfacher machen. Deshalb haben sie beide meine UnterstÃ¼tzung.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dario Nardella (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, se vogliamo che la PAC resti davvero al servizio degli agricoltori, dobbiamo dare una risposta concreta al problema della semplificazione. Oggi rischiamo di spendere piÃ¹ tempo in burocrazia che nei campi.

Ridurre procedure complesse, accelerare i pagamenti, rendere le regole piÃ¹ chiare e uniformi tra Stati membri non significa perÃ² avere controlli meno efficaci, ma restituire agli agricoltori tempo, dignitÃ  e fiducia. E soprattutto, nessuna misura volta a semplificare la politica agricola comune puÃ² comportare un indebolimento della condizionalitÃ  sociale, tratto distintivo di questa politica.

Con l'introduzione dei contratti scritti obbligatori facciamo un passo decisivo per correggere lo squilibrio che da troppo tempo penalizza gli agricoltori, rafforza la loro posizione nella filiera alimentare, in una situazione dove, per ogni euro speso dai consumatori in prodotti trasformati, poco piÃ¹ di 5 centesimi finiscono oggi nelle tasche dei produttori.

Per la prima volta, questo Parlamento riconosce che i costi di produzione devono essere un elemento centrale nella definizione del prezzo. Una svolta che dÃ  concretezza alla battaglia contro le pratiche sleali per un reddito agricolo equo. Questo voto Ã¨ la dimostrazione che il Parlamento vuole affrontare la sfida della sovranitÃ  alimentare e del reddito agricolo, anche per consentire ai giovani di avvicinarsi al mondo dell'agricoltura.

Un altro passo importante Ã¨ stato fatto in relazione alla canapa. Le modifiche che abbiamo proposto vanno nella direzione di non vietare la coltivazione, la lavorazione e la vendita delle infiorescenze, dando cosÃ¬ una risposta concreta ad un settore in fase di crescita e innovazione. Un risultato importante nella battaglia per una normativa europea univoca.

Ringrazio i relatori. Ã tempo di dare risposte chiare agli agricoltori e alle filiere che rappresentano il futuro della nostra economia rurale europea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Csaba DÃ¶mÃ¶tÃ¶r (PfE). â Tisztelt ElnÃ¶k Ãr! Sok vitÃ¡nk van a BizottsÃ¡ggal, de a helyes cÃ©lokat is meg kell emlÃ­tenÃ¼nk, ha vannak ilyenek. Az, hogy a gazdÃ¡k Ã©s a felvÃ¡sÃ¡rlÃ³k kÃ¶zÃ¶tti Ã¼gyleteket kÃ¶telezÅ lesz Ã­rÃ¡sba foglalni, az egy jÃ³ lÃ©pÃ©s, mert nÃ¶veli a gazdÃ¡k biztonsÃ¡gÃ¡t. AzÃ©rt van erre szÃ¼ksÃ©g, mert sokszor elÅfordul, hogy a gazdÃ¡kat nem fizetik ki, vagy ha igen, akkor kÃ©sve. De hiÃ¡ba tesznek aprÃ³, jÃ³ lÃ©pÃ©seket a gazdÃ¡k vÃ©delmÃ©re, hogyha kÃ¶zben buldÃ³zerekkel Ã©pÃ­tik le az uniÃ³s agrÃ¡rtÃ¡mogatÃ¡si rendszert. Itt is elmondom teljesen elfogadhatatlan az, hogy 20%-kal akarjÃ¡k megvÃ¡gni az agrÃ¡rtÃ¡mogatÃ¡sokat.

Biztos Ãºr a tanÃ¡csÃ¼lÃ©sen azt is elmondta, hogy miÃ©rt. AzÃ©rt, mert finanszÃ­rozni kell a tÃ¶rlesztÅrÃ©szleteket Ã©s a hadi programokat. Az UkrajnÃ¡bÃ³l kilÅtt rakÃ©tÃ¡k nem fognak segÃ­teni a gazdÃ¡knak. Ãs egy kÃ©rdÃ©s: miÃ©rt van az, hogy a bizottsÃ¡g elnÃ¶ke Ã³rÃ¡kig beszÃ©l a hÃ¡borÃºs politikÃ¡rÃ³l, de a gazdÃ¡kat mÃ©g csak meg sem hallgatja? SzÃ³val hiÃ¡ba tesznek le az asztalra egyÃ©bkÃ©nt tÃ¡mogathatÃ³ aprÃ³ szabÃ¡lyokat, ha kÃ¶zben minden mÃ¡s lÃ©pÃ©sÃ¼kkel az Ã¡ldozat szerepÃ©t szÃ¡njÃ¡k a gazdÃ¡knak.

TÃ¶bb megbecsÃ¼lÃ©st Ã©s valÃ³di tÃ¡mogatÃ¡st kÃ¶vetelÃ¼nk nekik. Arra kÃ©rem Ã¶nÃ¶ket, hogy vonjÃ¡k vissza a kÃ¶ltsÃ©gvetÃ©si terveket. Ãs ezzel Ã¶sszefÃ¼ggÃ©sben mÃ©g valami: aki csÃ¼tÃ¶rtÃ¶kÃ¶n a bizalmi szavazÃ¡son kiÃ¡ll Ursula von der Leyen mellett, az kiÃ¡ll a kÃ¶zÃ¶s agrÃ¡rpolitika leÃ©pÃ­tÃ©se mellett is. Ez ilyen egyszerÅ±.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Carlo Fidanza (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, i provvedimenti che ci accingiamo a votare domani vanno nella giusta direzione e danno finalmente un segnale importante di attenzione al mondo agricolo.

Con la riforma dell'organizzazione comune dei mercati introduciamo misure concrete per rafforzare la posizione degli agricoltori nella catena del valore, riequilibrare i rapporti contrattuali, valorizzare il ruolo delle cooperative agricole.

Bene anche le norme sull'etichettatura dei prodotti a base di carne, essenziali per garantire trasparenza e tutelare realmente i consumatori, e le misure che privilegiano i nostri prodotti negli appalti pubblici, rafforzandone la tracciabilitÃ  e l'indicazione d'origine.

Con la semplificazione della PAC, inoltre, riduciamo la burocrazia e diamo finalmente agli agricoltori strumenti piÃ¹ efficaci per innovare e per competere.

Positivi l'aumento del sostegno alle organizzazioni di produttori e le misure per una filiera zootecnica tutta europea, con buona pace di quelle associazioni ambientaliste che ancora oggi si sono schierate per la prosecuzione delle sciagurate politiche del Green Deal e a danno dei nostri agricoltori.

Ã tempo, invece, di tornare a una politica agricola che metta al centro produzione, reciprocitÃ , redditivitÃ  e sostenibilitÃ  economica.

Sul solco di questi due provvedimenti positivi, chiediamo quindi alla Commissione di andare avanti con piÃ¹ rapiditÃ , piÃ¹ forza e piÃ¹ coraggio nel sostenere gli agricoltori, la sicurezza e la sovranitÃ  alimentare dell'Europa, a partire dalla futura PAC post- 2027 e soprattutto dal prossimo Quadro finanziario pluriennale che, nella proposta formulata, continuiamo a considerare del tutto insoddisfacente.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Cowen (Renew). â MrÂ President,Â Commissioner, colleagues, tomorrow we are voting on two hugely important CAP files.

The first is a simplification package, which rightly addresses farmers' concerns and moves us away from a one-size-fits-all conditionality policy and recognises that different countries and regions have different needs. I welcome this, and I welcome the Commission's willingness too to build flexibility into the next CAP in this area also.

But that same logic has not been applied in the second file we vote on, as alluded to by my colleague and MEP, Flanagan: the common markets organisation. Instead of maintaining the market-orientated approach, the Commission has proposed rigid contractual measures that risk undermining what are successful cooperative models, especially and particularly those in my own country of Ireland.

This, I believe, is a clear inconsistency and we must have flexibility in this regard. And it is because of that, I would be proposing amendments catering for Member State opt-outs, where majority farm organisations request them, and clear derogations for cooperatives, so that systems that are already working well are allowed to keep succeeding. As I said, the EU should be about building on successful models, not necessarily undermining them.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna Strolenberg (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, this speech is a waste of everybody's time, because we are talking about burgers. We're talking about banning the word 'burger' if it's made of plant protein instead of animal protein. But I wonder: what if the burger is made of 51Â % animal protein? What if it's 50:50? What if it's not made in the region of Hamburg?

We could have spent this time debating the fact that our planet is on fire, the fact that we have a brutal war on our borders, and that our societies are getting angrier and more divided. And instead, when Europeans look at their leaders, what do they see? They see us discussing burgers. Why is this? It's because the meat lobby is trying to weaken its innovative food competitors. It's trying to weaken farmers, also livestock farmers transitioning to more plant-based products. And my colleagues in the EPP are happy to do the dirty work, instead of actually helping farmers. Because if you want to help farmers, give them stronger contracts, give them a better income, let's help them innovate. Let's give them the support they need to adapt to the climate crisis that is devastating their harvests. Our farmers are too important to be left in the past. So I'm asking all my colleagues: stop talking about burgers and start working on the issues that matter.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jessika Van Leeuwen (PPE), blue-card question. â Dear colleague, I just have one short question for you. You are questioning this 'waste of time' of a debate because apparently we are discussing burgers, but you are the first one mentioning this topic. It is only one amendment of two very important reports. So actually, I believe â and I mean this respectfully â this part was not a very useful contribution to this debate.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna Strolenberg (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. â Jessika Van Leeuwen, maybe you did not listen to your colleague on your left that brought this up in her contribution here â she made it one of the most important points of these files.

What I see is that it is used as a cultural debate although we know that consumers are not confused about the fact of whether a burger is plantâbased or if it is meat.

We see that farmers and also meat producers like RÃ¼genwalder MÃ¼hle, Aldi and Lidl are saying that this ban is not useful. It is a false promise to farmers. So, let's not waste any more time on this.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Everding (The Left). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Direkt vorab: Ich bin der NÃ¤chste, der darÃ¼ber sprechen mÃ¶chte. Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, haben Sie letztens die Schlagzeilen gesehen? Hunderte EuropÃ¤er, schwerst erkrankt, nachdem sie aus Versehen eine vegane Bratwurst gekauft haben. Haben Sie diese Schlagzeilen gesehen? Ich nicht.

Liebe Frau Imart, so ein VorstoÃ wie Ihr Ãnderungsantrag 113 wÃ¼rde komplett ein Urteil des EuropÃ¤ischen Gerichtshofs ignorieren, das besagt, dass die derzeitige Gesetzgebung bereits zum Schutz der Verbraucherinnen und Verbraucher komplett ausreichend ist. Produktnamen wie etwa veganes Sojaschnitzel sind bestens eingefÃ¼hrt. Und Umfragen zeigen, dass rund 80Â Prozent der Verbraucher keinerlei EinwÃ¤nde haben, wenn solche bekannten Begriffe verwendet werden.

Ziel solcher Bezeichnungen ist es, einfach nur zu verdeutlichen, wonach es schmecktÂ â beispielsweise nach Schnitzel. Pflanzliche Lebensmittel sind entscheidend fÃ¼r die Erreichung der EU-Ziele in Bereichen wie Klima, Gesundheit, Tierschutz, aber auch ErnÃ¤hrungssicherheit. Europa ist weltweit fÃ¼hrend im Markt fÃ¼r pflanzliche FleischprodukteÂ âÂ 2024 mit einem Marktvolumen von 2,7 MilliardenÂ Euro. Und dieser Sektor verspricht langfristige Chancen fÃ¼r Landwirte und auch mehr Auswahl fÃ¼r Verbraucherinnen und Verbraucher.

Deswegen stimmen Sie bitte morgen gegen diesen Ãnderungsantrag und gegen die Bevormundung von Verbraucherinnen und Verbrauchern.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Volker Schnurrbusch (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine Damen und Herren! Es geht hier nicht um den Burger oder die Kennzeichnung, es geht um unsere Landwirte. Es geht um die Menschen, die uns jeden Tag mit guten Lebensmitteln versorgen. Weniger BÃ¼rokratie, mehr RÃ¼cksicht auf nationale Eigenheiten, ein realistischer Umgang mit Umweltauflagen â all das fordern wir seit Langem. All das beinhalten die beiden Berichte. Vielen Dank dafÃ¼r auch an die Kollegen.

Unsere Landwirte brauchen eine faire Vertragsgestaltung, insbesondere durch die Verpflichtung, Preis, Menge und Lieferzeitraum im Voraus festzulegen. Genau diese Punkte waren Kernforderungen der Bauernproteste des letzten Jahres, die uns alle so beeindruckt haben. Die Gemeinsame Marktordnung muss mit zusÃ¤tzlichen Schutzmechanismen, mit Transparenzpflichten und fairen Verhandlungsbedingungen flankiert werden. Nur durch klare Regeln und Kontrollen kann sichergestellt werden, dass die Vertragsfreiheit, die zu begrÃ¼Ãen ist, nicht einseitig zulasten der Erzeuger ausgelegt wird. Wir werden beiden Berichten morgen zustimmen, denn wir stehen an der Seite unserer Landwirte, auch und gerade im Interesse der Versorgungssicherheit in unsicheren Zeiten.

Â Â Christophe Clergeau (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, la politique agricole commune est en danger. Je remercie mon collÃ¨gue AndrÃ© Rodrigues d'avoir dÃ©fendu les simplifications utiles, de s'Ãªtre battu pour garder la conditionnalitÃ© sociale et de se battre toujours pour Ã©viter le non-alignement de la PAC sur la lÃ©gislation environnementale de l'Union europÃ©enne. Je le remercie Ã©galement de se battre contre la furie dÃ©rÃ©gulatrice et ultralibÃ©rale du PPE dans cette assemblÃ©e.

Avec 50 milliards d'euros dÃ©pensÃ©s par an, nous pouvons nous demander ce qu'il reste de la conditionnalitÃ© Ã©cologique. Nous entendons maintenant le commissaireÂ â PPE, de droiteÂ â s'inquiÃ©ter des amendementsÂ â PPE, de droiteÂ â et espÃ©rer que le ConseilÂ â PPE, de droiteÂ â va permettre, avec notre aide, de corriger ces errements.

Oui, nous vous aiderons, mais nous sommes inquiets pour l'avenir de la PAC. Quand nous voyons les propositions qui sont prÃ©sentÃ©es pour le futur cadre financier pluriannuel, nous ne pouvons nous empÃªcher de penser que la politique agricole commune est en danger de mort: la renationalisation absolue de la PAC, la baisse de 20Â Ã  30Â % du budget, la mise en concurrence des agriculteurs avec le reste de la sociÃ©tÃ©, l'absence de nouveaux outils pour accompagner la transformation agroÃ©cologique, le financement de la gestion de crise pour affronter les crises climatiques sur le mÃªme budget que les paiements directs.

Qu'est-ce que cela signifie? Dans les prochaines annÃ©es, cela signifie l'absence de transformation de l'agriculture et de moins en moins d'argent pour les agriculteurs. Ce n'est pas la bonne direction que l'Europe doit suivre et j'espÃ¨re qu'ensemble, nous rejetterons ce cadre financier pluriannuel dans quelques semaines.

Â Â Martin HÃ¤usling (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Da fahren wild gewordene Bauern durch BrÃ¼ssel, und was macht sofort die Kommission? Sie sagt, unter dem Deckmantel EntbÃ¼rokratisierung werden viele Umweltvorschriften abgeschafft. Das ist eine EntÃ¶kologisierung der GAP. Herr Kommissar, Sie waren doch selbst dabei damals bei der Erstellung der neuen GAP als Berichterstatter des ENVI-Ausschusses. Haben Sie nicht damals auch gesagt: Das war eine gute Reform? Vergessen.

Wir werden die BiodiversitÃ¤tsziele verfehlen, wir werden GrÃ¼nlandumbruch akzeptieren, was Klima und Umwelt schÃ¤digt. Herr Kommissar, Ihr Vorschlag fÃ¼r die nÃ¤chste GAP ist eine nicht akzeptable Renationalisierung. An einem Punkt sind wir uns einig: Wir mÃ¼ssen die Rechte der Bauern in der Kette stÃ¤rken. Das ist, glaube ich, ein gemeinsames Ziel. Was ich da aus Deutschland hÃ¶re, gerade vom Deutschen Bauernverband, ist abenteuerlich. Die sagen tatsÃ¤chlich, es geht den Weg in den Sozialismus. Â Â Norbert Lins (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Das eine finde ich gut, beim anderen bin ich noch nicht ganz Ã¼berzeugt. Ãber was sprechen wir? Zum einen Ã¼ber die Reform der Gemeinsamen Marktordnung und zum anderen Ã¼ber das Vereinfachungspaket bezÃ¼glich der Gemeinsamen Agrarpolitik unserer EuropÃ¤ischen Union. Ich habe wirklich Bedenken bei der Reform der Gemeinsamen Marktordnung. Ich glaube, wir stellen jahrzehntelange traditionelle Beziehungen, Lieferbeziehungen, stabile WertschÃ¶pfungsketten und MÃ¤rkte infrage mit der jetzigen Reform. Ich glaube, wir schieÃen deutlich Ã¼bers Ziel hinaus. Ich sehe zumindest bei meiner Analyse in den Mitgliedstaaten, wo bisher zum Beispiel der ArtikelÂ 148 nicht umgesetzt worden ist, stabilere MÃ¤rkte, bessere Preise als in den Staaten, wo er bisher schon umgesetzt worden ist. Wir gehen jetzt genau in diese verpflichtenden Regelungen rein.

Ich bin sehr dafÃ¼r, dass wir die GAP weiter vereinfachen. Das geht absolut in die richtige Richtung. Ich glaube, durch unser Mandat stÃ¤rken wir dies noch. Ich bin gespannt auf die Triloge. Vor allem geht es darum, jetzt schnell zu einer LÃ¶sung zu kommen. Die Regeln mÃ¼ssen zum 1. Januar nÃ¤chsten Jahres in Kraft treten. Und wenn es um GrÃ¼nland geht: Ja, wer erzeugt denn den Druck auf GrÃ¼nland? Wenn du Ackerstatus erhalten musst, dann bist du eben nicht in der Lage, dauerhaft GrÃ¼nland weiter zu bewirtschaften. Zum anderen ist doch klar: Die Reduzierung der Rinderherden in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union, die sorgt fÃ¼r mehr Druck aufs GrÃ¼nland. Da sollten alle diejenigen mal darÃ¼ber nachdenken, die dauernd gegen tierische Lebensmittel zu Felde ziehen.

Â Â Camilla Laureti (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, cibo, territorio e ambiente: di tutto questo si occupano gli agricoltori. Per questo, rafforzare la loro posizione Ã¨ una battaglia giusta e garantire loro piÃ¹ sostegno e regole piÃ¹ chiare e meno burocrazia Ã¨ una prioritÃ . Ma semplificazione non puÃ² essere sinonimo di deregolamentazione e slegare la politica agricola comune dalle ambizioni e dalle leggi in materia ambientale non Ã¨ la strada giusta. Per non parlare del costante tentativo delle destre di cancellare la condizionalitÃ  sociale. Non Ã¨ cosÃ¬ che si tutelano gli agricoltori e non si protegge il settore distruggendo le regole che proteggono il clima, il suolo e l'acqua.

Questa PAC Ã¨ entrata in vigore solo due anni fa e siamo giÃ  alla seconda semplificazione. E intanto la Commissione ci presenta proposte per una nuova programmazione che ci preoccupano nel metodo e nel merito. Oltre a meno fondi, rischiamo di non parlare piÃ¹ di politica agricola comune ma di politica agricola della nazione.

Â Â Gilles Pennelle (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, nous devons dÃ©fendre la place de l'agriculteur dans la chaÃ®ne d'approvisionnement; sur ce point, bien Ã©videmment, nous sommes entiÃ¨rement d'accord. Mais c'est un arbre qui cache la forÃªt, une forÃªt bien hostile pour nos agriculteurs.

Ils nous demandent et ils vous demandent, Monsieur le Commissaire: pourquoi les abandonnez-vous? Pourquoi affaiblissez-vous le futur budget de la PAC et les futures aides directes? Pourquoi continuez-vous de leur imposer le funeste pacte vert, unique au monde? Pourquoi multipliez-vous les traitÃ©s de libre-Ã©change, avec l'Ukraine aujourd'hui et le Mercosur demain? Pourquoi faire? Pour financer une guerre dont les EuropÃ©ens ne veulent pas? Pour financer encore plus d'immigration dont les EuropÃ©ens ne veulent pas?

Monsieur le Commissaire, n'abandonnez pas nos agriculteurs. Ils font le plus beau mÃ©tier du monde: ils nous nourrissent, ils entretiennent nos paysages. Ne les abandonnez pas, Monsieur le Commissaire, parce que l'Europe ne peut vivre sans eux.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sergio Berlato (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario,Â troppa carta, troppe regole, troppi controlli che allontanano coloro che lavorano la terra dalla loro vera missione: produrre, innovare, garantire la manutenzione del territorio, garantire la nostra sicurezza alimentare.

Accogliamo con favore questo pacchetto di misure di semplificazione. Ã una risposta, forse tardiva ma necessaria, alle proteste legittime degli imprenditori agricoli che chiedono maggiore flessibilitÃ , meno obblighi burocratici, meno controlli. Ma soprattutto riconosce il ruolo fondamentale delle piccole aziende agricole, che rappresentano i due terzi di tutte le imprese del settore dell'Unione.

Va salutata con positivitÃ  la maggiore attenzione al ricambio generazionale, perchÃ© senza i giovani agricoltori non c'Ã¨ futuro per le nostre campagne. La politica europea, perÃ², non puÃ² essere un esperimento a tempo determinato. Deve offrire certezze e strumenti duraturi a chi produce cibo per l'Europa.

Grazie per la vostra attenzione.

Â Â Carmen Crespo DÃ­az (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, seÃ±or Rodrigues y seÃ±ora Imart, enhorabuena, porque yo creo que son dos objetivos que estamos consiguiendo. En este momento, la simplificaciÃ³n era uno de los objetivos fundamentales en el tema agrario: ampliaciÃ³n a los pequeÃ±os agricultores del pago, flexibilizaciÃ³n de la condicionalidad, ayuda ante catÃ¡strofes, incentivo a los seguros agrarios, simplificaciÃ³n de los planes estratÃ©gicos. Por lo tanto, esto es importantÃ­simo. Y lo mÃ¡s importante es que se va a empezar en el aÃ±o 2026. Por tanto, objetivo conseguido.

Â¿Y ahora quÃ©? Herramientas para un precio justo al agricultor por su producto y por su trabajo. Creo que es importantÃ­simo el equilibrio que se ha dado a este estudio y a este informe porque, ademÃ¡s de todo ello, es fundamental que a las cooperativas se les hayan dado excepcionalidades que en este momento se puedan anotar en los estatutos para quitar problemas. Porque en mi paÃ­s se hizo una ley de reforma de la cadena alimentaria y se hizo tan rÃ¡pido âporque los agricultores estaban en la calleâ que ahora lo que se ha puesto por escrito en los contratos a veces es dificultoso. Por tanto, bienvenidas sean esas excepcionalidades. Creo que es un texto absolutamente equilibrado.

SimplificaciÃ³n, refuerzo de los agricultores en la cadena: un acierto. Ahora tenemos que ir mÃ¡s allÃ¡: mecanismos para los acuerdos comerciales de control, una oficina de control de importaciones absolutamente necesaria que tiene que tener la UniÃ³n Europea. Y, por supuesto, arreglar el marco financiero. Pero no solamente en este caso la ComisiÃ³n, sino tambiÃ©n los Estados miembros. Es importantÃ­simo que le demos esta seÃ±al a todos los agricultores y ganaderos con estos dos aciertos de hoy.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Stefano Bonaccini (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, nonostante il nuovo contesto geopolitico, i cambiamenti climatici, concorrenza internazionale spesso sleale, volatilitÃ  dei prezzi energetici e materie prime, gli agricoltori che incontro non chiedono privilegi, ma solo poter vivere del proprio lavoro senza essere schiacciati dal mercato o dalla burocrazia.

Per rispondere a queste richieste, due obiettivi primari: sostenere redditivitÃ  delle aziende agricole e rafforzare la cooperazione tra agricoltori per poterne migliorare potere contrattuale e competitivitÃ .

I due testi di cui discutiamo sono un primo importante risultato: meno vincoli burocratici sulle spalle degli agricoltori e piÃ¹ forza a cooperative e organizzazioni di produttori, anche tramite contratti d'acquisto scritti obbligatori.

Ma servono anche strumenti nuovi per un rapporto di fiducia tra agricoltura e consumatori. Ã quello che abbiamo fatto con gli emendamenti per difendere l'origine agricola di prodotti come l'aceto e per estendere a tutti i prodotti alimentari l'obbligo di indicazione in etichetta dell'origine della materia prima, consentendo scelte d'acquisto informate e sostenendo il valore aggiunto della produzione agricola europea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mireia BorrÃ¡s PabÃ³n (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, ha pasado casi un aÃ±o ya desde que usted y la seÃ±oraÂ Von der Leyen prometieron que esta serÃ­a la legislatura del diÃ¡logo con los agricultores; que, tras aÃ±os de asfixia normativa, el campo volverÃ­a a ser escuchado y respetado.

Pero la realidad que les ofrecen es otra: costes de producciÃ³n disparados, precios hundidos y acuerdos comerciales desleales como el de Mercosur, que amenazan con arrasar sectores enteros. Solo nos quedaba la PAC, pero es que ya ni eso: la ComisiÃ³n firmÃ³ su acta de defunciÃ³n en julio con un recorte superior al 22Â %, todo un Ã©xito aquÃ­ de los populares y socialistas que gobiernan en Bruselas.

Y es que, comisario, se lo hemos dicho en infinidad de ocasiones: vamos a apoyar todas aquellas medidas que beneficien a nuestro sector primario, sÃ­; las que promuevan su desmantelamiento, no. Por tanto, sÃ­ apoyamos contratos escritos y transparentes para dar mÃ¡s seguridad y poder de negociaciÃ³n a aquellos que mÃ¡s lo necesitan. SÃ­ apoyamos la revisiÃ³n de los contratos a largo plazo para que ningÃºn agricultor venda por debajo de sus costes. SÃ­ apoyamos reforzar a las cooperativas y organizaciones de productores para que puedan negociar con las mejores condiciones. Y sÃ­, por supuesto, apoyamos mÃ¡s incentivos para los jÃ³venes, porque sin relevo generacional no hay futuro para el campo europeo.

Lo que no vamos a validar sin mÃ¡s es su polÃ­tica de rectificaciÃ³n tras comprobar que sus polÃ­ticas ecofanÃ¡ticas y su Pacto Verde Europeo solo nos conducen a la miseria, al abandono y a la despoblaciÃ³n. La simplificaciÃ³n de la PAC debe ser real y total. AsÃ­ que, seÃ±or comisario, devuÃ©lvanle al campo las tres palabras que Bruselas ha olvidado: productividad, rentabilidad y competitividad.

Â

Â Â Asger Christensen (Renew). â Hr. formand! Tak for ordet. Forenkling af landbrugspolitikken er meget, meget vigtig. Derfor har jeg taget gummistÃ¸vlerne pÃ¥ i dag, Hansen, og de er vasket. Mindre bureaukrati, sÃ¥ tiden kan bruges i stalden og i marken. Det giver jo ingen mening, at vi med den ene hÃ¥nd forenkler reglerne, og den anden skaber et nyt bureaukratisk monster. Forslaget om obligatoriske kontrakter i mejerisektoren er et slag i ansigtet pÃ¥ vores andelshavere og andelsselskaber. Det er lovgivning for lovgivningens skyld og ikke sund fornuft. Jeg har vÃ¦ret landmand i over fyrre Ã¥r, og vi har en stÃ¦rk andelsmodel, hvor landmÃ¦ndene ejer mejerierne i fÃ¦llesskab og deler risiko og gevinst. Det sikrer fuld gennemsigtighed. Vi bÃ¸r stÃ¸tte de modeller, der har fungeret i over 150 Ã¥r, og ikke Ã¸delÃ¦gge dem. Derfor skal andelsselskaberne fritages for denne kontaktmodel. Det vil smadre landmÃ¦ndene.

Â Â

Â Â Cristina Guarda (Verts/ALE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Italia chi coltiva canapa industriale Ã¨ trattato come un criminale. Questo Ã¨ uno degli esempi dei risultati della vostra deregolamentazione europea: una finta flessibilitÃ  che crea disparitÃ  tra Stati, lasciando i settori in balia dei capricci nazionali.

Noi chiediamo strategie chiare e meno pesanti per l'agricoltura e in questa relazione vi ho proposto di farlo per un settore, quello della canapa, e ci stiamo riuscendo. Per cui grazie, perchÃ© finalmente riusciamo e iniziamo a difendere una filiera che diversifica il reddito, rigenera i suoli, crea opportunitÃ  ed Ã¨ fatta prevalentemente da giovani e anche da donne.

Agli agricoltori servono nuove strategie sostenibili per generare reddito, non ostacoli, servono contratti sicuri e trasparenti e il divieto di vendere sottocosto di produzione, e sostenibili perchÃ© l'acqua e il suolo fertile non sono infiniti. E invece che cosa fate? Bandite termini termini come "burger vegetale", affaticando un nuovo mercato invece di sostenerlo. Lo fate per codardia o per proteggere chi ha paura. Ma paura di chi? Di chi produce verdure o di chi non vuole mangiare animali?

Â Â Eric Sargiacomo (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, ne nous trompons pas de combat. Dans leur grande majoritÃ©, les agriculteurs comme nos concitoyens ne veulent pas d'une simplification qui rime avec dÃ©rÃ©gulation. Ce que ne supportent pas les agriculteurs, c'est qu'on leur fixe des objectifs Ã©levÃ©s sans leur donner les moyens suffisants pour y parvenir, et on peut les comprendre.

Il faut donc passer de la contrainte Ã  l'incitation; c'est impÃ©ratif. Pour cela, il faut un budget Ã  la hauteur et il faut aussi des protections contre les prix trop bas et la concurrence dÃ©loyale. Le rapport de CÃ©lineÂ Imart va dans ce sens en renforÃ§ant notamment les organisations de producteurs. Mais qu'en restera-t-il si, dans le prochain cadre financier pluriannuel, il n'y a plus d'enveloppe rÃ©servÃ©e Ã  cet effet pour les fruits et lÃ©gumes, pour la viticulture et pour les autres filiÃ¨res?

L'Europe ne peut pas venir les mains vides. Les agriculteurs ne peuvent pas dÃ©pendre de la seule volontÃ© des Ãtats. Monsieur le Commissaire, c'est une Ã©pÃ©e de DamoclÃ¨s au-dessus de ce que la PAC a fait de mieux pour dÃ©velopper les coopÃ©rations entre agriculteurs et renforcer l'amont face Ã  l'aval. Il nous faut trouver les solutions pour rÃ©pondre Ã  ces inquiÃ©tudes lÃ©gitimes,Â pour que le travail qui a Ã©tÃ© fait par CÃ©line Imart, par notre camarade AndrÃ© Rodrigues et par ce Parlement soit respectÃ©.

Â Â

Â Â Barbara Bonte (PfE). â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, de Commissie wil ons doen geloven dat er nu een vereenvoudiging van het landbouwbeleid komt. In werkelijkheid is het maar een cosmetische ingreep, want in de praktijk blijven onze landbouwers verstrikt in een web van regels, sancties en prestatieverslagen, opgelegd door mensen die zelden of nooit een veld of een stal van dichtbij hebben gezien. Landbouwers moeten tegenwoordig elk jaar een hele prestatieboekhouding indienen alsof men hen niet meer vertrouwt. En alsof dat nog niet volstaat, zet de Europese Commissie intussen de deur open voor buitenlandse concurrentie via de Mercosurovereenkomst.

Onze landbouwers verdienen dit wantrouwen niet. Zij moeten opnieuw de vrijheid krijgen om te doen waar ze goed in zijn: produceren, verzorgen, beheren. Minder tijd aan papierwerk, meer tijd op het veld. Wij willen een landbouwbeleid dat ondernemen beloont, innovatie mogelijk maakt en de voedselzekerheid van Europa garandeert. Laat de boer eindelijk terug boer zijn.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Ãn realitate, vom avea parte de mai multÄ birocraÈie, mai multe controale Èi mai puÈinÄ libertate pentru cei care muncesc pÄmÃ¢ntul. Pentru 2028, Comisia pregÄteÈte o reformÄ cu buget Ã®ngheÈat Èi un fond unic, care dilueazÄ plÄÈile directe Èi slÄbeÈte solidaritatea Ã®ntre state. ÈÄrile cu bugete mici, precum RomÃ¢nia, vor avea de pierdut. Ãn timp ce fermierii europeni sunt sufocaÈi de reguli, piaÈa rÄmÃ¢ne deschisÄ produselor din afara Uniunii fÄrÄ sÄ respecte aceleaÈi standarde, ceea ce reprezintÄ un autosabotaj economic.

Sub pretextul Ã®ncurajÄrii tinerilor, Comisia vrea sÄ excludÄ fermierii pensionari de la plata pe hectar, dar Ã®n RomÃ¢nia ei lucreazÄ alÄturi de familiile lor. Excluderea lor nu aduce tineri Ã®n agriculturÄ, ci falimenteazÄ fermele familiale. Fermierii au rÄbdat destul. E timpul ca Uniunea EuropeanÄ sÄ-i asculte, nu sÄ-i pedepseascÄ, sÄ le dea Ã®ncredere, nu hÃ¢rtii de completat, libertate, nu piedici.

Â Â Jutta Paulus (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Und jetzt kommt noch Donald Trump mit seinen ZÃ¶llen. Was unsere Landwirtinnen und Landwirte zu Recht erwarten, sind LÃ¶sungen und keine rechtskonservativen Ablenkungsâ und Kulturkampfdebatten, welches Lebensmittel wie bezeichnet werden darf.

Der Imart-Bericht ist ja im Kern richtig: StÃ¤rkung der Position der Landwirte in der Versorgungskette, schriftliche VertrÃ¤ge als Standard, EinfÃ¼hrung verbindlicher Preisindikatoren, die die Produktionskosten und auch die Markttransparenz widerspiegeln. Aber Teile der Konservativen und leider auch der Liberalen wollen das jetzt aufweichen. VertrÃ¤ge â nur freiwillig. Preise â vage. Transparenz â ach, muss doch nicht sein. Das ist RealitÃ¤tsverleugnung, und das ist eine Ohrfeige fÃ¼r alle, die morgens um fÃ¼nf auf dem Trecker sitzen. Deswegen sagen wir GrÃ¼ne klar: Wir stehen an der Seite der Bauern. Wir sind die Partei der Natur, denn die lebendige Natur ist die Grundlage fÃ¼r alles, was wir ernten wollen. Und ohne faire Preise keine Zukunft auf dem Land.

Â Â Paulo Do Nascimento Cabral (PPE). â Senhor Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, hoje Ã© dia de o felicitar, a si e aos nossos relatores, a CÃ©line Imart e o AndrÃ© Rodrigues, por termos estas duas importantes propostas.

Estamos a reforÃ§ar a posiÃ§Ã£o dos agricultores na cadeia de abastecimento alimentar e a facilitar-lhes a vida.

Defendo que a previsibilidade, o equilÃ­brio negocial para os agricultores e o respeito pelo modelo cooperativo se garantem, tambÃ©m, se as relaÃ§Ãµes comerciais estiverem assentes, por princÃ­pio, em contrato escrito com as derrogaÃ§Ãµes existentes.

Tal como fizemos para o setor do leite, as designaÃ§Ãµes relacionadas com a carne tÃªm de ser efetivamente carne. A prioridade tem de ser dada ao que Ã© produzido na UniÃ£o Europeia e, sempre que exista contrataÃ§Ã£o pÃºblica, a prioridade tambÃ©m deve ser dada aos produtos DOP e IGP.

Quanto Ã  simplificaÃ§Ã£o, nÃ£o aceitamos que se tire dinheiro aos agricultores para financiar situaÃ§Ãµes de crise.

ReforÃ§amos o apoio aos pequenos agricultores para 5.000Â â¬ anuais, e os seus investimentos atÃ© 75.000Â â¬ passam a ter um regime altamente simplificado. Flexibilizamos ainda a gestÃ£o das pastagens permanentes, e as exploraÃ§Ãµes atÃ© 50 hectares ficam isentas da regra da diversificaÃ§Ã£o de culturas. Reduzimos a burocracia e os controlos para as exploraÃ§Ãµes biolÃ³gicas e parcialmente biolÃ³gicas e a Rede Natura 2000. Introduzimos flexibilidade entre o FEADER e o POSEI para os AÃ§ores e a Madeira, pela primeira vez aceite ââ e agradeÃ§o o apoio de todos os colegas a esta minha proposta.

Fica aqui tambÃ©m bem plasmado o nosso compromisso com os agricultores europeus: menos burocracia; menos papelada; mais justiÃ§a; mais rendimentos; mais tempo no terreno a fazer o que melhor sabem ââ produzir alimentos de qualidade, sustentÃ¡veis e a preÃ§os acessÃ­veis para os europeus. Mais tempo no campo, menos tempo nos gabinetes e, espero, posiÃ§Ãµes firmes do Parlamento Europeu.
