Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MaÅgorzata Gosiewska (ECR). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Z niepokojem obserwujemy, jak w wielu krajach Europy odradza siÄ antysemityzm. Na ulicach pojawia siÄ nienawiÅÄ. Å»ydowskie szkoÅy i synagogi muszÄ byÄ strzeÅ¼one, podobnie jak restauracje czy inne miejsca publiczne. To haÅba dla Europy. ChcÄ jednak wyraÅºnie podkreÅliÄ: w Polsce spoÅecznoÅÄ Å¼ydowska Å¼yje dziÅ bezpiecznie. Mamy wielowiekowÄ tradycjÄ ich obecnoÅci w naszej ojczyÅºnie. Å»ydzi wspÃ³Åtworzyli polskÄ tradycjÄ, polskÄ kulturÄ, naukÄ, gospodarkÄ. A my Polacy, ktÃ³rzy â jak narÃ³d Å¼ydowski â doÅwiadczyliÅmy niemieckiego ludobÃ³jstwa II wojny Åwiatowej, wiemy, czym jest nienawiÅÄ oraz pogarda wobec czÅowieka.

Szanowni PaÅstwo, wspÃ³Åczesny antysemityzm w Europie Zachodniej rodzi siÄ na Bliskim Wschodzie oraz z nielegalnej imigracji z krajÃ³w islamskich, gdzie nienawiÅÄ wobec Å»ydÃ³w jest czÄÅciÄ wychowania i ideologii. Polska, ktÃ³ra nie dopuÅciÅa do masowej migracji, pozostaje miejscem bezpiecznym. A to Wy ÅciÄgnÄliÅcie antysemityzm do swoich krajÃ³w. Europa nie potrzebuje dziÅ pustych sloganÃ³w, lecz odwagi, by broniÄ swoich granic, wartoÅci i obywateli.

(MÃ³wczyni nie zgodziÅa siÄ na pytanie zasygnalizowane przez podniesienie niebieskiej kartki przez Evin Incir)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Katri Kulmuni (Renew). â MrÂ President, one may ask why antisemitism needs to be addressed separately, not just as part of other forms of racism. However, the history of antisemitism is unique. It rises repeatedly, during different eras, among different cultures and religions.

After the Holocaust, Europeans said: 'never again'. But once again, Jewish people are afraid for their safety. Synagogue-guarding has had to be increased. Some youths have said that the Star of David necklace is better to be hidden in certain public places.

But this didn't begin with the Holocaust: persecutions, pogroms, the Inquisition. Antisemitism is not just one ugly page in the European history book; unfortunately, it is repeated in every chapter.

This has to be taken seriously, and all the people â and the governments â of Europe must raise their voices against it. The history and development of Europe is strongly based on Judeo-Christian values. Jewish history is part of our history, and there shouldn't be any place for antisemitism on this continent.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Antonio LÃ³pez-IstÃºriz White (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, hace exactamente dos aÃ±os el mundo fue testigo del ataque mÃ¡s brutal y horrendo contra el pueblo judÃ­o desde el Holocausto. Dos aÃ±os despuÃ©s, muchos siguen mirando hacia otro lado y demasiados se niegan a llamar barbarie a lo que es una barbarie.

Es profundamente trÃ¡gico que mi propio paÃ­s, una naciÃ³n cuya identidad, progreso y cultura son inseparables de la comunidad judÃ­a, se haya convertido hoy en uno de los epicentros del antisemitismo en Europa. Una vez mÃ¡s, la historia se repite. Algunos polÃ­ticos utilizan el antisemitismo como arma, canalizando la frustraciÃ³n de los ciudadanos para ocultar su incompetencia y su corrupciÃ³n, alimentando el populismo en lugar de ejercer liderazgo.

La Vuelta a EspaÃ±a, uno de los eventos deportivos mÃ¡s importantes de mi paÃ­s, fue suspendida tras ataques violentos âÃºnicamente porque participaban atletas israelÃ­esâ alentados por el propio Gobierno. Creyentes, deportistas, e incluso niÃ±os en aviones, se han convertido en chivo expiatorio, en blancos mÃ³viles, vÃ­ctimas de la cobardÃ­a polÃ­tica.

Seamos claros, Europa tiene un problema de antisemitismo, y estÃ¡ creciendo. Pero esto debe acabar ya.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannes Heide (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Heute, am 7.Â Oktober, gedenken wir der 1200 Ermordeten und 250Â Verschleppten des terroristischen Ãberfalls der Hamas. Am Nova Music Festival wurden 370Â junge Menschen, die feiern und Freiheit erleben wollten, brutal ermordet. Mir sind auch jene Szenen aus Ãberwachungskameras in Erinnerung geblieben, als ein Familienvater vor den Augen seiner zwei SÃ¶hne â im Alter meines Sohnes â ermordet wird. Ein Terrorist trinkt anschlieÃend vor den verletzten Kindern aus einer Flasche aus deren KÃ¼hlschrank. Und immer noch sind Menschen in der Gewalt dieser Terroristen.

Und es ist nicht tolerierbar, wenn in der Folge das AusmaÃ des Holocaust relativiert, auf Internetplattformen geleugnet, in Lokalen jÃ¼dischen GÃ¤sten der Zutritt verweigert wird. Bildung hat Teil jeder Strategie im Kampf gegen Antisemitismus zu sein. So sollte jede europÃ¤ische SchÃ¼lerin, jeder europÃ¤ische SchÃ¼ler eine Holocaust-GedenkstÃ¤tte besuchen, dort die Dimension dieser Verbrechen gegen die Menschlichkeit erkennen kÃ¶nnen. JÃ¼disches Leben und Kulturerbe sind Teil meiner Ã¶sterreichischen und unserer europÃ¤ischen IdentitÃ¤t. Jede JÃ¼din und jeder Jude in Europa muss sich sicher fÃ¼hlen und sicher sein. Das ist unser Auftrag.

ÎÎ½ Î¸Î­Î»Î¿ÏÎ½ ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ®Î½Î·, Î±Ï Î±ÏÎµÎ»ÎµÏÎ¸ÎµÏÏÏÎµÎ¹ Î· Î§Î±Î¼Î¬Ï ÏÎ¿ÏÏ Î¿Î¼Î®ÏÎ¿ÏÏ ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î½Î± Î´ÎµÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ½ ÏÎ¿ ÏÏÎ­Î´Î¹Î¿ Î¤ÏÎ±Î¼Ï. ÎÎ»Î»Î¹ÏÏ, Î½Î± ÎµÎ¾Î±ÏÎ±Î½Î¹ÏÏÎ¿ÏÎ½. Î Î±Î»Î±Î¹ÏÏÎ¹Î½Î¹Î±ÎºÏ ÎºÏÎ¬ÏÎ¿Ï Î´ÎµÎ½ ÏÏÎ¬ÏÏÎµÎ¹.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexandr Vondra (ECR). â MrÂ President, just two years after the most horrific pogrom against Jews since the Second World War, we see the European Jewry once again facing daily abuse, verbal attacks, beatings, even killings.

We see activists, including some of our own Members here, sailing alongside those who sponsored Hamas terrorists, supporting their claim to deny Israel's very right to existence in security and peace. I would never have imagined that such a picture could return to our continent. At the very peak of this iceberg of hatred stands Greta Thunberg, a once-celebrated young woman who has already filled this House with her radical green ideology. It is our moral duty to do everything in our power to melt this iceberg of antisemitism down, to eradicate this most ancient and repugnant form of hatred from our societies.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Miriam Lexmann (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, two years ago we woke up to the tragic news of Hamas's brutal and barbaric evil attack on Israel â an attack that left thousands of civilians dead or taken hostage and unleashed incredible suffering that continues today.

Today, in Europe, we are witnessing a growing wave of antisemitism, or Jew hatred, to put it in blunter terms. Jew hatred manifested in attacks on synagogues, Jewish businesses and cemeteries being vandalised frequently. This picture hurts, because Europe was built on Judaeo-Christian heritage. It is our duty to stand firm on our values. If we look away when facing hatred in society, we betray our core European DNA.

Today, we live in hope that what many of us call the Holy Land will embark on the path of peace. Let us therefore unite in prayer for peace, for the Israeli and Palestinian people, and for the return of all hostages. Let us pray that tolerance will fill our hearts in Europe, where many Jewish people have their homes.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julie Rechagneux (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, je viens d'un pays, la France, oÃ¹, enÂ 2024, plus de 2Â 000Â de mes compatriotes de confession juive ont fait leur alya vers IsraÃ«l. Je viens d'un pays oÃ¹, enÂ 2024, plus de 1Â 500Â actes antisÃ©mites ont Ã©tÃ© recensÃ©s. Ces personnes se disent parfois plus en sÃ©curitÃ© dans un pays en guerre que dans les rues de leur propre pays.

La cause de cette crainte est claire: il y a bien eu un Â«effet 7Â octobreÂ». Les atrocitÃ©s commises par le Hamas contre des civils israÃ©liens ont dÃ©sinhibÃ© des passions antisÃ©mites malsaines. Le Hamas a rÃ©ussi Ã  unir les luttes de l'extrÃªme gauche et de l'islamisme dans un mÃªme islamo-palestinisme, nouveau visage de l'antisÃ©mitisme. Ã Bordeaux, oÃ¹ je vis, l'extrÃªme gauche a poussÃ© la majoritÃ© Ã©cologiste Ã  suspendre le jumelage de notre ville avec le port d'Ashdod en IsraÃ«l. C'est lÃ  que se trouve l'antisÃ©mitisme contemporain. Il faut avoir le courage de le combattre sur ce terrain en prioritÃ©.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, io penso che non siamo qui soltanto oggi per esprimere il grande sentimento di cordoglio a due anni dai fatti del 7 ottobre. Siamo qui per riflettere sulle ragioni di un crescente antisemitismo in Europa e penso che ognuno di noi, quale che sia la propria appartenenza, si riconosca anche nel grido di preoccupazione che Papa Leone XIV, proprio su questo tema, in Europa e parlando al mondo, ha lanciato.

Ma la risposta sull'antisemitismo di oggi, che Ã¨ molto diverso da quello tragico del Novecento, viene dal Suo portafoglio, commissario Brunner, che Ã¨ quello degli Affari interni e della migrazione. PerchÃ© saremmo molto lontani dalla veritÃ  se non riconoscessimo che in un'Europa che ha dimenticato le proprie radici cristiane, le proprie radici giudaico-cristiane, oggi c'Ã¨ un fenomeno di immigrazione che rischia di essere incontrollato. E allora a Lei anche questo compito.

(L'oratore accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D), blue-card question. â You and your colleagues in ECR are shamelessly blaming Greta Thunberg and migration for antisemitism, but I have a question for you.

Have you discussed with your colleagues in the Sweden Democrats, why they support a government in Sweden that made an agreement with the antisemite and Hamas-loving Prime Minister of Somalia? They made a secret agreement wherein Somalia will take 28 Somalian refugees, and they will get SEKÂ 100Â million â EURÂ 10Â million â as an exchange. A Hamas-loving, antisemite Prime Minister. How does the discussion go in ECR about your colleagues in the Sweden Democrats?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ruggero Razza (ECR), risposta a una domanda "cartellino blu". â Non Ã¨ un'opinione del gruppo dei Conservatori e Riformisti che una parte dell'antisemitismo di oggi sia legato a fenomeni di islamizzazione di massa. Semmai il tema Ã¨ la risposta, non guardare un singolo caso. E, se devo essere sincero, collega, cercare in una giornata come questa la polemica a tutti i costi non penso Le faccia onore.

Â Â CÃ©line Imart (PPE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers commissaires, en ce jour anniversaire des massacres du 7Â octobre et, en cette annÃ©eÂ â faut-il le rappeler?Â â qui marque les 80Â ans de la libÃ©ration d'Auschwitz, l'Histoire devrait nous rappeler jusqu'oÃ¹ peut mener le poids de nos compromissions.

Â Â Silvia Sardone (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'attentato di Manchester Ã¨ solo l'ultimo drammatico episodio di violenza legata all'estremismo islamico. Fino a quando farete finta di nulla di fronte al pericolo dell'Islam radicale? Fino a quando starete in silenzio di fronte ad attentati, pestaggi e intimidazioni nei confronti degli ebrei? Fino a quando l'Europa si sottometterÃ  a chi sogna la sharia nel nostro continente? PerchÃ©, diciamolo chiaramente, Ã¨ esattamente quello che sta accadendo.

Le istituzioni europee, la sinistra in particolare, sono succubi delle comunitÃ  islamiche e chiudono gli occhi di fronte all'estremismo che si diffonde sempre piÃ¹ forte, prendendo di mira gli ebrei, ma anche noi cristiani.

Un'Europa debole e silenziosa non serve a nulla, se non difendiamo i nostri valori e le nostre libertÃ , finiremo per essere calpestati da chi sogna un'Europa islamica. Noi non dobbiamo piegarci. Io non voglio l'Europa islamica.

Â Â Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Antisemitismus ist Antisemitismus ist Antisemitismus. Daran gibt es nichts zu relativieren. Antisemitismus bekÃ¤mpfe ich aber nicht mit Rassismus. Antisemitismus ist nie weg gewesen. Er war nicht plÃ¶tzlich vom Himmel gefallen vor zwei Jahren. Er ist nicht 2015, 2016 vom Himmel gefallen. Nein, er war schon seit Hunderten von Jahren in Europa. Er ist nie weg gewesen. Er ist nie weg gewesen im Bildungsbereich, im Medienbereich. Wenn wir JÃ¼dinnen und Juden fragen, sagen sie einem das auch.

Ich bekÃ¤mpfe nicht Menschenfeindlichkeit mit Menschenfeindlichkeit. Wir sollten uns auch ganz klar vergegenwÃ¤rtigen: diejenigen, die von Diskriminierung, Ausgrenzung und Hass und Hetze betroffen sindÂ â und das sind sehr, sehr viele â, die Opfer des Faschismus waren, und diejenigen, die sich plÃ¶tzlich solidarisch an die eine oder andere Seite stellenÂ â das ist heuchlerisch.

Also mÃ¼ssen wir uns auch klar vor Augen fÃ¼hren: Was kann Antisemitismus bekÃ¤mpfen? Es ist nicht die KÃ¼rzung von Demokratieprojekten, wie es gerade bei uns in Deutschland passiert im Rahmen von Haushaltsplanberatungen. Es ist nicht damit getan, dass manÂ gute Reden hÃ¤ltÂ â sondern Antisemitismus bekÃ¤mpfe ich auf unterschiedlichen Ebenen. Das muss uns allen klar sein. DafÃ¼r brauchen wir Geld, Strukturen und nicht mehr Hass und Hetze und falsche SolidaritÃ¤t von rechts.

Â Â Alexander Sell (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Vor wenigen Tagen hat die Berliner Polizei drei Hamas-Terroristen bei der Ãbergabe von Waffen festgenommenÂ â mitten in Berlin. Hamas-FunktionÃ¤re leben in Berlin vom BÃ¼rgergeld, und die Berliner CDU wirft diesen Leuten noch den deutschen Pass hinterher. Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Menschen Europas! Es gibt rechtsextremen Antisemitismus, es gibt linksextremen Antisemitismus, es gibt migrantischen Antisemitismus, es gibt Antisemitismus in Witzen und wenn er ernst gemeint ist. Nach diesem Urteil darf man den ThÃ¼ringer Landesvorsitzenden und ThÃ¼ringer Fraktionsvorsitzenden der AfD, BjÃ¶rn HÃ¶cke, wegen seiner nationalsozialistischen Aussagen einen Faschisten nennen. Â Â Tiago Moreira de SÃ¡ (PfE). â Senhor Presidente, hoje, no segundo aniversÃ¡rio do 7 de outubro, a Europa enche-se de manifestaÃ§Ãµes, nÃ£o para lembrÃ¡-lo, mas para apagÃ¡-lo, nÃ£o para recordar as vÃ­timas, nem para exigir a libertaÃ§Ã£o dos refÃ©ns ainda em Gaza, mas para ignorÃ¡-los.

A Europa estÃ¡ de novo doente, da mesma infeÃ§Ã£o moral que hÃ¡ menos de um sÃ©culo conduziu Ã  perseguiÃ§Ã£o e ao extermÃ­nio dos judeus. Na Europa do nunca mais, os judeus, para nÃ£o terem de partir, voltam a esconder-se, e os que nÃ£o querem esconder-se voltam a partir.

Assistimos, diariamente, Ã  inversÃ£o do holocausto, Ã  apropriaÃ§Ã£o miserÃ¡vel do sofrimento do povo judeu para acusar os prÃ³prios judeus.

A Europa entrega-se, hoje, de novo, Ã s suas duas paixÃµes prediletas: a condolÃªncia e a condenaÃ§Ã£o. Adora exibir condolÃªncias por judeus mortos e condenar os judeus que se recusam a morrer.

Hoje, cada um de nÃ³s pode ainda escolher o lado em que estÃ¡ no combate contra o anti-semitismo. O que nÃ£o podemos escolher Ã© o lugar que a histÃ³ria reserva para nÃ³s, ou contra nÃ³s, para toda a eternidade.

Â Â Jaume Asens LlodrÃ  (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as del Partido Popular, ninguna lecciÃ³n de antisemitismo o judeofobia, porque los palestinos tambiÃ©n son semitas. Nosotros somos los hijos polÃ­ticos de quienes con los judÃ­os luchamos frente al nazismo y al fascismo. Ustedes son los hijos polÃ­ticos de quienes les perseguÃ­an y los querÃ­an fuera. Por eso se aliaron con los sionistas, porque tambiÃ©n tenÃ­an el mismo objetivo.

Y claro, parece que ya no odian a los judÃ­os. OrbÃ¡n, por ejemplo. Pero el problema no es que hayan dejado de odiar a los judÃ­os, es que odian mÃ¡s a los musulmanes y por eso se han aliado con Israel. Porque como decÃ­a el canciller alemÃ¡n: Israel hace el trabajo sucio de Occidente. Y ustedes, cuando confunden lo que hace Israel con la judeofobia, estÃ¡n haciendo un flaco favor a los judÃ­os. Porque hay muchos judÃ­os que son crÃ­ticos con lo que hace Israel. El mismo Primo Levi, Jean AmÃ©ry, vÃ­ctimas del holocausto, judÃ­os que criticaban a Israel por lo que hacÃ­a a Palestina.

SegÃºn ustedes, odiarÃ­an a los judÃ­os, se odiarÃ­an a sÃ­ mismos. Dejen de mezclar los conceptos, porque estÃ¡n promoviendo la judeofobia y estÃ¡n ofendiendo a las vÃ­ctimas reales de ella.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Magnus Brunner, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, the European Commission â and I can just say that again, like I did before â stands firmly against all forms of antisemitism, hatred and also discrimination altogether. We stand ready to defend our democratic values, and we have to do this all together â as the Commission, as Parliament, as the Member States, with the Council. We rely on allies like you as elected Members of this House, of course, and also, of course, on governments across Europe to each do their share, and we engage with civil society as we aim for an 'all of society' approach to uphold a prosperous democracy in an open, inclusive and equal society in the European Union.

Thanks again for all your contributions today. I think we saw that we are all fighting on the same side, mostly.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you, Commissioner, for the statement and for the debate.

The debate is closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR), in writing. â Jewish people no longer feel safe in Europe.

They â once again â have to learn to hide their identities.

They're called "Zionists" as an insult.

They're called "white colonizers" despite Jewish Israelis are really diverse.

But when you look deeper, what they actually mean is: "Jew".

Antisemitism always hides behind different names to conceal itself, as it has for millennia.

And it's no different today.

This is not the first time Jews have been demonised on this continent: refused services, barred from competitions and attacked on the streets for their ethnicity.

History is sadly repeating itself.

Shame on Europe for not learning from its past mistakes.

The only difference now is that the Jewish people have a homeland and they can â and will â defend themselves.

Â 

13. Situation in Afghanistan: supporting women and communities affected by the recent earthquakes (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is Council and Commission statements on 'Situation in Afghanistan: supporting women and communities affected by the recent earthquakes' (2025/2872(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, let me start by expressing our full support to women and communities affected by the recent earthquakes in Afghanistan. We have seen the heart-breaking pictures in the eastern part of the country â from Kunar, from Nangarhar and from Laghman.

The numbers are devastating: over 2Â 000 dead, thousands of injured and around 5Â 000 homes destroyed. The recent earthquakes have put at risk the means of living of around half a million people. This is a huge challenge in a country where 23Â million Afghans â half of the population â need humanitarian assistance to survive. The Taliban regime is responsible for further complicating the terrible humanitarian and economic situation.

The situation is serious, and the US stopping all funding for humanitarian aid does not help. This year, the EU and its Member States have so far provided almost EURÂ 210Â million. This is more than 30Â % of all humanitarian aid to Afghanistan this year. When it comes to the response to the recent earthquakes, the EU has also been one of the leading actors. The EU Humanitarian Air Bridge delivered 130 tonnes of supplies.

Now, the second phase of response begins with the preparation for the winter. Afghans need winter-ready shelters, food and cash assistance, clean water, health services; women, children and the elderly need protection.

But let us not forget that Afghanistan has become one of the most difficult places for NGOs to operate. The oppression of women is a major problem. Time and again, we have reaffirmed our commitment to gender equality and gender-responsive action. We have repeatedly recalled the crucial role of women for the maintenance and promotion of peace and security in Afghanistan. We need to make the voices of Afghan women heard again.

We have heard the critical voices in Afghanistan about the Taliban response to the earthquake, including the lack of female doctors and the need for trained female professionals. We will continue calling on the Taliban to guarantee that all aid workers, including all female staff, can continue their aid delivery and have access to the entire territory. We especially request the Taliban to immediately end the ban on female Afghan staff accessing UN premises.

The EU strongly condemns the decisions by the Taliban that have led women and girls to be excluded from public life, denying their human rights, including the right to education, work and freedom of movement. We will closely monitor the humanitarian situation and the situation of women in Afghanistan and coordinate our action with our like-minded international donors.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, allow me to start by saying that our thoughts go out to all those who have lost loved ones, the injured and the emergency responders. The European Union stands in solidarity with the people of Afghanistan.

On the night of 31Â August, a powerful earthquake struck Afghanistan near the border with Pakistan. Measuring over 6 on the Richter scale, it caused massive destruction across Nangarhar and Kunar provinces. Almost 2Â 000 people were killed. More than 3Â 600 were injured. The UN estimates that at least 450Â 000 people were directly affected. This earthquake also caused large-scale damage to infrastructure. Roughly 6Â 700 homes were destroyed, along with schools, community centres, transport links, water and sanitation systems. The risk of a widespread cholera outbreak is now extremely high, made worse by shortages of essential hygiene and sanitation items in a region where cholera was already spreading. This devastation struck an area close to the Pakistani border, where thousands of vulnerable Afghans were forced to return from Pakistan over the past year. The earthquake has deepened an already dire humanitarian crisis. With winter approaching, urgent needs for food, shelter, health and protection will only increase, so we must urgently scale up humanitarian assistance to avoid further deaths, epidemics, starvation and human suffering.

Dear colleagues, the European Union responded swiftly and effectively. Within hours, DG ECHO staff based in Afghanistan flew to the affected areas to carry out a rapid assessment and identify the most urgent needs. We immediately mobilised EURÂ 1.5 million in emergency funding for humanitarian partners, with local presence and rapid response capacity. The Commission also organised six humanitarian air bridge flights, delivering 450 metric tonnes of life-saving supplies and this included medical relief, shelter and winterisation materials. We are also deploying a specialised emergency medical team, including trauma physiotherapists, to support the survivors. At the same time, EU humanitarian programmes already active in these regions, including support for Afghan returnees from Pakistan, were quickly adapted to meet the most urgent needs of women, children and displaced families.

Women and children are paying the highest price. They face heightened protection risks in displacement in a society where they have no choice and cannot move freely. And this is why our response prioritises food and livelihood assistance, medical help, the rehabilitation of water and sanitation facilities, shelter and protection. All actions are designed to meet the specific needs of the most vulnerable and especially women. Our humanitarian funding is gender-sensitive. It supports gender-specific needs, and all programmes are tailored to the specific vulnerabilities of women and girls. In the health sector, it supports maternal and reproductive healthcare, which are critical for women, especially in the disaster-affected area. Pregnant and lactating women are central to our food security and nutrition interventions. Our partners underground include local organisations that work closely with civil society, and this ensures a better understanding of the situation in the field and the specific needs of women in Afghanistan. Through the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent and the direct involvement of the Afghan Red Crescent Society, we are also able to work with Afghan women beyond the health sector.

Dear colleagues, in Afghanistan, every act of solidarity counts. Every life saved, every woman supported, every child protected is a step towards hope. Europe will continue to stand by the Afghan people with compassion, with determination, and with the conviction that even in the darkest moments, humanity must prevail.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ingeborg Ter Laak, namens de PPE-Fractie. â Voorzitter, commissaris, collegaâs, de dagelijkse strijd die veel vrouwen en meisjes moeten voeren in Afghanistan is al erg genoeg. Dat ze bij een verschrikkelijke ramp als een aardbeving niet de zorg krijgen die ze nodig hebben, is echter onacceptabel. Voor vrouwen en meisjes is de situatie na de aardbeving in Oost-Afghanistan nog beklemmender geworden. De enorme hoeveelheid restricties voor vrouwen die door de taliban zijn opgelegd, maken hulp aan vrouwen onmogelijk, terwijl die hulp keihard nodig is. Door het isolement waar vrouwen toe worden verplicht, kunnen vrouwen niet naar school en dus ze kunnen geen hulpverlener worden. En aangezien mannen vrouwen niet mogen helpen, krijgen vrouwen geen medische hulp. De taliban hebben vrouwen niet alleen hun waardigheid afgenomen, maar ook het recht op gezondheidszorg.

De Europese Unie moet blijvend druk uitoefenen om mensenrechten, en in het bijzonder de rechten van vrouwen en meisjes, te beschermen. Geen enkele samenleving kan zich ontwikkelen, kan wederopbouwen en kan tot zijn recht komen als de helft van de bevolking wordt uitgesloten. De taliban moeten vrouwen actief betrekken bij hulpverlening, en medische zorg voor vrouwen toelaten, zodat alle slachtoffers, ongeacht hun geslacht, toegang krijgen tot medische hulp. Laat er een einde komen aan de structurele uitsluiting van vrouwen en meisjes.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alessandra Moretti, a nome del gruppo S&D. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, il terremoto che ha colpito il nord-est dell'Afghanistan ha ucciso piÃ¹ di 2Â 000 persone. Tra queste sono certamente piÃ¹ numerose le donne degli uomini. PerchÃ©? PerchÃ©, a causa delle leggi imposte dai talebani, le donne non possono essere toccate dagli uomini. Infatti, i soccorritori, che ovviamente erano tutti uomini, perchÃ© ricordiamo che le donne non possono lavorare, le hanno lasciate morire sotto le macerie semplicemente perchÃ© non potevano toccarle.

Inoltre, alle donne Ã¨ negata l'assistenza sanitaria, anche l'assistenza d'urgenza che potrebbe salvare loro la vita. E questo perchÃ©, sempre alle donne, Ã¨ vietato lavorare come medici.

Quella a cui stiamo assistendo Ã¨ un'intollerabile violazione di ogni diritto, Ã¨ la negazione di ogni libertÃ  basata esclusivamente sul genere. CiÃ² rappresenta un apartheid, un apartheid di genere, e dovremmo avere tutti il coraggio di riconoscere questo crimine all'interno del diritto internazionale e all'interno della civiltÃ  giuridica europea.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Matthieu Valet, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, alors que le pays se relÃ¨ve encore d'une guerre de vingtÂ ans entre les talibans, Daech et le pouvoir dÃ©mocratique, l'Afghanistan est frappÃ© par un tremblement de terre inÃ©dit et meurtrier.

Comme beaucoup, j'ai apprÃ©ciÃ© que ce dÃ©bat mette les femmes au cÅur de l'enjeu humanitaire; ces femmes, victimes au mÃªme titre que tous les civils, mais que les secours refusent parfois de toucher par dogmatisme religieux. Mais dans ce pays comme dans tant d'autres, les femmes Ã©taient des victimes bien avant les catastrophes naturelles et le resteront malheureusement aprÃ¨s.

En effet, le vÃ©ritable tremblement de terre a lieu ici mÃªme. C'est l'abandon complice par l'Europe de rÃ©gions entiÃ¨res du monde, ou pire, de rÃ©gions livrÃ©es aux islamistes. C'est le soutien affichÃ©, dÃ©complexÃ©, comme en Syrie, Ã  des leaders issus du terrorisme qui frappaient autrefois la France en plein cÅur, comme d'autres pays d'EuropeÂ â notamment l'Espagne et, autrefois, le Royaume-Uni.

N'attendons pas des phÃ©nomÃ¨nes naturels pour nous rÃ©volter. Les femmes, comme bien d'autres minoritÃ©sÂ â notamment les chrÃ©tiens d'OrientÂ â sont systÃ©matiquement les victimes de tous ces nouveaux rÃ©gimes qui profitent de votre lÃ¢chetÃ© politique, de l'hypocrisie qui rÃ¨gne dans ce Parlement et dans nos institutions.

En conclusion, permettez-moi de saluer la mÃ©moire de nos soldats, nos soldats franÃ§ais, mais aussi europÃ©ens, qui ont perdu leur vie en Afghanistan pour lutter jusqu'au bout de leurs forces contre cet islamisme.

Â

DziÅ oczywiÅcie nie wiemy, co wydarzyÅo siÄ i co zrobiÅby prezydent Trump, lecz z pewnoÅciÄ nie pozwoliÅby talibanowi przejÄÄ sprzÄtu wojskowego wartoÅci 7 miliardÃ³w dolarÃ³w ani zaprzepaÅciÄ dorobku caÅej spoÅecznoÅci miÄdzynarodowej z ostatnich dwÃ³ch dekad. W tym czasie talibowie ukryli siÄ w jaskiniach w gÃ³rach, a mieszkaÅcy Kabulu, takÅ¼e kobiety, mogli korzystaÄ z edukacji i innych dÃ³br cywilizacji. Przez pÃ³Åtora roku przed ewakuacjÄ armia USA nie poniosÅa strat. DorastaÅo wtedy caÅe pokolenie AfgaÅczykÃ³w, ktÃ³re zostaÅo porzucone i zdane na ÅaskÄ radykalnych islamistÃ³w. Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle, on behalf of the Renew Group. â MrÂ President, dear Minister, CommissionerÂ Lahbib, dear Hadja, dear colleagues, last month, an earthquake took the lives of at least 2Â 200 people in Afghanistan, swallowing villages whole and leaving entire communities devastated.

Now imagine being a woman, a girl, in this situation, under the Taliban regime. Food and water are being handed out in the valley below your village, but you are not allowed to travel there â without a man, anyway â so you starve. Doctors are providing aid, but they are not willing to help you because they fear the consequences of touching you. While winter is setting in, you have no place to go, because as a woman alone, you are not welcome anywhere. The best you can do is actually get married again, quickly, but who will take you?

Natural disasters tend to affect the most vulnerable the most. But the Taliban are taking this to a whole new level. What we see in Afghanistan is not inequality or simple discrimination. It has a name, and that name is gender apartheid: a system built to erase women from public life and, eventually, a system to erase women as human beings.

The world needs to acknowledge this reality and recognise gender apartheid in international law. Make it a crime, not just a tragedy we watch in the news. And hold those guilty accountable.

Let us be the voice of those who are now silenced â for Afghan women, for all women.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Just one piece of information: they are fixing the screen so that the names are shown. But I will use the list that I think most of you are aware of.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannah Neumann, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, Afghanistan is the only country where girls are forbidden to go to school, where women are banned from work, from travel â even from speaking. Half a nation erased from public life. This is not culture. It is not internal affairs. It is gender apartheid.

While rescuers work to save families buried by earthquakes, the Taliban cut the internet, isolating 40Â million in a deliberate digital darkness. This is calculated cruelty.

Dear colleagues, three years ago, all of us in here said we will not turn our back on Afghanistan. Today, that promise is being broken. Instead of standing with Afghan women, European governments travel to Kabul in secret, bargaining with terrorists over deportations, trading away rights and promises for short-sighted deals and a handful of votes.

But our duty is the opposite: to name the Taliban's crimes, not to normalise them; to speak up for Afghan women, not to silence them; and to codify gender apartheid as a crime against humanity, so that evidence is preserved, justice is possible, and these shady deals are denounced as what they are â complicity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ãzlem Demirel, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die katastrophale Lage von Frauen und MÃ¤dchen in Afghanistan spitzt sich zu. Das Erdbeben hat die Lage noch verschlimmert. Unter den Taliban sind Frauen in Afghanistan faktisch aus dem Ã¶ffentlichen Leben, von Bildung, von Hilfe, von Medizin ausgeschlossen. Es herrscht eine Geschlechterapartheid, und die Lage ist geprÃ¤gt von Verbrechen gegen die Menschlichkeit.

Kolleginnen und Kollegen! 20Â Jahre NATO-Krieg haben bewiesen, dass Krieg Gewalt gegen Frauen verstÃ¤rktÂ â nicht verbessert. Mit dem chaotischen Abzug hat man den Taliban sogar noch Hightech-FoltergerÃ¤t und Spyware Ã¼berlassen. Die aktuellen Verhandlungen der EU-Mitgliedstaaten mit den Taliban fÃ¼r schnellere Abschiebungen haben bewiesen, dass sie nicht fÃ¼r hehre Werte stehen. KrokodilstrÃ¤nen helfen tatsÃ¤chlich nicht den Menschen vor Ort. Was Afghaninnen und Afghanen brauchen, sind humanitÃ¤re Hilfe, Verhandlungen zur StÃ¤rkung der Rechte von Frauen, FamilienzusammenfÃ¼hrungen in Europa und ein Ende der Abschiebungen nach Afghanistan. Was sie nicht brauchen, sind Ãberlegungen fÃ¼r neue Kriegsspielchen im Nahen Osten.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Mandl (PPE). â Mr President, Commissioner, Minister, today marks 7Â October. After 11Â September 2001, the world took care of Afghanistan, but it didn't work out well. After 7Â October 2023, we will see whether the world takes care of the Palestinian territories and where it will lead, because the ideology of different rights for men and women, the ideology of treating not only men and women differently, but also boys and girls, different communities differently, leads always from bad to worse. This happens in many kinds of ideological regimes, and this happens in Islamist regimes especially, and these days in particular.

This is why the earthquake of 31Â August is a big tragedy. It caused more than 2Â 000 losses in terms of lives and many other victims, and the European Union must take care of it. Humanitarian aid is provided by the European Union, and the EU remains the largest donor of humanitarian aid on Earth, also in this case. But women are affected more and girls are affected more, and communities which are not supported by the regime are affected more. This is what we must not allow to happen anywhere on Earth. And this is why humanitarian aid also must come along with a clear political message for human dignity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora presidenta en ejercicio del Consejo, seÃ±ora comisaria, hoy venimos a denunciar una vez mÃ¡s una nueva situaciÃ³n de vulneraciÃ³n del Derecho internacional humanitario, una vuelta de tuerca mÃ¡s al insoportable apartheid de las mujeres en AfganistÃ¡n.

Y queremos denunciar, alto y claro, el desamparo deliberado al que el rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n ha condenado a las mujeres vÃ­ctimas de la catÃ¡strofe del terremoto, muchas sepultadas bajo los escombros sin ser rescatadas y sin ningÃºn derecho a recibir atenciÃ³n mÃ©dica o asistencia. Nos fuimos de AfganistÃ¡n y las hemos dejado abandonadas a su suerte, porque hemos enviado ayuda humanitaria ây yo le agradezco mucho su esfuerzo, seÃ±ora comisariaâ, pero debemos exigir âla comunidad internacional debe hacerloâ que las trabajadoras humanitarias puedan actuar y puedan repartir la ayuda.

Y, sobre todo, debemos hablar alto y claro. Debemos decir de una vez por todas que estamos hablando de un crimen de lesa humanidad contra el 50Â % de la poblaciÃ³n afgana, y que la comunidad internacional y la UniÃ³n Europea deben implicarse para que esto no vuelva a ocurrir.

Â Â Anna Maria Cisint (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Afghanistan il terremoto di agosto ha portato morti a madri, figli, nonne. Sono state lasciate morire sotto le macerie mentre imploravano aiuto.Â I soccorritori, tutti uomini, non le hanno toccate. Erano donne e quindi condannate a morire, drammaticamente e nel silenzio piÃ¹ assordante della sinistra, delle femministe, dell'Albanese, della Salis. Nessun titolo, nessuna trasmissione tv. Tutti zitti. PerchÃ© prendere posizione contro l'Islam radicale Ã¨ troppo pericoloso. La radicalizzazione uccide, ma anche il vigliacco e ideologico silenzio.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). â MrÂ President, allow me to speak directly to my sisters in Afghanistan: all my respect for your fight and resilience, for your strength and courage. We see you, we hear you, and we are going to fight with you.

Because we â women in Europe â know the fight for gender equality too well. But those who are trying to oppress us here just come in nicer suits. I know that gender equality is possible in Afghanistan, even if being a woman today in Afghanistan feels like being trapped in a nightmare.

There is no doubt there is a gender apartheid in Afghanistan. And MadamÂ Commissioner, we have to, as an EU, work towards having gender apartheid as a crime against humanity. We need also to focus on and prioritise women and girls in our humanitarian response, because we have to be the force that carries and the force that empowers girls and women in Afghanistan.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena Schilling (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Ein Erdbeben: tausende Menschen, die gestorben sind und verletzt wurdenÂ âÂ TÃ¶chter, MÃ¼tter, VÃ¤ter, BrÃ¼derÂ â, zerstÃ¶rte HÃ¤user. Eine Katastrophe in der Krise, in der Krise von einem Regime, das Frauen und MÃ¤dchen unterdrÃ¼ckt. Und so wie in jeder Krise sehen wir eine Sache: Es passiert immer wiederÂ â Frauen und MÃ¤dchen, die unterdrÃ¼ckt werden. Wenn so was passiert, sind sie die Hauptbetroffenen. Es gibt keine gescheite medizinische Versorgung, es gibt niemanden, der sich ordentlich um sie kÃ¼mmert. Und vor allem gibt es keine Hilfe.

Und hier und jetzt mÃ¼ssen wir aus dieser Debatte rausgehen und das tun, was notwendig ist, nÃ¤mlich endlich auch Verantwortung Ã¼bernehmen fÃ¼r diese MÃ¤dchen und jungen Frauen und Frauen, die hier unterdrÃ¼ckt werden. Das kann nicht das Ende sein, zu betrauern, dass Menschen gestorben sind, und das tue ich. Sondern das muss der Anfang davon sein, dass wir als EU ein ganz, ganz, ganz klares Zeichen setzen. Und vor allem, dass wir in keinem Fall uns mit Regimen wie dem Regime der Taliban treffen. Nicht in Ãsterreich und auch sonst nirgendwo. Das muss hier und jetzt klargemacht werden.

ÎÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿ ÏÏÏÎ¬Ï ÏÏ ÎµÎ¹ÏÎ·Î³Î®ÏÏÎ¹Î± Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ¿ ÎÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÏ ÎÎ±ÏÎºÏ ÎÏÎ¼Î¼Î± Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ· Î´Î¹Î±Î¼ÏÏÏÏÏÎ· ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ®Ï Î³Î¹Î± ÏÎ·Î½ Î±Î½Î¸ÏÏÏÎ¹ÏÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® Î²Î¿Î®Î¸ÎµÎ¹Î± ÏÎµ ÏÎµÏÎ¹Î¿ÏÎ­Ï ÎºÏÎ¯ÏÎµÏÎ½ âÏÏÏÏ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ Î· ÎÎ¬Î¶Î±, Î±Î»Î»Î¬ ÏÏÏÏ ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÎ¿ ÎÏÎ³Î±Î½Î¹ÏÏÎ¬Î½.

Â Â ChloÃ© Ridel (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, en Afghanistan, le calvaire des femmes sous le rÃ©gime des talibans se poursuit. Le 31Â aoÃ»t, un terrible tremblement de terre a ravagÃ© le pays et les femmes ont Ã©tÃ© volontairement oubliÃ©es sous les dÃ©combres. Les blessÃ©es ont Ã©tÃ© isolÃ©es, privÃ©es de soins dans les zones sinistrÃ©es. Les ONG n'avaient mÃªme pas accÃ¨s aux camps rÃ©servÃ©s aux femmes.

VoilÃ  oÃ¹ conduit l'obscurantisme religieux. Que va devenir la santÃ© des femmes dans ce pays oÃ¹ elles ne peuvent Ãªtre soignÃ©es que par des femmes, mais oÃ¹ on leur interdit l'accÃ¨s aux Ã©tudes et aux professions mÃ©dicales? C'est un crime prÃ©mÃ©ditÃ© d'une perversion absolue. VoilÃ  quatreÂ ans que les talibans font mÃ©ticuleusement disparaÃ®tre 28Â millions de femmes de l'espace public. Interdites de chanter, de parler ou d'Ãªtre, tout simplement. Nous l'avons dit et redit, il s'agit d'un apartheid de genre qui doit Ãªtre inscrit dans le droit international comme un crime contre l'humanitÃ©.

Mais la lutte pour les droits des femmes partout dans le monde n'est pas qu'une question de justice; c'est un enjeu de sÃ©curitÃ© internationale. Quand nous parlons de la dÃ©fense europÃ©enne, il ne faut pas oublier d'investir dans l'Ã©mancipation des femmes et des filles, car lÃ  oÃ¹ il y a plus d'Ã©galitÃ© entre les femmes et les hommes, il est prouvÃ© qu'il y a aussi plus de dÃ©veloppement et plus de sÃ©curitÃ©. Moins les femmes seront en sÃ©curitÃ©, moins nous le serons toutes et tous. Tenons-en donc compte et investissons dans l'Ã©mancipation des femmes et des filles.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Isabella Tovaglieri (PfE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in Afghanistan l'Islam radicale mostra tutta la sua spietata disumanitÃ . Donne, ragazze e bambine vengono lasciate morire, sepolte vive sotto le macerie, perchÃ© l'interpretazione talebana del Corano vieta ai soccorritori maschi di toccare i loro corpi. CosÃ¬, dopo aver strappato alle donne il diritto di studiare e di lavorare, gli estremisti islamici tolgono loro anche il diritto di essere salvate.

Oggi parliamo del loro dramma in quest'Aula. Ma con che credibilitÃ  l'Europa si preoccupa di queste donne dopo che essa stessa ha contribuito a diffondere l'Islam, anche quello fondamentalista, con assurde iniziative come la promozione del velo islamico o del Corano in Europa? Saman Abbas e tutte le altre ci ricordano come certi soprusi oggi siano la triste realtÃ  anche di casa nostra.

Per cui, se l'Europa vuole lanciare un messaggio di speranza alle donne di Kabul, condanni per prima l'Islam oscurantista in casa propria.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Andrews (Renew). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, colleagues, the reality is that we have seen the cost of aid cuts in real time in Afghanistan at the beginning of this month and the country that has done most to impoverish Afghanistan â the United States â is responsible for the massive reduction in aid support.

Back in 2022, EURÂ 3.5Â billion were distributed in aid in Afghanistan. This year, it is just EURÂ 750Â million, so it is an extraordinary reduction. The good thing is that the European Union, by contrast, has increased its contributions, has provided medical teams, has provided flights full of relief and aid. And we're not there because we want to extract resources; we're not there because we want to win procurement contracts. We're doing it because of fellow feeling, because of that sense of global solidarity for our fellow humans.

It is the best of us and something that we should absolutely try to save. I am really pleased that we are having this debate and that we are keeping the women and girls of Afghanistan at the top of our agenda here at the European Parliament.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rosa EstarÃ s Ferragut (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±ora comisaria, seÃ±orÃ­as, desde la llegada al poder de los talibanes, hace ahora cuatro aÃ±os, la cascada de prohibiciones y atentados contra los derechos humanos y de crÃ­menes contra la humanidad ha sido constante en AfganistÃ¡n. Las mujeres han sido borradas del espacio pÃºblico, no pueden estudiar mÃ¡s que hasta los doce aÃ±os, no pueden trabajar ni hacer deporte, no pueden salir si no es con un familiar, no pueden recibir atenciÃ³n mÃ©dica por parte de hombres. Se han prohibido incluso los libros escritos por mujeres. Y despuÃ©s del terremoto, con mÃ¡s de dos mil doscientos muertos y miles de heridos, no han podido ser atendidos y han sido vÃ­ctimas desproporcionadas las mujeres y las niÃ±as.

Sumamos a ello, ademÃ¡s, el apagÃ³n de internet hace unos dÃ­as y, por lo tanto, es absolutamente imprescindible que alcemos la voz. Â¿DÃ³nde estÃ¡n los activistas que en tantos conflictos han estado y en este no estÃ¡n? Llevan cuatro aÃ±os las mujeres sin derechos y solamente hacemos debates. Â¿DÃ³nde estÃ¡n los activistas para parar esta atrocidad? Alcemos la voz y seamos su voz para que no sean invisibles, paremos esta atrocidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â MrÂ President, we must express our deepest solidarity with all those affected by the devastating earthquakes in Afghanistan, and especially with the brave Afghan women and men working tirelessly in relief efforts.

But we have also seen how the cruel restrictions on Afghan women and girls have blocked life-saving aid to half of the population. Even before the earthquake, Afghanistan was already one of the worst places in the world to be a woman, a place where gender apartheid takes its most brutal form. It reminds us ofÂ The Handmaid's Tale, but this is no fiction. It is the daily reality for millions of Afghan women and women and girls living under a regime of fear and oppression. We must stand firm for the Afghan women and for women everywhere, especially during a time where we see attacks on women's rights in Afghanistan, but we also see different kind of attacks on women's rights here in our own Union.

So, for all women and girls out there, we need to ensure that we the European Union, in all our efforts, ensure to support targeting women and girls, especially during a time of crisis, as right now in Afghanistan.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rody Tolassy (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, ce qui se joue aujourd'hui en Afghanistan dÃ©passe le drame humanitaire. C'est un crime contre l'humanitÃ©. Des femmes, des mÃ¨res, des fillesÂ â plus de la moitiÃ© des victimesÂ â sont condamnÃ©es Ã  mourir deux fois: sous les dÃ©combres, puis sous le poids des lois iniques imposÃ©es par un rÃ©gime islamiste qui nie jusqu'Ã  leur droit d'exister.

Elles ne peuvent ni Ã©tudier, ni travailler, ni mÃªme demander de l'aide pour survivre sans eau, sans soins, sans dignitÃ©, tandis que leurs bourreaux, au nom d'une idÃ©ologie, interdisent Ã  un homme de leur tendre la main. Ce n'est pas la culture qu'il faut respecter, c'est la vie. Ce n'est pas la tradition qu'il faut protÃ©ger, c'est l'humanitÃ©.

Comme le disait SimoneÂ Veil, l'Europe doit soutenir ces femmes et leur rendre leur voix, leur savoir, leur libertÃ©. Car lÃ  oÃ¹ la femme est humiliÃ©e, c'est toute l'humanitÃ© qui s'effondre, et lÃ  oÃ¹ se relÃ¨ve la femme, c'est la civilisation qui renaÃ®t. Chez nous, en outre-mer, la femme est sacrÃ©e, elle est la vie. Elle est le pilier de la famille, du foyer et la voie du changement. Puissent les femmes afghanes se dÃ©faire du joug taliban et retrouver cette place centrale dans la sociÃ©tÃ©.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Raquel GarcÃ­a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew), blue-card question. â My question is as follows. I think that you gave a very, very good description of the horrible situation in which the women of Afghanistan are at this moment. So my question to you, given your very good perception of how grave the situation is, is if you agree with me â and with the European Court of Justice â that the women of Afghanistan have the right to protection as refugees in the European Union on the basis of their gender. Thank you.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rody Tolassy (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Vous connaissez trÃ¨s bien ma position. Je suis un patriote, et tout ce qui concerne la diplomatie relÃ¨ve des Ãtats-nations et non de l'Union europÃ©enne.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Å½eljana Zovko (PPE). â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Commissioner, in what kind of horrific world could we imagine the deprivation of medical care for women after natural disasters due to a lack of women doctors? In what sort of dreadful world could we envision that a woman's voice could not be heard singing in a public? As inconceivable as it may seem, this terrifying situation exists.

The Taliban authorities relentlessly invade every remaining inch of freedom for girls and women. The recent internet blackout in Afghanistan as part of an offensive against immoral practices is merely a symptom of the growing repression of the authorities to isolate the population of Afghan women even further.

The European Union must act urgently to intensify pressure on the Taliban through increased sanctions against those responsible for these human rights violations. The European Union must do everything it can to ensure that Afghan women can study, work or sing again wherever they wish and move freely without a male guardian. It's a matter of defending fundamental human rights that the European Union must uphold with vigour.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Andreas Schieder (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Frau Kommissarin! Vor vier Jahren haben die Taliban die Macht in Afghanistan Ã¼bernommen, das heiÃt vier Jahre systematische UnterdrÃ¼ckung afghanischer Frauen und MÃ¤dchen. Sie werden ins Private gedrÃ¤ngt, das Recht auf EigenstÃ¤ndigkeit und auf Bildung wird ihnen entzogen. Und sie sind auch Ã¼berproportional von der verheerenden humanitÃ¤ren Lage im Land betroffen. Und in den letzten Monaten ist diese Spirale aus Gewalt und UnterdrÃ¼ckung eskaliert. Bei dem Erdbeben Ende August wurde auÃerdem die Rettung von Frauen und MÃ¤dchen verunmÃ¶glicht und verzÃ¶gert, weil MÃ¤nner Frauen laut den Taliban angeblich nicht anfassen dÃ¼rfen. Und Ende September ist fÃ¼r 48 Stunden auch das Internet abgedreht worden, damit Frauen und MÃ¤dchen auch ihre letzte Verbindung zur AuÃenwelt noch einmal verlieren.

Die Taliban machen genau das Gegenteil dessen, was eine Regierung fÃ¼r ihr Land tun muss. Und dagegen mÃ¼ssen wir auftreten in der EuropÃ¤ischen Union. Deswegen habe ich auch kein VerstÃ¤ndnis, wenn einzelne europÃ¤ische Regierungen oder einzelne europÃ¤ische Minister glauben, mit den Taliban verhandeln zu kÃ¶nnen. Mit denen gibt es kein Verhandeln. Wir mÃ¼ssen auf der Seite der Menschenrechte stehen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â Mr President, dear colleagues, we must keep at the forefront of our minds the immense suffering of the people in Afghanistan. The devastating earthquake has compounded an already dire humanitarian situation and, of course, the Taliban's restrictions have severely hampered aid delivery, with bans on women working, internet blackouts, as was mentioned before, and obstructive administrative burdens. At the same time, we must not lose sight of the broader context. The regime that now controls Afghanistan continues to violate human rights and deny women their fundamental freedoms. In the face of this, our response must remain value based. In the dark hours, Europe can show, on the one hand, that it stands with the people of Afghanistan, but, on the other hand, in the same way, we have to criticise this horrific regime in Afghanistan.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marit Maij (S&D). â Voorzitter, commissaris, hoe kun je om hulp vragen als je stem niet gehoord mag worden? Hoe kan je geholpen worden als je geen medische hulp van een man mag ontvangen, maar vrouwen niet mogen werken? Voor veel vrouwen in Afghanistan zijn er geen opties meer. De aardbeving, die honderden doden en duizenden gewonden heeft veroorzaakt, legt bloot dat de bescherming van vrouwen al heel lang was afgebrokkeld.

Dus ja, we moeten opkomen voor de getroffen gebieden, maar daarmee lossen we de problemen van vrouwenrechten niet op. Die liggen onder het puin. De EU moet zich harder uitspreken tegen het verbod voor vrouwen om door te leren, het verbod voor vrouwen om naar een park te gaan, het verbod voor vrouwen om te werken, om ook te werken als humanitair werker. Dat moet verdwijnen.

Het is goed dat de Europese Unie fondsen heeft voor humanitaire hulp, maar we moeten wel zeker stellen dat die hulp evenveel bij vrouwen als bij mannen terechtkomt.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). â SeÃ±or presidente, cuando AfganistÃ¡n sufriÃ³ hace unas semanas un terrible terremoto, supimos que el rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n, un rÃ©gimen islamista, priorizÃ³ en los rescates a los hombres sobre las mujeres, dejÃ¡ndolas literalmente vivas debajo de los escombros. Terrible.

Este horror que nos escandaliza me provoca tambiÃ©n una triste reflexiÃ³n porque, mientras en Europa centramos nuestros esfuerzos de debates y las movilizaciones sobre la situaciÃ³n de las mujeres âcontinente donde, por cierto, gozamos de todos los derechos y libertadesâ, la verdadera persecuciÃ³n de la mujer se estÃ¡ cometiendo fuera de nuestras fronteras sin que tenga casi ninguna presencia mediÃ¡tica. Â¿No deberÃ­a ser esta la principal causa del feminismo europeo? Porque estas mujeres, como las afganas, sÃ­ que nacen vÃ­ctimas y, por lo tanto, deberÃ­an ser objeto principal de la lucha feminista en Europa.

Desde aquÃ­ exijo sanciones contra el rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n y la activaciÃ³n de todos los mecanismos de presiÃ³n a nuestro alcance.

(La oradora acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la Â«tarjeta azulÂ»)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew), blue-card question. â You said something very interesting: that the Afghan women were born oppressed, on the one hand, and on the other hand that we women in Europe are already gender-equal.

What is your answer to the women in, for example, Malta or Hungary, who are not allowed to have a safe and legal abortion? Many of them have damage for life, and some of them have actually lost their lives.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE), respuesta de Â«tarjeta azulÂ». â Esto es precisamente lo que estaba diciendo. Creo, sinceramente, que la principal causa del feminismo europeo es alzar la voz por las mujeres, en este caso, como las de AfganistÃ¡n u otras muchas, que es que ni siquiera tienen reconocidos los derechos como personas. Y Â¿de verdad nuestra preocupaciÃ³n ahora es hablar del aborto? Â¡Es que es muy triste! Es que yo lo que estoy diciendo es que lo que estÃ¡ ocurriendo en AfganistÃ¡n es dramÃ¡tico.

Creo que debe ser una de las principales causas del feminismo europeo, y espero que usted estÃ© de acuerdo conmigo en que defender los derechos de las mujeres en AfganistÃ¡n lo debe ser. Intentar politizar eso me parece que no ayuda a las mujeres en AfganistÃ¡n, y me parece que tampoco ayuda a la causa feminista en Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â SeÃ¡n Kelly (PPE). â A UachtarÃ¡in, Afghanistan has suffered two earthquakes.

One, the geological earthquake, which was devastating, but more devastating was the political earthquake when the Taliban took over, because their attitude to women is animalistic. It's sadistic; it's illogical. To think that any man who was born because of a woman, who has sisters, wives, daughters, etc., can treat women like that is just absolutely abominable.

And I think we have to highlight this far more internationally and look at international partners who can put an end to this gender apartheid. In fact, it's a crime against humanity, because it's a crime against women â because from the moment they are born to the moment they die, they are given absolutely no treatment other than disrespect all the way. They have no rights; they have nothing.

We've got to stand up, and international partners have to come on board to set a date by which this nonsense has to end.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Juan Fernando LÃ³pez Aguilar (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, la UniÃ³n Europea es el primer actor global en ayuda humanitaria y cooperaciÃ³n para el desarrollo. Pero, del mismo modo que no hemos sido capaces de traducir toda la ayuda al pueblo palestino en unidad para imponer sanciones al Gobierno genocida de Netanyahu y suspender el Acuerdo de AsociaciÃ³n UEâIsrael, tampoco hemos sido capaces de aliviar el sufrimiento de las vÃ­ctimas del terremoto del 31 de agosto en AfganistÃ¡n y, particularmente, de las mujeres sometidas a la brutalidad discriminatoria del rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n.

La UniÃ³n Europea es el primer contribuyente de la Corte Penal Internacional y, sin embargo, del mismo modo que no hemos sido capaces de imponer unidad a los Estados miembros en el respeto a la orden de detenciÃ³n internacional contra Netanyahu, tampoco hemos sido capaces, hasta ahora, de llevar la causa de delito de lesa humanidad âque es competencia de la Corte Penal Internacional, junto con el genocidio y los crÃ­menes de guerraâ contra los responsables del rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n.

Y, por tanto, este es el mensaje de este punto en el orden del dÃ­a: que la UniÃ³n Europea lleve como responsables de delitos de lesa humanidad a los miembros del rÃ©gimen talibÃ¡n que han impuesto ese brutal apartheid y acabar con el silencio, el apartheid de gÃ©nero y tambiÃ©n con la impunidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

IN THE CHAIR: JAVI LÃPEZ
Vice-President

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Margarita de la Pisa CarriÃ³n (PfE). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, en AfganistÃ¡n, como hemos visto en los recientes terremotos, las mujeres no pueden ser atendidas por un hombre, aunque sea para salvarles la vida. Â¿Por quÃ©? Porque allÃ­ donde rige la ley de la sharÃ­a se prohÃ­be al hombre tocar a una mujer que no sea su esposa o familiar cercana. Hasta el punto de dejarlas morir. Es un ejemplo claro del trato que reciben las mujeres, pero no solo en AfganistÃ¡n. Saben muy bien quÃ© subyace tras esta cruel mentalidad. Â¿CÃ³mo se trata a la mujer en PakistÃ¡n, en Arabia SaudÃ­, en tantos paÃ­ses del Ãfrica subsahariana? En IrÃ¡n, al menos trescientas dos mujeres han sido ejecutadas desde 2007.

Las mujeres afganas o iranÃ­es necesitan que se reconozca en esta casa lo que, sorprendentemente, el feminismo niega, lo que es evidente. Necesitan que actuemos sin complejos ante la ideologÃ­a que denigra la dignidad de la mujer allÃ­ donde se aplica, que es solo el islamismo radical. Una ideologÃ­a que estÃ¡ invadiendo sin resistencia Europa y que nos pone en el mismo riesgo a las mujeres aquÃ­.Â Porque un hombre que no respeta a la mujer en AfganistÃ¡n tampoco lo hace en Bruselas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Mr President, the Taliban's Afghanistan is a living hell for women. Girls are banned from schools. Women are locked out of work. Faces of women must be covered with an Islamic face veil. These are not cultural traditions, they are calculated acts of oppression rooted in extremist ideology.

Even after a deadly earthquake, Taliban monsters stick to their insane principles. The UN is trying to help, but Afghan women workers are banned from entering the UN compounds. Helping becomes difficult, while at the same time Afghan women are restricted from speaking to men.

This is Islamist insanity. We cannot remain silent. We must refuse to legitimise the Taliban in any form. We are defending women and girls by opposing Islamist tyranny.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomasz Froelich (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Die Lage der Frauen in Afghanistan ist schlimm, keine Frage. Und die Frauenrechte dort zu stÃ¤rken, ist sicherlich ein nobler Gedanke. Allerdings ist bei solchen Vorhaben immer Ã¤uÃerste Vorsicht geboten, um nicht als neokolonialistisch wahrgenommen zu werden. Denn laut einer Analyse der renommierten ConfÃ©rence Sciences Po fÃ¼hrt dieser Interventionismus insbesondere in Afghanistan nur zu einer weiteren Radikalisierung. Man will etwas verbessern, aber verschlimmert es nurÂ â gesinnungsethisch nachvollziehbar, verantwortungsethisch oftmals verheerend.

Die Frage lautet also: Was wollen wir erreichen und wie wollen wir es erreichen? Der Ansatz der letzten Jahrzehnte ist gescheitert. Zwischen 2001 und 2020 Ã¼berwies die EU rund 4 MilliardenÂ Euro Entwicklungshilfe an Afghanistan. Ein betrÃ¤chtlicher Teil hiervon war fÃ¼r die StÃ¤rkung der Frauenrechte vorgesehen. Aber hat sich seither die Lage der Frauen in Afghanistan verbessert? Wohl kaum. Wir mÃ¼ssen also unsere Entwicklungshilfe und unsere Afghanistanpolitik komplett Ã¼berdenken. Und das impliziert eben auch, dass wir mit den herrschenden KrÃ¤ften vor Ort in den Dialog treten, ob wir sie mÃ¶gen oder nicht.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Mr President, dear people of Europe, women and girls in Afghanistan, when we have a debate about the situation of women and girls in Afghanistan, what usually happens is the following: everyone in the room feels sad, compassionate, maybe even a little pity. A few minutes later, the feeling is gone, forgotten. We continue with the next debate.

The reason why that is the case is that we do not take it as seriously as we should. But let me be the last one to remind you of one thing: the suffering of the female part of the Afghan population is not something that should just make us feel sad for one debate. It is something that should make us so angry that we cannot resist the urge to provide actual, practical support to the female population of Afghanistan, especially now after the recent earthquakes.

We need to help the most vulnerable part of the population â women and girls. We stand, as the European Union, for freedom and democracy. Those values do not stop at European borders. International efforts to improve the quality of life are the core of humanitarian aid. And that is exactly why I, the Party of Progress â and I am happy that also this Chamber â call for action.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â Mr President, dear colleagues, first of all, thank you for your engaging insights on this important topic. This earthquake in Afghanistan has struck communities already struggling with one of the most severe humanitarian crises in the world. And today, half of Afghanistan's population, 23Â million people depend on humanitarian aid to survive.

Many of you have raised deep concerns about the particularly alarming situation of Afghan women â and you are right. Women and girls face the harshest reality: denied access to education, to work and to public life. Afghanistan remains the only country in the world where girls are banned from school after the age of 12. More than 1.4Â million girls are being robbed of their future. And yet, humanitarian aid is drying up. Since 2022, global funding has fallen from USDÂ 3.8Â billion to just USDÂ 789Â million this year. This limits the scale, capacities and the speed of humanitarian actors. Only 28Â % of this year's humanitarian response plan is currently funded, compared to 53Â % last year.

In this context of growing disengagement, the European Union stands firm; we will not abandon the Afghan people. Through our presence in Kabul and through predictable, principled support funding we remain one of the last and most reliable partners on the ground. In 2025 alone, we have allocated more than EURÂ 180Â million in lifesaving humanitarian assistance for Afghans in the country and across the wider region.

But humanitarian aid alone will not solve this crisis â it is just a Band-Aid. We need long-term sustainable solutions, not just to help people survive, but to help them stand on their own two feet. The best support we can offer is opportunity and empowerment, creating jobs and building social and climate resilience because strong societies lead to greater stability.

Stability is the foundation of security and prosperity. That is why our support goes far beyond humanitarian aid. The Commission is preparing an additional over EURÂ 100Â million in development assistance to help Afghans meet basic needs, rebuild their lives and reintegrate returnees. This will focus on livelihoods, resilience and economic recovery. We are bringing together the EU's humanitarian, development and political tools a nexus approach in action. We are paying special attention to women, girls and the most vulnerable because no solution is sustainable unless it includes everyone.

Political and humanitarian diplomacy must go hand in hand. We need diplomatic engagement with the Taliban, the de facto authorities, especially when they are systematically erasing the rights of half of their population, and disengagement is vital to delivering aid and securing access to those who need it most. We continue to call on the Taliban to reverse any ban that strips women and girls of their basic rights.

The EU will continue to stand by Afghanistan's people, especially its women and girls, with solidarity and with compassion. Their rights, their dignity and their humanity must never be forgotten. Thank you.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, Commissioner, we will keep monitoring the humanitarian situation in Afghanistan and also count on your support.

The Council remains committed to gender equality and gender-responsive action. We need to counter all forms of discrimination and violence against women and children and ensure accountability. We will maintain EU engagement with the de facto authorities, but without giving them legitimacy.

Through its presence in the country, the EU will continue to support and engage with the Afghan people.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â I have received eight motions for resolutions to wind up this debate.

The debate is closed and the vote will be held on Thursday.

Â 

14. New Strategic EU-India Agenda (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is Council and Commission statements on the new strategic EU-India agenda (2025/2873(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, we are in a transformative moment for EU-India relations. The visit of the College of Commissioners to India in FebruaryÂ 2025 was not ceremonial diplomacy; it led to the foundation of a renewed strategic partnership shaped for the 21st century.

I'm honoured to present our new strategic EU-India agenda, an agenda that turns proximity of values into proximity of action. Why now? Because our interests and our responsibilities converge. India, the world's largest democracy and a powerhouse of growth, matters profoundly to Europe's security, prosperity and resilience. And Europe, India's trusted partner in rules, standards and investment, matters profoundly to India's inspirations. This is not just about cooperation between two regions; this is about two democratic powers joining forces to shape a more secure, prosperous and sustainable world. Together, we represent nearly a quarter of global GDP and population. Together, we can move the global needle.

Europe and India are also converging by necessity. Recent wars, great power rivalry and fragile supply chains are forcing both to adapt. Europe is de-risking after Russia's aggression. India seeks reliable partners without formal alignment. What unites us? Secure sea lanes, resilient infrastructure, trusted technology and climate-compatible growth. Two big democracies partnering where it matters: market access, clean energy, safe technology and connectivity that closes strategic distances. Because the world offers no softer alternative choice.

Our agenda rests on five pillars that I will detail. First, prosperity and sustainability: the EU is India's largest trading partner; India is our largest partner in the Global South. We aim to conclude a high-ambition free trade agreement by the end of this year, unlocking new flows of trade investment. Through Global Gateway, we are mobilising billions for clean energy, digital networks and sustainable transport. Prosperity without sustainability is short-lived. Our clean energy and climate partnership on green hydrogen, offshore wind and energy efficiency will accelerate the transition and create good jobs on both continents.

Second, technology and innovation: the future will belong to those who shape standards and skill breakthroughs. Our EU-India innovation hubs will connect labs, start-ups and industry on AI, semi-conductors and quantum. Through Horizon Europe, we open more doors for joint research that solves global problems from health to climate to secure connectivity.

Third, security and defence in a world of hybrid threats, coercion and contested seas, we will deepen consultations, expand maritime domain awareness, and conduct more joint exercises. In the Indo-Pacific, we stand for a free, open and rules-based maritime order that benefits all.

Fourth, connectivity and global issues: the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) will future-proof supply chains and unite all regions more closely. All trilateral projects from Africa to South Asia will deliver reliable infrastructure, solar power and disaster-resilient systems, partnering where we add the most value.

Fifth, the human enablers: our partnership is powered by people. The first pilot European legal gateway office in India will make skilled mobility simpler and safer. Our education and skills dialogue will deepen links between universities and training systems. The vibrant Indian diaspora in Europe already shows what we can achieve together. All of this sits within a wider commitment to defend the UN Charter, support Ukraine's sovereignty, counter terrorism and organised crime, reform multilateral institutions and deliver on the Paris Agreement. This is a partnership of democratic resilience, not against any one country, but for a world governed by rules, dignity and opportunity.

Today, we choose to turn a shared agenda into shared outcomes. Let us get to work.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â David McAllister, on behalf of the PPE Group. â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Commissioner, it is indeed now three weeks ago that the European Commission presented the joint communication on a new strategic EU-India agenda. It entails more than 60 concrete deliverables. This is welcome. I can underline what the Commissioner said: India is indispensable to Europe's global reach and resilience.

However, dear colleagues, let us be cautious. The European Union sometimes tends to overpromise. It is not the scale of our commitments, but our capacity to see them through that will, in the end, define our credibility.

India is, in some ways, a strategic litmus test for the European Union. It doesn't fit neatly into our categories of like-minded partner. The world's most populous country is not and has never been willing to be bound by traditional alliances. Instead, India pursues cooperation Ã  la carte, guided by its own national interests. This poses a challenge, but also an opportunity for the European Union.

If we act pragmatically, we can build real progress in three key areas. First: trade and technology, where a free trade agreement could open vast opportunities. Second: connectivity and clean energy, where India's growth and our global gateway align. And third: security and defence, where our joint maritime operation in the Indian Ocean already points the way forward.

So, dear Commission, less rhetoric, more delivery. Only then can the EU-India partnership live up to its thriving potential.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Thierry Mariani, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, dans la nouvelle architecture du monde, l'Inde est devenue, sous la direction de Narendra ModiÂ â Ã©lu enÂ 2014, rÃ©Ã©lu enÂ 2019 et en 2024Â â, un pÃ´le de stabilitÃ© et une puissance d'Ã©quilibre.

Face aux gÃ©ants chinois et amÃ©ricains, l'Inde offre Ã  l'Europe une voie vers l'indÃ©pendance et la coopÃ©ration. L'Union europÃ©enne a cependant trop longtemps nÃ©gligÃ© le partenariat indien. Seulement 2,4Â % de notre commerce extÃ©rieur est rÃ©alisÃ© avec ce pays, loin derriÃ¨re les Ãtats-Unis et la Chine. C'est une faute stratÃ©gique, car en ignorant les puissances Ã©mergentes, en tournant le dos aux BRICS, l'Europe se condamne Ã  l'isolement diplomatique et Ã©conomique et, pire encore, Ã  une tutelle amÃ©ricaine.

Aujourd'hui, la Commission propose une feuille de route pour accÃ©lÃ©rer la conclusion d'un accord, ce qui va dans le bon sens. Mais l'Europe ne souhaite pas un partenariat de faÃ§ade. Elle a besoin non seulement d'un accord concret et Ã©quilibrÃ© qui renforce nos liens Ã©conomiques, technologiques et Ã©nergÃ©tiques, mais aussi d'une coopÃ©ration ferme pour lutter contre le terrorisme et l'islamisme, afin de nous ouvrir les portes de la quatriÃ¨me Ã©conomie mondiale.

Cet accord devra aussi protÃ©ger nos travailleurs et nos industriels. Il n'est pas question d'ouvrir nos marchÃ©s sans condition face Ã  des disparitÃ©s sociales considÃ©rables ou de transfÃ©rer nos technologies sans compensation Ã©quitable. Notre continent peut se maintenir dans le concert des nations, Ã  condition de tendre une main amicale au Premier ministre Modi et Ã  son pays, sans arrogance, sans paternalisme et en les traitant d'Ã©gal Ã  Ã©gal. C'est la seule stratÃ©gie valable pour une Europe qui cherche Ã  renouer avec sa puissance.

Indie w 2022 roku staÅy siÄ jednym z gÅÃ³wnych odbiorcÃ³w rosyjskiej ropy, umoÅ¼liwiajÄc tym samym finansowanie agresji Rosji na UkrainÄ. Dopiero decyzja Donalda Trumpa o naÅoÅ¼eniu ceÅ na towary z Indii zmieniÅa kierunki zakupu ropy.

NaleÅ¼y wiÄc zapytaÄ: w jaki sposÃ³b Unia zamierza realizowaÄ swoje âszczytne cele strategiczneâ wobec Indii? IdÅºmy dalej. Jak bÄdzie Unia realizowaÄ wspÃ³lnÄ pomoc z Indiami innym krajom, krajom trzecim, skoro Indie same zmagajÄ siÄ z problemami spoÅecznymi: bieda, gÅÄbokie nierÃ³wnoÅci, utrwalony system kastowy?

A co z prawami kobiet? Jak Unia chce wspÃ³lnie z Indiami budowaÄ politykÄ rÃ³wnoÅci, skoro w tym kraju kobiety wciÄÅ¼ sÄ systemowo dyskryminowane, marginalizowane, a przemoc wobec nich ma charakter powszechny i czÄsto pozostaje bezkarna?

Podobnie w sferze bezpieczeÅstwa. Jak moÅ¼na mÃ³wiÄ o wspÃ³lnej strategii bezpieczeÅstwa z paÅstwem, ktÃ³re utrzymuje bliskie relacje z RosjÄ?

Â Â Svenja Hahn, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! China baut seinen autoritÃ¤ren Einfluss aus. Die USA sichern ihre Interessen mit Ellenbogen. Und Europa fragt sich: Welchen Platz finden wir eigentlich in dieser Welt-Unordnung? Wir brauchen jetzt mehr Handel mit mehr Partnern. Die Zusammenarbeit mit Indien ist ein wichtiger Schritt fÃ¼r die Zukunft Europas. Wir mÃ¼ssen uns doch klar machen: Geopolitische StÃ¤rke beginnt mit WettbewerbsfÃ¤higkeit. Ohne Wachstum keine SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤tÂ â ganz einfach.

Jedes Handelsabkommen ist ein Schritt in Richtung europÃ¤ische StÃ¤rke. Wenn wir relevant bleiben wollen als Europa, mÃ¼ssen wir handeln und nicht nur endlos verhandeln. Die EU ist bereits Indiens grÃ¶Ãter Handelspartner. Lasst uns das mit dem Freihandelsabkommen ausbauen. FÃ¼r Zusammenarbeit statt Protektionismus, fÃ¼r Partnerschaft statt Abschottung. Unsere Unternehmen verdienen es, dass wir ihnen endlich neue MÃ¤rkte Ã¶ffnen. Wenn Europa wachsen will, muss es wieder an sich selber glauben, an seine Ideen, an seine Unternehmen und an seine Menschen. Deshalb sage ich auch ganz klar: Wer mehr Handel blockiert, blockiert Europas Zukunft. Wer Angst vor Wettbewerb hat, wird am Ende Wohlstand in Europa und Einfluss in der Welt verlieren. Deswegen, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, lasst uns nicht lÃ¤nger reden. Lasst uns wortwÃ¶rtlich handelnÂ â mit Indien, fÃ¼r Europa, fÃ¼r unsere Zukunft.

Â Â Vladimir PrebiliÄ, v imenu skupine Verts/ALE. â SpoÅ¡tovane kolegice, cenjeni kolegi! Nova strateÅ¡ka agenda med Evropsko unijo in Indijo je vizija partnerstva, ki bo doloÄila vlogo Evrope v indo-pacifiÅ¡ki regiji. Indija je najveÄja demokracija in najhitrejÅ¡e rastoÄe veliko gospodarstvo na svetu. Evropa si ne more privoÅ¡Äiti, da bo zgolj opazovala s strani. Potrebujemo Indijo na podroÄju trgovine, varnosti in energije.

Vendar je Äas kljuÄnega pomena. Kot stalni poroÄevalec za Indijo odloÄno pozivam k zakljuÄku pogajanj do konca leta 2025. Skupaj lahko poveÄamo proizvodnjo energije iz obnovljivih virov, vodimo globalno tekmo za zeleni vodik in zagotovimo cenovno dostopno Äisto energijo za evropsko industrijo in gospodinjstva.

V svetu negotovosti in novih geopolitiÄnih tekmovanj je Indija partnerica, s katero mora Evropa oblikovati stabilnejÅ¡o prihodnost. Zdaj je Äas za drzne odloÄitve. Potrebujemo nove prijatelje in Indija mora biti na vrhu tega seznama.
