ÐÐ¼ÐµÑÑÐ¾ Ð´Ð° Ð¿Ð¾ÑÑÐ°Ð²Ð°Ð¼Ðµ Ð´ÑÑÐ³Ð¸ÑÐµ, ÑÑÑÐ±Ð²Ð° Ð´Ð° Ð·Ð°Ð¿Ð¾ÑÐ½ÐµÐ¼ Ð´Ð° ÑÐ²Ð°Ð¶Ð°Ð²Ð°Ð¼Ðµ ÑÐ¾Ð±ÑÑÐ²ÐµÐ½Ð¸ÑÐµ ÑÐ¸ ÐºÐ¾ÑÐµÐ½Ð¸ Ð¸ ÑÑÐ¸ÑÑÐ¸ÑÐ½ÑÐºÐ¸ÑÐµ ÑÐ¸ ÑÐµÐ½Ð½Ð¾ÑÑÐ¸.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â MichaÅ Szczerba (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear Commissioner, colleagues, for 20 years we have called the EU-India relationship a 'strategic partnership', yet too often it has been words without delivery.

India is on track to become the world's third-largest economy by 2030, but security must be at the centre. India still relies on Russian-made weapons for more than half of its arsenal. Europe should help reduce that dependence through technology transfer and defence cooperation, but only under strict conditions.

In an amendment together with David McAllister on common foreign and security policy, we stress stronger security ties, joint naval exercises, links between the Indian Navy and EU missions, and a Security of Information agreement by 2026.Â But the key: clear rules, measurable targets, annual progress reports and suspension triggers if India moves closer to Russian military structures.

I must raise a serious concern. India joined the Russian-Belarusian Zapad 2025 exercise â a 65Â % Indian contingent trained alongside 100Â 000 troops, including simulated nuclear and cross-border operations. This casts doubt on India's shift away from Moscow.

The EU cannot deepen security ties with a partner that still joins Russian-led war games, while Russia frightensÂ our neighbourhood.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nacho SÃ¡nchez Amor (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, este marco es un perfecto ejemplo de la nueva polÃ­tica exterior de la UniÃ³n Europea. Buscamos socios por el mundo con un cartel en la frente que pone Â«Estamos desesperados. Tenemos ansiedad. Necesitamos amigosÂ». Y nuestros interlocutores lo saben. Lo saben y lo utilizan. Y eso no es un buen punto de partida para ninguna negociaciÃ³n.

Otra de nuestras limitaciones es que negociamos con la amenaza implÃ­cita de que nuestro interlocutor se puede buscar otros padrinos internacionales y se puede pasar al lado oscuro: otra debilidad que muchos explotan. Y, por tanto, no es extraÃ±o que en los nuevos formatos de acuerdos desaparezca nuestra arma diferencial, los valores y principios, y se imponga una alergia a meter nada que pueda molestar mÃ­nimamente a la otra parte.

En el caso de la India no hablo ya de la violencia contra minorÃ­as religiosas. No hablo de prisiones preventivas durante aÃ±os. Ni siquiera los convenios de la OIT, ni siquiera una menciÃ³n al desarrollo sostenible. Pura realpolitik y nada de proximity values.

La tentaciÃ³n de la Gran Suiza estÃ¡ llamando. Una UniÃ³n Europea introvertida, amurallada, rica, envejecida, aunque libre. Â¿Ese es el modelo?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marion MarÃ©chal (ECR). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, dans un monde qui oppose de plus en plus la Chine aux Ãtats-Unis, les pays europÃ©ens ont tout intÃ©rÃªt Ã  nouer une coopÃ©ration approfondie avec l'Inde, puissance Ã©mergente et dynamique.

Cette coopÃ©ration doit d'abord se faire sur le plan sÃ©curitaire. L'Inde, comme nous, fait face Ã  une menace islamiste intense. Dans cette lutte, nous devrions davantage nous inspirer de NarendraÂ Modi, qui n'a pas les pudeurs occidentales.

Elle doit ensuite se faire en matiÃ¨re de dÃ©fense. DÃ©tentrice de l'arme nuclÃ©aire, l'Inde accorde des moyens colossaux pour garantir son indÃ©pendance stratÃ©gique. Cette recherche d'autonomie s'est concrÃ©tisÃ©e par l'achat rÃ©cent d'avions Rafale et de sous-marins de classe ScorpÃ¨ne, une dÃ©marche que nous devons continuer Ã  accompagner.

La coopÃ©ration doit aussi Ãªtre Ã©nergÃ©tique. Puissance industrielle Ã©mergente, l'Inde investit Ã©normÃ©ment dans le dÃ©veloppement de filiÃ¨res Ã©nergÃ©tiques dÃ©carbonÃ©es, mais sa filiÃ¨re nuclÃ©aire reste encore balbutiante. Nouer des partenariats privilÃ©giÃ©s avec New Delhi pour piloter la crÃ©ation de centrales nuclÃ©aires doit donc Ãªtre une prioritÃ©.

Enfin, la coopÃ©ration doit Ãªtre commerciale. Alors que l'Inde, sous l'impulsion d'un gouvernement patriote, protÃ¨ge son marchÃ©, l'Europe a au contraire ouvert le sien sans contrepartie sÃ©rieuse. Pour rÃ©sorber notre dÃ©ficit commercial, nous devons donc rompre avec cette approche naÃ¯ve, qui est la marque de fabrique de la Commission europÃ©enne depuis trop longtemps.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Cowen (Renew). â Mr President, after two decades of onâandâoff negotiations, the EUâIndia trade talks are finally nearing conclusion. The 14th round of negotiations begins this week, and personally I am honoured myself to have accepted an invitation to join the European Parliament's International Trade Committee delegation to India later this month, as we enter a decisive phase.

This deal carries vast economic promise. India is now the world's fastestâgrowing large economy and home to 1.5Â billion people â a market of enormous potential for European and indeed Irish exporters. My own country's whiskey exports to India, for example, have grown tenfold in five years, while our dairy, pharma, MedTech and nutrition sectors are ready to do similar once barriers are lifted.

But this partnership is about more than trade alone. It's about Europe's place in a rapidly changing world. As the United States turns inwards and Russia and China grow more assertive, deepening ties with India and others is essential to strengthen global stability and ensure Europe remains an active shaper rather than a passive observer of the century ahead. Yet, like many other trade agreements, it must be fair. We must defend high European standards, including protecting our farmers and ensuring sustainability commitments are real. An ambitious, forwardâlooking deal can work for both sides, strengthening Europe's resilience while opening new doors for the likes of Irish business.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Alexander Sell (ESN). â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Indische Raffinerien laufen auf Hochtouren. Erst im September ein neuer Rekord: 10Â Millionen Barrel Diesel hat Indien nach Europa exportiert â in nur einem Monat. Die Inder verdienen sehr viel Geld mit dem Verkauf von russischem Ãl. Das ist das Ergebnis der RusslandâSanktionen von Ursula von der Leyen. In Indien brummt das GeschÃ¤ft, deutsche Raffinerien wie PCK in Schwedt an der Oder stehen still. Die Leidtragenden der BrÃ¼sseler Sanktionspolitik sind wir EuropÃ¤er. Nach wie vor tanken wir russisches Ãl, nur mÃ¼ssen wir fÃ¼r den Umweg Ã¼ber Indien viel zu hohe Preise zahlen.

MinisterprÃ¤sident Modi verfolgt eine pragmatische AuÃenpolitik. Indien pflegt gute Beziehungen mit Washington, Paris oder Berlin, aber auch mit Peking oder Moskau. Die indische Regierung stellt die Interessen der Inder an erste Stelle. Das ist echte SouverÃ¤nitÃ¤t, und wir sollten uns diese Klugheit zum Vorbild nehmen. Verkneifen sollten wir uns dagegen moralische Belehrungen Ã¼ber den Klimawandel oder LGBTQ. Indien ist kein Zirkus fÃ¼r die lÃ¤cherliche Ideologie der BrÃ¼sseler BÃ¼rokratie, sondern ein strategischer Partner und ein wichtiger Absatzmarkt fÃ¼r unsere Industrie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Niels Flemming Hansen (PPE). â Mr President, dear Commissioner, more than one hundred years ago, Albert Einstein reminded us that we owe a lot to the Indians. They taught us how to count, without which no worldwide scientific discovery could ever have been made. We have admired and learned from each other for centuries. Now we must turn shared knowledge into shared prosperity.

It is high time to conclude a modern freeâtrade and investment agreement, opening markets, protecting investors and setting standards the world will follow. We must secure supply chains, from critical raw materials to resilient manufacturing, so both economies can thrive.

On security, let us engage with clear eyes: India will pursue its own interests, but deeper ties with Russia will not serve its longâterm stability. Instead, let us expand maritime, cyber and defence industrial cooperation between democracies that believe in a ruleâbased order. Together, we can lead in technology, AI, semiconductors and mobile networks, and connect our startups from Bangalore to Berlin. Now we can drive the green transition, renewables, green hydrogen and sustainable finance through the Global Gateway.

Europe and India have traded ideas for centuries. Today, our trade is not only in goods, it is in hopes â hope for a more connected and more sustainable and more peaceful world. Let us build the future together.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Brando Benifei (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, tra qualche settimana parteciperÃ² alla missione della commissione per il commercio internazionale dell'Europarlamento a Nuova Delhi, in un momento cruciale per il futuro del partenariato strategico.

In un contesto globale segnato dal ritorno del protezionismo e da catene del valore sempre piÃ¹ vulnerabili, la cooperazione Ã¨ una scelta strategica per rafforzare la sicurezza economica e industriale per entrambe le parti. Ã essenziale avanzare nei negoziati per un accordo di libero scambio che sia equilibrato, ambizioso e reciprocamente vantaggioso, ma anche fondato su impegni vincolanti in materia di diritti umani, lavoro e ambiente.

Servono regole eque e una vera collaborazione industriale per contrastare dumping, sussidi distorsivi e barriere non tariffarie che penalizzano settori chiave, come l'acciaio, l'alluminio e i metalli di base.Â Li cito perchÃ© le nuove misure di salvaguardia per l'acciaio presentate dalla Commissione oggi sono sicuramente un importante passo avanti per difendere l'industria europea, una condizione essenziale per garantire paritÃ  di condizioni nel commercio globale e quindi molto importanti anche in questo dialogo con l'India.

Regole chiare, obiettivi comuni e una prospettiva condivisa: solo cosÃ¬ si potrÃ  costruire un partenariato forte, un vero patto per una globalizzazione piÃ¹ giusta e sostenibile, di cui c'Ã¨ assoluto bisogno.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sandro Gozi (Renew). â Mr President, Commissioner, Europe and India share vital interests and face similar challenges. We both push to rethink and relaunch multilateralism, starting with the reform of the UN. We have both a strong interest in contributing to global stability. We should also push India to rethink its relationship with Russia and to be very careful with its recent openings toward China. We are both convinced that strategic autonomy is a key element of our sovereignty, and we both need to renew and differentiate our trade relationship.

This is why we must intensify our effort to reach a trade agreement before the end of the year, and we must strengthen our cooperation in the Indian Ocean, also fully exploiting the potential of our European outermost regions like RÃ©union.

Last, but certainly not least, we must deepen our dialogue on artificial intelligence and digital issues. I am convinced that after the success of the AI summit in Paris, the new AI summit in Delhi can be a turning point for our bilateral relationship and for our common global digital action.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Mandl (PPE). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, colleagues, while here in the European Parliament we have to discuss and vote on confidence votes against our Commission's President from the extreme right and from the extreme left, which is already kind of a shame, the Commission's President has just visited India and reached out for prosperous future cooperation. There are not many things sustainably so important as this. The reason is India is not only the biggest country on Earth, it is also the country where Europe can prove its strength to the outside in the very fields we Europeans care about.

I have already fought my election campaigns with the clear message. I want Europe to thrive, with more strength to the outside and more freedom to the inside, of course. India, in terms of labour, in terms of people, in terms of education, in terms of skills, and in all the many other fields colleagues have already mentioned, from technology to security, is and will remain and become an even more important partner for us Europeans. So let's go for it.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Cristina Maestre (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, en estos momentos, en los que el escenario geopolÃ­tico estÃ¡ agitado y en los que algunos prefieren romper puentes en vez de tejer lazos, es lÃ³gico pretender un acuerdo de libre comercio con la India, uno de los mayores socios estratÃ©gicos de la UniÃ³n Europea. SÃ­, necesitamos socios fiables y estables, necesitamos diversificar nuestras cadenas de suministro, pero tambiÃ©n necesitamos forjar lazos cimentados no solo en interÃ©s econÃ³mico, sino tambiÃ©n en los valores de la libertad, la democracia y la sostenibilidad.

Pero este acuerdo âsi saleâ debe llevar incorporado un compromiso con los derechos humanos, el respeto al Estado de Derecho y la protecciÃ³n medioambiental. Y miren, yo creo que llegar a un acuerdo de libre comercio de aquÃ­ a fin de aÃ±o es absolutamente ciencia ficciÃ³n. En unas cuestiones tan importantes como pueden ser sectores muy sensibles, como el vehÃ­culo o la agricultura, en los que la India tiene un proteccionismo exacerbado, va a ser muy complicado. Y luego, efectivamente, Rusia es el elefante en la habitaciÃ³n.

Yo creo, seÃ±ora comisaria, que debemos procurar un acuerdo âsÃ­, intentarlo, sÃ­â, pero no llevÃ¡ndonos a Europa al marco de otros, sino defendiendo nuestros valores y mirando por nuestros sectores estratÃ©gicos. AsÃ­ que yo creo que tenemos que tener bien claras las lÃ­neas rojas. Espero que asÃ­ lo tenga la ComisiÃ³n Europea.

Â Â Engin Eroglu (Renew). â Herr PrÃ¤sident,Â sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin Lahbib! Vielen lieben Dank fÃ¼r Ihren Redebeitrag. Es geht genau darum, was Sie gesagt haben. Aus der Sicht des Parlaments ist klar: Der Schritt kommt viel zu spÃ¤t. Wir â auch die Mitgliedstaaten â hÃ¤tten schon viel, viel frÃ¼her mit Indien besser zusammenarbeiten mÃ¼ssen. Das haben wir hier im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament, aber auch in den Mitgliedstaaten versÃ¤umt. Nun ist es aber auch klar: Wir dÃ¼rfen auch nicht blind sein, wir dÃ¼rfen unsere GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit nicht verlieren.

Ja, wir wollen hier im Haus mehr mit Indien zusammenarbeiten. Wir wollen Indien sozusagen als groÃe Demokratie, als grÃ¶Ãte Demokratie mit uns weiterentwickeln in einer WinâWinâSituation. Damit meine ich ganz klar: Wenn wir Konzerne aus der ganzen Welt mit Sanktionen belegen, weil sie den Krieg von Russland unterstÃ¼tzen, dann mÃ¼ssen wir das auch in Indien machen, wenn es zu einem Freihandelsabkommen kommt.

Ich sage ganz deutlich: Wir wollen das Freihandelsabkommen mit Indien haben. Aber ganz klar: Unsere GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit als Demokraten dÃ¼rfen wir dabei nicht verlieren. Bitte tun Sie Ihr Bestes dafÃ¼r, ein vernÃ¼nftiges Abkommen mit Indien abzuschlieÃen. Wir im Parlament werden Sie dabei unterstÃ¼tzen.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ zu antworten.)

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. â Mr President, in accordance with our Rules of Procedure, I would also like to be able to react to the reply that my colleague will give me.

Vielen Dank, Herr Kollege, dass Sie die blaue Karte dieses Mal akzeptiert haben. Ich habe Ihnen sehr aufmerksam zugehÃ¶rt, und Sie haben gesagt, dass wir unsere Beziehungen mit Indien vertiefen mÃ¼ssen. Was mich interessiert: Sehen Sie das auch so wie ich, dass wir das vor allen Dingen auch deshalb tun mÃ¼ssen, um eben ein Gleichgewicht zu der Ausweitung der Macht, die der chinesische Staat gerade in dieser Region betreibt, aber auch fÃ¼r uns als EuropÃ¤ische Union zu habenÂ âÂ gerade in Ihrer Funktion als Delegationsleiter?

Â Â Engin Eroglu (Renew), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Herr Sieper, vielen lieben Dank fÃ¼r Ihre Frage. Die China-Frage ist ein ganz wichtiger Punkt. Wir haben gesehen, dass bei dem BRICSâTreffen ja innige Umarmungen stattgefunden haben. Auch da mÃ¼ssen wir aufpassen. Letztendlich haben wir, glaube ich â das hatte ich ja eben in meiner Rede klar gesagt â, den Fehler gemacht und Indien nicht richtig behandelt in den vergangenen Jahren.

Deswegen sage ich ganz klar und deutlich: Wir mÃ¼ssen mit einem Land wie Indien, das Ã¤hnliche Werte hat wie wir â ich wÃ¼rde nicht sagen die gleichen Werte â, enger zusammenarbeiten. Wir brauchen dieses Freihandelsabkommen, aber wir dÃ¼rfen unsere GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit dabei nicht verlieren. Wenn wir unsere GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit verlieren, dann verlieren wir am Ende des Tages auch unsere Demokratie. Das gilt es auch in Indien und auch in China zu verteidigen.

Â Â President. â Mr Sieper,Â I would remind you that it's not your right to have an answer, but exceptionally I will give you 30Â seconds to answer.

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Herr Kollege, ich kann Ihnen auch da nur zustimmen. Ich hÃ¤tte mich gefreut, wenn Sie meine Frage tatsÃ¤chlich auch beantwortet hÃ¤tten. Ich entnehme dem, was Sie zwischen den Zeilen gesagt haben, jedoch, dass Sie das auch so sehen. Ich glaube, unsere GlaubwÃ¼rdigkeit hÃ¤ngt vor allen Dingen auch davon ab, dass wir, nur weil wir jetzt enger mit Indien zusammenarbeiten wollen und das eben auch tun sollten, trotzdem nicht Ã¼ber die Dinge hinwegsehen, die uns in der Vergangenheit davon abgehalten haben. Das sind Menschenrechtsfragen, das sind Verbraucherschutzfragen, das sind all diese ganzen Fragen, die hinter den Werten stehen, die wir als EuropÃ¤ische Union haben.

Â Â Carla Tavares (S&D). â Senhor Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, a Nova Agenda EstratÃ©gica UniÃ£o-Ãndia Ã© um passo importante que permite reforÃ§ar a cooperaÃ§Ã£o entre as duas maiores democracias do mundo em Ã¡reas como o comÃ©rcio, a tecnologia e a defesa.

Nova Deli Ã© um ator central no diÃ¡logo com diversas regiÃµes que irÃ£o permitir diversificar os parceiros comerciais e tecnolÃ³gicos da UniÃ£o Europeia, alÃ©m dos Estados Unidos e da China. Â Â Morten LÃ¸kkegaard (Renew). â Hr. formand! Lad os nu slÃ¥ det fast fra starten af. Europa og Indien har brug for en handelsaftale. Punktum. Det handler om at styrke Europas Ã¸konomi, men det handler ogsÃ¥ om at binde Indien tÃ¦ttere til os alle sammen i en usikker verden. Indien er som sagt verdens stÃ¸rste demokrati og en Ã¸konomi i hastig vÃ¦kst. Et stÃ¦rkere partnerskab med Indien vil selvfÃ¸lgelig fremme den grÃ¸nne omstilling, innovationen og lÃ¸fte flere hundrede millioner indere ud af fattigdom. Og det tror jeg ikke, der kan vÃ¦re nogen uenighed om, selv om vi har forskellige opfattelser af, hvad der skal til her i salen. Som formand for Indien-delegationen i den tidligere periode lÃ¦rte jeg det, og jeg lÃ¦rte ogsÃ¥, at det er vigtigt at tale deres sprog. Det er vigtigt - ikke bare sprogligt, men ogsÃ¥ politisk og kulturelt. Indien er et stolt og ambitiÃ¸st folk og tÃ¦nker i partnerskaber, ikke i almisser. Hvis vi skal have succes, mÃ¥ vi mÃ¸de dem som ligevÃ¦rdige partnere og bygge tillid over tid. SelvfÃ¸lgelig skal vi ikke vÃ¦re naive. Indien rÃ¦kker hÃ¥nden ud mod Europa, men holder fast i Moskva og Beijing med den anden. Det er jeg klar over. Men vi skal lave en aftale pÃ¥ europÃ¦iske prÃ¦misser med klare krav om vÃ¦rdier og sikkerhed. Men vi skal ogsÃ¥ vÃ¦re klar over, at inderne selvfÃ¸lgelig ogsÃ¥ forlanger noget. Men lad os nu gribe dagen og fÃ¥ den aftale i stand med inderne.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â SeÃ¡n Kelly (PPE). â A UachtarÃ¡in, in today's increasingly multipolar and complex world, the European Union benefits from cultivating strong and reliable partnerships in order to continue serving as a global standardâbearer for liberal democracy. India, soon to be the world's thirdâlargest economy, stands out as a natural partner in this effort.

The strategic agenda and upcoming free trade agreement will help to solidify a diverse and complementary relationship. European farmers and businesses can benefit from India's massive demand for dairy products, digital services and pharmaceuticals, to name but a few areas. Additionally, the planned legal migration pathways in the agenda should be welcomed and used as a template for controlled, skilled migration, which our businesses and health services badly need.

Differences remain, including over India's ties with Russia, that provide a further justification for cultivating a closer friendship with India. Protecting Ukraine and protecting Europe remain the top priority, but delivering a strong partnership with India is the most desirable aspiration.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kristian Vigenin (S&D). â MrÂ President,Â Commissioner, colleagues, today we discuss the partnership between the two largest democratic unions in the world â that's the European Union and the Indian Union.

Our relationship has been forged over the years, yielding remarkable achievements in trade, investment, scientific collaboration and cultural exchange. Now, in a rapidly changing world, it is imperative that the European Union and India deepen their cooperation in critical areas that will shape the future.

Our commitment to peace, respect for international law, multilateralism and a rules-based international order must be unwavering. Together, we can champion diplomacy, foster stability, and uphold the principles that have long ensured global security and prosperity.

Climate change remains an existential threat. Our partnership in clean energy, sustainable development and environmental protection is crucial for securing a liveable planet for future generations.

Artificial intelligence presents both tremendous opportunities and significant risks. By aligning our approaches to regulate it, the EU and India can ensure AI development is ethical, transparent and respects fundamental rights.

As digital technologies underpin every aspect of our societies, we also must work together to build resilient digital infrastructures and establish common standards to protect privacy and security.

(The speaker concluded in a non-EU language)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Mika Aaltola (PPE). â MrÂ President, Commissioner, at a time of profound geopolitical change, strengthening EU-India relations is a strategic imperative. If we have any strategic sense left, we will move towards that goal.

India has been and should continue to be a counterweight to China. India â the world's fastest growing major economy, the largest democracy â is a natural partner in building resilience, diversifying trade and shaping a more balanced global order. The EU-India free trade agreement lies at the core of this effort.

Yet, partnership cannot come at the expense of principles. India's continued engagement with Russia â a regime waging a war of aggression against a European state â must be addressed openly. A true partnership is built on trust, responsibility and a shared vision for global stability.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Î Î¿Î¹ÎºÎ¿Î½Î¿Î¼Î¹ÎºÏÏ Î´Î¹Î¬Î´ÏÎ¿Î¼Î¿Ï ÎÎ½Î´Î¯Î±Ï-ÎÎ­ÏÎ·Ï ÎÎ½Î±ÏÎ¿Î»Î®Ï-ÎÏÏÏÏÎ·Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î· ÏÏÎ¿Î³ÏÎ±ÏÎ® ÎµÎ¼ÏÎ¿ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ®Ï ÏÏÎ¼ÏÏÎ½Î¯Î±Ï Î¸Î± ÎµÎ¯Î½Î±Î¹ ÏÏÎ¿Ï ÏÏÎµÎ»Î¿Ï ÏÏÎ½ ÎµÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÏÎ½ ÎµÏÎ¹ÏÎµÎ¹ÏÎ®ÏÎµÏÎ½ ÎºÎ±Î¹ ÏÏÎ½ ÎÏÏÏÏÎ±Î¯ÏÎ½ ÏÎ¿Î»Î¹ÏÏÎ½, Î±Î»Î»Î¬ Î· ÏÏÎ½ÎµÏÎ³Î±ÏÎ¯Î± Î±ÏÏÎ® ÏÏÎ­ÏÎµÎ¹ Î½Î± Î³Î¯Î½ÎµÎ¹ Î¼Îµ ÏÎµÎ²Î±ÏÎ¼Ï ÏÏÎ± ÎµÏÏÏÏÎ±ÏÎºÎ¬ ÏÏÏÏÏÏÎ± ÏÎ¿Î¹ÏÏÎ·ÏÎ±Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î±ÏÏÎ¬Î»ÎµÎ¹Î±Ï ÎºÎ±Î¹ Î¼Îµ ÏÏÎ±Î³Î¼Î±ÏÎ¹ÎºÎ® ÏÏÏÏÎ²Î±ÏÎ· ÏÏÎ¹Ï Î±Î³Î¿ÏÎ­Ï.

Â Â Angelika Winzig (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Frau Kommissarin! Einerseits sind wir mit Indien seit 2004 strategische Partner, aber andererseits haben wir auch klare Meinungsverschiedenheiten, ob es um den Erwerb von russischem ErdÃ¶l geht, aber auch um die gemeinsamen MilitÃ¤rÃ¼bungen mit Belarus und Russland. Da sich unsere Welt aber in einem tiefgreifenden geopolitischen Wandel befindet, braucht Europa strategische Partnerschaften mit LÃ¤ndern, die fÃ¼r Wettbewerb und Innovation stehen.

Indien hat mit seiner jungen, gut ausgebildeten BevÃ¶lkerung groÃes Potenzial fÃ¼r eine Zusammenarbeit mit Europa. Auch das Freihandelsabkommen fÃ¼r offenen, freien Handel mit Indien sollte man jetzt dringend in Angriff nehmen. Mit der neuen strategischen Agenda schaffen wir die Grundlage fÃ¼r eine Partnerschaft, die sowohl wirtschaftlich als auch technologisch sehr wertvoll sein wird.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). â MrÂ President, MadamÂ Commissioner, the new strategic EU-India agenda is a timely and adequate response to the process of weakening multilateralism and fragmentation of the international order. The geopolitical competition between the United States and China is reshaping trade and international relations, affecting all of us. Therefore, if we don't want to remain a mere spectator, we need to adapt and move fast.

It is in that context that we need to understand this new approach to India as well, for example, as the strategic partnership with Latin America that we will discuss later on. Of course, we are aware that there are areas of disagreement with India â a BRICS founding member. Yet, if we manage to get an FTA with India to boost defence and technology cooperation and improve connectivity, current differences could be overcome by the geopolitical gains of a mutually beneficial relationship.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

PRESIDENZA: PINA PICIERNO
Vicepresidente

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Åukasz Kohut (PPE). â Pani PrzewodniczÄca! Pani Komisarz! Z liczbami trudno dyskutowaÄ. Unia â 450 mln obywateli, Indie â pÃ³Åtora miliarda ludzi. Demografia jest nieubÅagana. GÅÄbsza integracja i wyjÅcie z egoizmÃ³w narodowych, rewolucja social mediowa, lockdown, rosyjska napaÅÄ na UkrainÄ, zbrodnie na Bliskim Wschodzie czy izolacjonizm w USA â Åwiat siÄ caÅkowicie zmieniÅ na naszych oczach. I wÅaÅnie dlatego potrzebujemy silnych i stabilnych demokracji. Potrzebujemy krajÃ³w, ktÃ³re stawiajÄ czoÅa zbrodniczym reÅ¼imom. Åwiat potrzebuje Indii stojÄcych po stronie wartoÅci demokratycznych. Hindusi doÅwiadczyli bezwzglÄdnej, obcej wÅadzy, chcÄcej meblowaÄ ich Å¼ycie. I tym bardziej dziwi, Å¼e Delhi wciÄÅ¼ kupuje surowce od Rosji, od wspÃ³Åczesnych neokolonialistÃ³w. Mam nadziejÄ, Å¼e Indie siÄ zreflektujÄ. WierzÄ, Å¼e bliÅ¼sze sÄ im liberalne wartoÅci niÅ¼ autorytaryzm, przeciwko ktÃ³remu tak dÅugo siÄ buntowali.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â Madam President, dear colleagues, India is one of the world's fastestâgrowing major economies, with GDP growth of more than 7Â % in the first half of this year. Their demographic strength, rising technological capabilities and also their big economy show how important this country is.

For the EU, a new strategic agenda with India can offer opportunities in trade and investment. As India's global influence rises, so do its responsibilities toward stability, peace and respect for a rulesâbased world order. And here India must show that India is going the way we did already. They have to diversify their energy supplies away from Russia. Europe has undertaken this painful but necessary shift, and I think we have to discuss it with India, so that they also reduce their dependence on Russia.

India and the EU are natural partners, as I see it. They are democracies with shared interests and a global reach.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Angelika Niebler (PPE). â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Indien ist nicht nur ein faszinierendes Land, sondern fÃ¼r uns EuropÃ¤er auch kÃ¼nftig ein ganz, ganz wichtiger strategischer Partner. 1,4 Milliarden Menschen, die viertgrÃ¶Ãte Volkswirtschaft der Welt, ein demokratisches System: All das spricht dafÃ¼r, dass wir unsere Beziehungen mit Indien intensivieren. Deshalb begrÃ¼Ãe ich die neue strategische Agenda. Ich hoffe sehr und wÃ¼nsche der Kommission auch sehr, sehr viel GlÃ¼ck, dass bis Ende des Jahres das Freihandelsabkommen unterzeichnet werden kann. Und ich glaube, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, wir mÃ¼ssen auch in anderen Bereichen noch die Zusammenarbeit intensivieren: im Bereich Technologie und Fortschritt, im Bereich Sicherheit und Verteidigung, im Luftverkehrsbereich. All das ist angedacht, und das ist gut so.

Und lassen Sie mich auch sagen: Strategische Partner, Freunde kÃ¶nnen auch offene Worte miteinander finden, kÃ¶nnen auch schwierige Themen ansprechen. Kollegen haben es ja schon aufgegriffen: Indiens VerhÃ¤ltnis zu Russland, zu Belarus, die Frage der Menschenrechte â all das sind Themen, die wir natÃ¼rlich auch aufgreifen mÃ¼ssen, gerade wir im EuropÃ¤ischen Parlament. Aber nichtsdestotrotz: Das Wichtigste ist erst mal, hier eine enge strategische Kooperation auf die Reihe zu kriegen. Und dann kÃ¶nnen wir auch einen offenen, intensiven Austausch und Diskurs fÃ¼hren, gerade auch Ã¼ber die Themen, die fÃ¼r uns besonders wichtig sind.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael McNamara (Renew). â Madam President, Madam Commissioner, colleagues, at this time when instability and change are the only constants, I think it is vital that the European Union look across the world and try to reach out to countries with which it shares values and look to accentuate that which it shares, rather than accentuate differences.

India has much in common with the European Union and its Member States. It cherishes democracy. It cherishes the rule of law â it has doneÂ since its foundation. It also builds its future on education, trying to move forward, trying to build technology in that regard. With regard to the rule of law it shares a common law legal system with at least one Member State, namely my own.

But in looking to develop a future with India, it would be really foolhardy of us to lecture India on where it should position itself in the geopolitics of the world. India has always pursued a middle way. When there were revolutions and wars on its frontiers, it pursued a middle way. It will pursue its own course, whether we like it or not. The question is whether we can respect and value India as much as it deserves as a trading partner to be respected and valued. Not lecture and hector to it, but rather to respect it and try to move forward together and to accentuate that which we share, rather than our differences.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â MadamÂ President, honourable Members, today's debate shows the importance and challenges also lying ahead for EU-India relations. But one thing is very clear: 60 years after establishing diplomatic ties, Europe and India are ready to write a new chapter â practical, ambitious and accountable as well.

Our joint communication is not a manifesto to be framed, it is a plan to be implemented. While in the coming days we will adopt Council conclusions on India, we will soon propose a joint EU-India comprehensive strategic agenda for adoption at our next summit, which will demonstrate the commitment of our Indian partners to this joint cooperation.

This partnership goes beyond business or tech. It backs open societies and markets, open science, clean energy for growth, secure seas for trade and mobile talent that can learn and thrive. India's success strengthens Europe. Europe's success supports India. And our success together strengthens something larger still: a rules-based international order, where sovereignty is respected, climate goals are met and prosperity is broadly shared.

However, we do not ignore the challenges ahead. Yes, India is an important importer of Russian oil; unjust participation in the Zapad military exercise, albeit at a much lower level than originally planned â our concerns have been directly communicated to the Indian authorities. At the same time, the Commission is convinced that engagement is the right way forward. Prime Minister Modi, in a recent call with our President, reaffirmed that India opposes Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine and that now is the moment to deepen cooperation with Europe.

The 21st century will be shaped by partnerships that dare to deliver, so let us choose delivery over rhetoric. The future is EU-India and the future begins now.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â La discussione Ã¨ chiusa.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Tomasz Froelich (ESN), schriftlich. â UnipolaritÃ¤t war gestern, MultipolaritÃ¤t ist heute. Indien spielt in dieser neuen Weltordnung eine wichtige Rolle: Indiens Wirtschaft wÃ¤chst, politisch ist das Land stabil. Das ist auch der FÃ¼hrung von Narendra Modi zu verdanken. Leider hat Europa die Partnerschaft mit Indien lange Zeit vernachlÃ¤ssigt - zugunsten Chinas und der USA. Das hat sich bis in die Gegenwart kaum geÃ¤ndert. Dadurch entgehen uns Chancen. Denn souverÃ¤n ist, wer Alternativen hat. Indien ist eine solche Alternative. Wenn wir solchen aufstrebenden MÃ¤chten den RÃ¼cken kehren, isolieren wir uns am Ende nur selbst - und wenn wir die Menschenrechtsideologie und moralische Belehrungen statt gutem Handel ins Zentrum unserer bilateralen Beziehungen stellen, erst recht; und zwar sowohl Ã¶konomisch, als auch diplomatisch. Wir mÃ¼ssen unsere AuÃenpolitik endlich multivektoral ausrichten. Und hierbei kommen wir an Neu-Delhi nicht vorbei: Indien ist mehr als nur ein wichtiger Absatzmarkt, dessen Potentiale wir noch nicht ausgeschÃ¶pft haben, sondern auch ein strategischer Partner in Fragen der Technologie, Energie und im Kampf gegen den Terror. Es wird hÃ¶chste Zeit, dass wir die Partnerschaft mit Indien ausbauen.

Â 

15. EU political strategy on Latin America (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â Presidente. â L'ordine del giorno reca la relazione di Davor Ivo Stier, a nome della commissione per gli affari esteri, Strategia politica dell'UE per l'America latina (2025/2083(INI)) (A10-0171/2025).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Davor Ivo Stier, ponente. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, con esta ResoluciÃ³n vamos a definir las prioridades y el mensaje del Parlamento con vistas a la prÃ³xima cumbre UE-CELAC. En un mundo con menos multilateralismo y mÃ¡s fragmentaciÃ³n, AmÃ©rica Latina no puede quedar al margen de nuestro pensamiento estratÃ©gico. Por el contrario, debe ser comprendida como nuestro aliado natural, con el cual compartimos no solamente siglos de historia, sino tambiÃ©n los mismos valores y el reto de adquirir una autonomÃ­a estratÃ©gica evitando dependencias de otras potencias globales.

Sin esta alianza con AmÃ©rica Latina, Europa no serÃ¡ nunca un sujeto geopolÃ­tico; y, sin estrechar lazos con la UniÃ³n Europea, AmÃ©rica Latina tampoco podrÃ¡ alcanzar su autonomÃ­a estratÃ©gica. Por ello abogamos por una alianza birregional, un Ã¡rea transatlÃ¡ntica europeo-latinoamericana de paz, democracia y prosperidad.

Al igual que nosotros, los pueblos de AmÃ©rica aman la libertad, y este debe ser el concepto ordenador de nuestras relaciones. Libertad para poder relacionar mÃ¡s estrechamente nuestras sociedades, nuestras economÃ­as, con acuerdos como el alcanzado con Chile, MÃ©xico o Mercosur. La conclusiÃ³n y aplicaciÃ³n de estos acuerdos enviarÃ¡n al mundo un claro mensaje de que las democracias pueden comerciar entre sÃ­ libremente, sin levantar barreras arancelarias, pero sÃ­ respetando los derechos humanos, los laborales, los del consumidor, protegiendo el medio ambiente.

La libertad incluye tambiÃ©n la liberaciÃ³n del flagelo del narcotrÃ¡fico, la corrupciÃ³n y el crimen organizado. Y por ello buscamos intensificar la cooperaciÃ³n birregional en materia de seguridad y expandirla en el Ã¡mbito de la justicia.

Es tambiÃ©n claro que Europa debe ayudar a dar respuesta a las causas profundas de muchos de estos males y, por lo tanto, no podemos renunciar al financiamiento del desarrollo en AmÃ©rica Latina. En este sentido, Global Gateway es un elemento esencial de nuestra polÃ­tica, particularmente en la atracciÃ³n de inversiones en sectores estratÃ©gicos. Pero es necesaria una mayor transparencia, hace falta control parlamentario y, ademÃ¡s, no se debe desatender el objetivo primordial de la polÃ­tica de desarrollo, que es erradicar la pobreza.

La libertad como principio ordenador nos impone tambiÃ©n actuar de manera mÃ¡s decisiva en defensa de la democracia y contra el autoritarismo. Por ello, exigimos la liberaciÃ³n de todos los presos polÃ­ticos. Hoy en particular, hago referencia a Jan Darmovzal, ciudadano checo, y a los mÃ¡s de ochocientos presos polÃ­ticos en Venezuela, varios de ellos ciudadanos europeos. Demandamos que la dictadura de Maduro cese de inmediato la represiÃ³n a la sociedad civil y a la oposiciÃ³n.

Condenamos tambiÃ©n la participaciÃ³n de mercenarios en la agresiÃ³n rusa contra Ucrania y demandamos al Consejo que imponga sanciones contra los responsables de la persistente violaciÃ³n de derechos humanos en Cuba. Buscamos asimismo la activaciÃ³n de la clÃ¡usula democrÃ¡tica por la sistemÃ¡tica violaciÃ³n de derechos humanos en Nicaragua y la expansiÃ³n de las sanciones contra el clan Ortega-Murillo.

Si bien estas son las peores dictaduras, vemos tambiÃ©n con preocupaciÃ³n la erosiÃ³n de los valores democrÃ¡ticos, del sistema de pesos y contrapesos, en otros paÃ­ses de la regiÃ³n, notablemente en El Salvador. Por ello, buscamos que en la cumbre de Santa Marta se incluya un debate sobre los principios democrÃ¡ticos y los derechos humanos y que la declaraciÃ³n final defienda claramente nuestros valores.

En la ResoluciÃ³n incluimos diecisÃ©is recomendaciones concretas para que en Santa Marta se acuerde una cooperaciÃ³n mÃ¡s estrecha en defensa de la democracia, la paz, la seguridad, la prosperidad y el desarrollo sostenible invirtiendo en transiciÃ³n digital, energÃ­as renovables, educaciÃ³n y salud, y tambiÃ©n en bienestar social con un pacto de cuidados birregional. Buscamos la creaciÃ³n de un espacio institucional permanente que, entre cumbre y cumbre, coordine los esfuerzos y efectÃºe un seguimiento del progreso alcanzado.

Sin ignorar los desafÃ­os, Europa y AmÃ©rica Latina estÃ¡n llamadas hoy a tomar un puesto mÃ¡s protagÃ³nico en las relaciones internacionales. Esta ResoluciÃ³n es una contribuciÃ³n en ese sentido y un llamado a la acciÃ³n para desarrollar entre ambas regiones un Ã¡rea transatlÃ¡ntica de paz, democracia y prosperidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n, ponente de opiniÃ³n de la ComisiÃ³n DEVE. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, en unas semanas celebraremos la Cumbre UE-CELAC y es una enorme oportunidad para estrechar las relaciones con una regiÃ³n del mundo con la que compartimos los mismos valores, la misma forma de entender el mundo y los mismos intereses. Dicho de otra manera, es una regiÃ³n del mundo a la que conocemos, a la que reconocemos y con la que tenemos los mismos intereses.

Pero la cumbre debe ser una oportunidad para pasar de los valores a las cosas concretas. Tenemos que acometer juntos la reforma del sistema financiero internacional y condonar la deuda de paÃ­ses de renta media, como los del Caribe, por ejemplo. TambiÃ©n acordar e impulsar definitivamente y aprobar los acuerdos con Mercosur y MÃ©xico, que van a impulsar nuestras relaciones comerciales, pero tambiÃ©n garantizar nuestros derechos ambientales.

Debemos invertir mÃ¡s en la cooperaciÃ³n a travÃ©s de Global Gateway y tambiÃ©n de la ayuda humanitaria. Tenemos que crear un foro de sociedad civil para que la sociedad civil participe. Y, por Ãºltimo, queremos tambiÃ©n un pacto birregional por los cuidados que ponga en el corazÃ³n de la agenda polÃ­tica los cuidados. No quiero acabar sin el consejo de mi amigo Forges: Â«No te olvides de HaitÃ­Â». Porque HaitÃ­ es el paÃ­s mÃ¡s pobre de AmÃ©rica Latina y requiere de nuestra atenciÃ³n y de nuestro compromiso.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, allow me first of all to congratulate you on this report. We see eye to eye with the European Parliament on the importance of the partnership with Latin America and the Caribbean. The world is changing fast. Geopolitical tensions are rising. The very principles of cooperation and dialogue are at stake. At this moment, the partnership between the European Union and the Latin American and the Caribbean is not only useful, it is essential. We do not always agree with the Latin American and Caribbean countries, but they are still the world's closest regions, with most of the region sharing our core values: democracy, human rights and the rule of law.

During the UNGA highâlevel week in New York in September, the HR/VP, together with her Colombian counterpart, chaired a meeting of the EU and CELAC foreign ministers, bringing together 60Â countries â one third of the United Nations â in the EU delegation in New York. The political signal was very clear: we are committed to building a stronger partnership to face the challenges ahead, be it the security of our societies, the defence of our territorial integrity, diversification, boosting trade and investment, the future of AI or the fight against climate change, we need to work together.

Since the EU summit held in Brussels in 2023, our relationship has advanced steadily: over 30Â highâlevel EU visits to the region, more dialogue and tangible progress on sensitive issues such as citizens' security and the fight against organised crime. The benefits of our cooperation speak for themselves. We are the first investor with accumulated FDI with stocks similar to the combined investment of China, India, Japan and Russia. We are the thirdâlargest trading partner and a key cooperation partner of the region. Trade between our regions, based on a dense network of agreements, has grown by more than 45Â % since 2013, exceeding EURÂ 410Â billion annually to date. Trade has increased by over 150Â % between the EU and our Central American partners, with whom we held the first ever Association Council in July.

We have the densest network of agreements compared to any region in the world. This network has been reinforced with the new agreement with Chile, and we hope the European Parliament and all Member States will support the new modernised agreement with Mexico and the new agreement with Mercosur.

Through Global Gateway, with 45Â billion mobilised until 2027, we are driving strategic projects in renewable energy, digital transformation and sustainable infrastructure, from power interconnections to satellite connectivity, and from clean technologies to tackling sargassum in the Caribbean.

The summit with CELAC in Colombia in November will be an opportunity to deepen our cooperation.

Our focus rests on strengthening democracy and multilateralism, because we must work with the region to defend democracy. Here, your role in parliamentary diplomacy, together with our electoral observation missions â four in the region this year â is really essential. As is advancing the green digital and social transitions, with a focus on energy; boosting trade and investment to improve our resilience, because in a world tempted by tariffs and protectionism, we choose more trade and more investment to promote growth and opportunities; and stepping up cooperation in citizens' security and against organised crime, because our citizens demand more joint action against violence and organised crime.

Finally, our partnership is not just about governments, it is about people. That is why we will organise, in the margins of the summit, sideâevents with civil society and the business community.

Our partnership with the region is a strategic asset. Let us make it stronger. The European Parliament is an important ally in this endeavour. I am looking forward to listening to your views.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Gabriel Mato, en nombre del Grupo PPE. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, a ninguno de ustedes les sorprenderÃ¡ oÃ­rme hablar de la importancia que tiene AmÃ©rica Latina para Europa. Pero la reiteraciÃ³n no lo hace ni menos cierto ni menos urgente. Este informe es la prueba de que este Parlamento al fin entiende la necesidad que tenemos de AmÃ©rica Latina. La UniÃ³n Europea quiere desarrollar una polÃ­tica exterior efectiva. Empecemos por lo fÃ¡cil, consolidemos nuestra relaciÃ³n con AmÃ©rica Latina que en cierto modo ya nos ha esperado mucho tiempo.

Si queremos ser capaces de competir con China, con Rusia, con los Estados Unidos, por la amistad de las repÃºblicas latinoamericanas y sus sociedades, no podemos contentarnos con medias tintas. Tenemos que volcarnos hacia la regiÃ³n y tenemos que ser constantes. Los acuerdos comerciales son fundamentales, pero son solo el principio. Debemos combatir conjuntamente aquellos males que nos afligen a ambos y tampoco podemos ignorar el retroceso democrÃ¡tico en la regiÃ³n. Los regÃ­menes de Venezuela, Cuba y Nicaragua son un claro ejemplo de ello.

La Cumbre UniÃ³n Europea-CELAC puede ser un dÃ­a que pase a la historia o puede unirse a la lamentable lista de cumbres medio vacÃ­as que no logran nada. Yo tengo clara cuÃ¡l debe ser nuestra apuesta. Por eso quiero llamar a todos los jefes de Estado y de Gobierno para que no desaprovechen esta oportunidad.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Javi LÃ³pez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, este informe tan bien llevado por el ponente Stier envÃ­a un mensaje claro a las puertas de la Cumbre UniÃ³n Europea-CELAC. El Parlamento Europeo apuesta por una relaciÃ³n estratÃ©gica con AmÃ©rica Latina, una relaciÃ³n que creemos que es un imperativo geopolÃ­tico, un acierto estratÃ©gico y una oportunidad econÃ³mica.

GeopolÃ­ticamente, en un mundo fragmentado, hostil y muy competitivo, podemos, de la mano de los latinoamericanos, defender el multilateralismo, defender un comercio abierto y con normas, y proteger nuestras democracias. Podemos, estratÃ©gicamente, defender nuestra autonomÃ­a estratÃ©gica, reduciendo asÃ­ dependencias, y, al mismo tiempo, una oportunidad econÃ³mica, materias primas, industrializaciÃ³n, transferencia tecnolÃ³gica. Todo eso, Â¿cÃ³mo? Institucionalizando mejor y coordinando mejor nuestras posiciones en el mundo. Por eso la cumbre es tan relevante, UniÃ³n Europea-CELAC. Necesitamos un nexo permanente en este sentido. Al mismo tiempo, tenemos los acuerdos de asociaciÃ³n, el acuerdo con Mercosur, que es el que va a marcar la diferencia en la regiÃ³n, y la modernizaciÃ³n del acuerdo con MÃ©xico.

Y por Ãºltimo utilizar mejor nuestros socios estratÃ©gicos, Brasil y MÃ©xico, enormes gigantes en el mundo diplomÃ¡tica y econÃ³micamente. Sin duda, lo que queremos es pasar de una vieja amistad a una alianza estratÃ©gica para empujar el mundo en la buena direcciÃ³n y defender nuestros legÃ­timos intereses en un mundo fragmentado y muy competitivo.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hermann Tertsch, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±ora presidente, este informe pudo ser otra cosa, pero vuelve a ser una cruel estafa de este Parlamento a las naciones de IberoamÃ©rica, que sufren la represiÃ³n, la violencia, el terror y la miseria del narcosocialismo, como la cumbre de la CELAC âese invento que se hicieron en todos los movimientos del chavismo y del narcosocialismo para quitarse de encima la OEAâ, que volverÃ¡ a ser una estafa mÃ¡s.

Y lo es porque el Partido Popular vuelve a preferir aliarse con una izquierda cÃ³mplice de todos los tiranos y jefes del crimen organizado. Sin la verdad no se arregla ninguno de los problemas que tenemos en esta relaciÃ³n, que debiera ser otra. Este informe no pide la suspensiÃ³n de la inaudita financiaciÃ³n europea a la dictadura comunista de Cuba. AÃºn califica el rÃ©gimen usurpador de Maduro como Gobierno, cuando es el criminal cartel de los Soles. No exige a la UniÃ³n Europea y a sus miembros que reconozcan al legÃ­timo presidente venezolano, Edmundo GonzÃ¡lez Urrutia. No denuncia la criminal deriva de Gustavo Petro hacia la narcodictadura. Ignora la brutal represiÃ³n desatada por el presidente Lula da Silva en Brasil, con miles de presos polÃ­ticos, como ignora las maniobras de Xiomara Castro en Honduras. Este informe estÃ¡ saturado de mantras globalistas de la Agenda 2030, izquierdismo tonto que genera problemas artificiales y no resuelve ninguno de la vida real de la gente en IberoamÃ©rica.

El Partido Popular no ha querido una mayorÃ­a posible contra el narcosocialismo. Ha preferido hacer un informe con los socialistas cÃ³mplices de las fuerzas criminales en IberoamÃ©rica. Pues asÃ­ ha salido.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Carlo Fidanza, a nome del gruppo ECR. â Signora Presidente,Â onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria,Â oggi, in troppi paesi latinoamericani, i valori di libertÃ  e democrazia sono minacciati da regimi autoritari e dall'influenza crescente di potenze ostili. Mentre Cuba, Venezuela e Nicaragua continuano a reprimere oppositori e a violare i diritti fondamentali, l'Unione europea sceglie sempre la via piÃ¹ comoda, quella dei compromessi al ribasso e del timore di scomodare gli autocrati.

Ma sulla libertÃ  non si negozia. Per questo abbiamo presentato proposte per rafforzare questa risoluzione, chiedendo con chiarezza di sospendere l'accordo con Cuba e di designare il CÃ¡rtel de los Soles con il suo capo Maduro come organizzazione terroristica, come hanno fatto l'amministrazione Trump e altri governi della regione.

Ma Ã¨ anche il tempo di occuparci delle gravissime violazioni dello Stato di diritto in corso in Brasile contro gli esponenti dell'opposizione conservatrice da parte del duo Lula e de Moraes.

Solo un'Europa che ritrovi il coraggio della veritÃ  potrÃ  difendere davvero la libertÃ , dentro e fuori i propri confini.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Cowen, on behalf of the Renew Group. â MadamÂ President, in these uncertain times for global trade and international relations, building stronger partnerships matters more than ever. As the United States turns inwards, Europe must strengthen its position in regions such as Latin America in order to promote our values of democracy and free trade, and to ensure we don't fall behind other interested parties such as China.

My own country of Ireland already enjoys a EURÂ 1 billion trade surplus with the Mercosur nations of Latin America, showing the strong potential for many European sectors, including pharmaceuticals, machinery, medical devices, dairy and spirits.

But these partnerships are not purely about profit; Latin America has still has a distance to travel before matching Europe's environmental, labour and food safety standards. Legitimate concerns remain over the proposed safeguards for agreements like the Mercosur one, especially given the projected increase of 99Â 000 tonnes of beef into the European market. The Commission must show that standards will not be sacrificed for short-term gain, and that agri-food producers will not be undermined for the benefit of other sectors.

I have said before that I will not reject any deal out of hand, but I will not support one that undermines our own citizens either. Europe's partnership with Latin America can have a bright future, but only if it is built on shared values, high standards and mutual respect.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Diana Riba i Giner, en nombre del Grupo Verts/ALE. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, ante el agitado contexto geopolÃ­tico, Europa debe afianzar alianzas estratÃ©gicas con los socios que comparten la defensa del diÃ¡logo, la paz, el progreso mutuo y un orden internacional basado en las normas, especialmente en un momento en el que nuestros supuestos socios histÃ³ricos de la UniÃ³n Europea viven una deriva autoritaria.

Por estos motivos, AmÃ©rica Latina y el Caribe deben convertirse en socios prioritarios para la UniÃ³n Europea, porque no solo compartimos con ellos vÃ­nculos histÃ³ricos, sociales y culturales, sino tambiÃ©n un renovado compromiso para promover el fortalecimiento del Estado de Derecho, la democracia y el desarrollo regional sostenible.

Pero no debemos confundir las cosas: mÃ¡s cooperaciÃ³n e integraciÃ³n no es mÃ¡s negocio para las multinacionales ni para el IBEXÂ 35, como quiere el acuerdo actual del Mercosur. Y sabÃ©is que aquÃ­ no nos vais a encontrar, pero sÃ­ para trabajar juntos, para proteger a nuestros trabajadores, agricultores y consumidores, tanto en Europa como en AmÃ©rica Latina.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Irene Montero, en nombre del Grupo The Left. â SeÃ±ora presidenta, escribiÃ³ Eduardo Galeano que los metales arrebatados a los nuevos dominios coloniales de AmÃ©rica estimularon el desarrollo econÃ³mico europeo y hasta puede decirse que lo hicieron posible. Seis siglos despuÃ©s, Europa sigue sin reconocer que AmÃ©rica Latina no fue descubierta, sino invadida. EspaÃ±a y otras potencias europeas cometieron un enorme saqueo y un genocidio y deben pedir perdÃ³n y reparar a nuestros pueblos hermanos de AmÃ©rica.

Lejos de hacerlo, la relaciÃ³n actual de Europa con AmÃ©rica Latina sigue siendo una forma de colonialismo. Las cadenas de explotaciÃ³n, iniciadas entonces, continÃºan hoy bajo nuevas formas: el extractivismo, la deuda, los tratados comerciales desiguales como Mercosur, la devastaciÃ³n ambiental, el racismo o las migraciones forzadas. Las Ã©lites europeas desean la riqueza de AmÃ©rica Latina, pero cierran fronteras, niegan derechos a las migrantes y desprecian las demandas de justicia social, soberanÃ­a alimentaria o comercio justo mientras promueven la militarizaciÃ³n disfrazada de cooperaciÃ³n en seguridad.

Y ademÃ¡s, seguimos definiendo nuestra relaciÃ³n con AmÃ©rica Latina en funciÃ³n de los intereses de los Estados Unidos, especialmente contra China. Pero Â¿por quÃ© le hacemos el trabajo sucio a Donald Trump, responsable de golpes de Estado, de desatar una guerra Ã©tnica con deportaciones masivas, del bloqueo ilegal a Cuba y de ataques a la soberanÃ­a de paÃ­ses como Colombia, Venezuela o MÃ©xico bajo la excusa del narcotrÃ¡fico? Â¿Acaso no son las farmacÃ©uticas estadounidenses y la ausencia de sanidad pÃºblica las principales responsables de la crisis del fentanilo?

Seis siglos despuÃ©s, Europa, sumisa a Trump, belicista, cÃ³mplice del genocidio en Palestina, se sigue creyendo dueÃ±a de lo que nunca le perteneciÃ³. No sÃ© cuÃ¡ndo se van a dar cuenta, comisaria, de que asÃ­ estÃ¡n cavando la tumba de esta Europa moribunda. Como dijo SimÃ³n RodrÃ­guez, o inventamos o erramos.

Â Â Markus Buchheit, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. â Frau PrÃ¤sidentin! Ja, wenn wir von Strategie hier im Hause reden, dann ist es immer ein Warnsignal. Meistens ist es bestenfalls taktisch oder operativ, was hier rauskommt. Ich mÃ¶chte dem Kollegen Tertsch nicht widersprechen, das wÃ¼rde ich mir nie anmaÃen. Aber wir sollten froh sein, dass wir doch einmal darÃ¼ber reden, wie wir unsere Position zu Lateinamerika genauer definieren wollen.

Das groÃe Problem ist doch hier: Wir reden von Demokratie und wollen dann unsere Werte verkaufen. Was ist das denn? Es ist der Green Deal, der hier gerade getarnt rÃ¼bergebracht wird. Es ist wieder Sozialismus und Planwirtschaft, die aus Europa rÃ¼berschwappen, diesmal nach SÃ¼damerika. Und ich wÃ¼rde selten meinen kommunistischen Vorrednern hier zustimmen, aber jawohl, das hat neokoloniale ZÃ¼ge. Aber Sie reden von Sicherheit und kritisieren dann El Salvador. Sie reden von wirtschaftlicher Freiheit und gehen mit keinem Wort auf Argentinien ein, auf den gigantischen Weg, den Milei hier zurÃ¼ckgelegt hat.

Was wir hier gerade Ã¼bertragen wollen, das ist ein Konzept, das wir hier in BrÃ¼ssel zur GenÃ¼ge sehen. Sie haben es heute wieder gesehenÂ â wenn wir von Demokratie reden, von RechtsstaatlichkeitÂ â, was dann passiert mit Leuten, die mit HÃ¤mmern auf andere einhauen. Das wird Ã¼bertragen. Wir wollen unsere Werte, unsere sogenannten Werte, die Ã¼berhaupt keine Zukunft haben und uns dahin gebracht haben, wo wir gerade stehenÂ â nÃ¤mlich im wirtschaftlichen und industriellen RuinÂ â, die wollen wir dorthin Ã¼bertragen â mit einer vÃ¶llig gescheiterten Energiewende.

Wir setzen uns mitnichten dafÃ¼r ein, dass es der Zivilgesellschaft besser geht, mitnichten dafÃ¼r ein, dass es den Schwachen besser geht, sondern wir Ã¼bertragen Konzepte, die sich hier schon nicht bewÃ¤hrt haben, meine Damen und Herren!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Å½eljana Zovko (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear Commissioner, Latin American partners are our natural allies. Despite these close ties, the European presence has significantly declined in recent decades, giving way to Chinese and Russian influence and enabling them to create economic dependencies that influence the foreign policy direction of Latin American countries.

It is high time to re-engage with the region on areas ranging from trade to security and supply of critical raw materials. In this regard, the EU-Mercosur Partnership Agreement is key as it will participate in EU strategy to conclude new mutually beneficial agreements with like-minded partners and will contribute to counterbalancing the influence of the EU's competitors in the region.

Likewise, the European Union must increase its coordination with Latin America to fight against criminal organisations that constitute a threat to our European security, and yes, narcotrafficking â that's a huge issue for our European citizens and we should start dealing with this.

Moreover, as a guardian of freedom of religion, the European Union must protect religious communities worldwide and cannot, therefore, remain silent in the face of the persecution of human rights defenders and members of the Catholic Church in Nicaragua. It is high time to start acting and to stop this communist persecution that we have seen during 15Â years of communist persecution in Eastern Europe. This is happening in in Latin America now, and you should act upon that.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Evin Incir (S&D). â MadamÂ President,Â the European Union's partnership with Latin America has never been more important. As the international order undergoes profound change, our cooperation must become even stronger with like-minded countries â more strategic and more ambitious.

I warmly welcome the many new political and economic agreements between our regions, but we must go further. Together, we face common threats: organised crime and drug trafficking that devastate families, endanger children and undermine the security of both our continents. The flow of illicit drugs from Latin America to Europe is not just a regional issue; it's a shared challenge that demands a united response.

I call, therefore, on the Commission to deepen cooperation between our Latin American partners and EU agencies such as Europol, CEPOL, Eurojust and EUDA. Only by standing shoulder to shoulder can we protect our citizens and secure a safer future for Europe, for Latin America and for all.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Matthieu Valet (PfE), rÃ©ponse carton bleu. â Je vous reconnais bien lÃ , l'Ã©lu LFIste. Prenez votre respiration, j'avais peur que vous fassiez une syncope. Â Â Nora Junco GarcÃ­a (ECR). â SeÃ±ora presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, voy a hablar de otra parte importante que es la cooperaciÃ³n entre a ambos lados del ocÃ©ano.

Â Â Francisco JosÃ© MillÃ¡n Mon (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, yo felicito, en primer lugar, al ponente, mi colega y amigo Davor Ivo Stier, por su muy equilibrado informe. AdemÃ¡s, avanzaremos en nuestra autonomÃ­a estratÃ©gica con estos acuerdos.

Quiero referirme a dos puntos mÃ¡s que tambiÃ©n contempla el informe. Por un lado, la denuncia de las dictaduras de Cuba, Venezuela y Nicaragua, que deben seguir siendo objeto de nuestra condena. Y por otro, la necesaria cooperaciÃ³n en la lucha contra el narcotrÃ¡fico, una preocupaciÃ³n central para muchos paÃ­ses latinoamericanos y europeos. Debemos intensificar nuestros esfuerzos contra esta lacra y reforzar la cooperaciÃ³n entre los puertos mediante mÃ¡s controles, mÃ¡s inspecciones, mÃ¡s operaciones conjuntas, mÃ¡s escÃ¡neres y mÃ¡s radares, tambiÃ©n en los puertos de AmÃ©rica Latina.

El momento de adopciÃ³n de este informe es oportuno, a un mes de la prÃ³xima Cumbre UEâCELAC en Santa Marta. Me gustarÃ­a que esta cumbre, como ya se ha dicho, no sea inÃºtil, sino que sea provechosa.

Â Â Francisco Assis (S&D). â Senhora Presidente, Senhora ComissÃ¡ria, naturalmente comungo das vÃ¡rias consideraÃ§Ãµes que jÃ¡ aqui foram feitas sobre a importÃ¢ncia do reforÃ§o da cooperaÃ§Ã£o entre a UniÃ£o Europeia e a AmÃ©rica Latina, por motivos de ordem geopolÃ­tica, econÃ³mica, civilizacional e cultural.

Ã absolutamente inaceitÃ¡vel que se estabeleÃ§a qualquer confusÃ£o entre a ditadura cubana e a democracia brasileira. O Brasil Ã© atÃ© um exemplo para o mundo, com o que se tem vindo a passar nos Ãºltimos tempos.

O que aconteceu foi o seguinte: houve um presidente que, na fase final do seu mandato, percebendo que ia perder as eleiÃ§Ãµes, elaborou um plano, tendo em vista a concretizaÃ§Ã£o de um golpe de Estado que impediria a tomada de posse do presidente eleito, neste caso do presidente Lula, e pressupunha tambÃ©m, atÃ©, o assassinato de algumas das principais figuras da vida polÃ­tica e da vida judicial do Brasil.

O plano era um plano macabro. Era um plano que avanÃ§ava no sentido do assassinato do presidente Lula e de algumas das principais figuras da polÃ­tica brasileira. Ora, o que aconteceu foi que o Supremo Tribunal Federal, no pleno exercÃ­cio das suas competÃªncias e no respeito pelo princÃ­pio da separaÃ§Ã£o dos poderes, tomou uma deliberaÃ§Ã£o, por quatro votos contra um, no sentido de considerar que JaÃ­r Bolsonaro era culpado de uma tentativa de golpe de Estado e, como tal, foi devidamente condenado. Â Â EnikÅ GyÅri (PfE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, AmÃ©rica Latina es una regiÃ³n con un enorme potencial, ademÃ¡s de ser un aliado que comparte nuestros valores. El problema es que los dirigentes de la UE han descuidado durante aÃ±os las relaciones y han apostado por los socios equivocados.

Â Â Jessika Van Leeuwen (PPE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, seÃ±ora comisaria, la cooperaciÃ³n con AmÃ©rica Latina es de gran importancia estratÃ©gica para la UniÃ³n Europea, especialmente en estos tiempos de incertidumbre geopolÃ­tica. Â Â AndrÃ© RougÃ© (PfE). â Madame la PrÃ©sidente, chers collÃ¨gues, je mâexprime ce soir en tant que membre supplÃ©ant de la dÃ©lÃ©gation pour les relations avec la RÃ©publique fÃ©dÃ©rative du BrÃ©sil, puissance qui reprÃ©sente 91Â % des Ã©changes commerciaux du Mercosur.

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â MadamÂ President, dear colleagues, Latin America is a key partner in many areas like trade, natural resources, the fight against organised crime or tackling climate change.

We also share a large common culture, history, religion, values and strong people-to-people ties, like in Spain or Portugal, and Mercosur countries are already our biggest export market â even bigger than the USA and China. Yet Europe's attention has been drifting elsewhere, leaving strategic space for China or Russia.

We face an increasingly contested international environment, and if the European Union wants to continue shaping the rules-based global order, we must act proactively. So I hope the upcoming EU-CELAC Summit in November 2025 will be a key opportunity to launch a new roadmap.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â OndÅej KolÃ¡Å (PPE). â MadamÂ President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, I am very well aware of the fact that we are discussing our approach toward the whole region, but I would like to focus on, or point out, one particular country and one overlooked fact.

The country is Venezuela and the fact is that there are 41 EU citizens from 12 EU Member States who have now, for more than a year, been imprisoned by Maduro's regime.

One of them is a Czech citizen, Jan Darmovzal. I am in contact with his parents, who are asking me every week what the EU has done to help get their son back home. Unfortunately, all I can answer to them is that I don't know. I've read letters that they have received from the Commission and from the EEAS â letters full of comforting words and, unfortunately, empty phrases. They don't need that. They need action.

I am very grateful for how the EU denounces totalitarian regimes and dictators, but that is not enough. I also want to see serious action which will result in these prisoners being brought back home, and not allowing the gangster Maduro to further blackmail us.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). â SeÃ±ora presidenta, AmÃ©rica Latina y el Caribe son socios naturales de Europa, pero tenemos que procurar unas relaciones entre iguales, con respeto, unas relaciones no entre sÃºbditos, sino unas relaciones âinsistoâ entre iguales.

AmÃ©rica Latina no es una colonia, sino una serie de paÃ­ses soberanos que deben tener relaciones diplomÃ¡ticas, culturales y econÃ³micas tambiÃ©n entre iguales, no con criterios de extractivismo o amenazas a la soberanÃ­a.

La verdad es que, viendo este Parlamento vacÃ­o, conÂ diputados que auspician golpes de Estado, ya le gustarÃ­a a alguno ser como el presidenteÂ Lula, como la presidentaÂ Claudia Sheinbaum o como el presidente Gustavo Petro, queÂ estÃ¡Â clamando contra el genocidio en Gaza. Como dijo recientemente el presidenteÂ Lula, abogando por el multilateralismo y pidiendo erradicar el hambre del mundo y generar bienestar e igualdad, es necesario redefinir el lugar de AmÃ©rica Latina en el nuevo orden global. La soberanÃ­a y la democracia son innegociables, como lo son el derecho de la autodeterminaciÃ³n de los pueblos de AmÃ©rica Latina y de los pueblos originarios.

Desde nuestro Grupo, siempre apoyaremos a la sociedad civil latinoamericana en las denuncias contra la represiÃ³n, contra las desigualdades, contra la discriminaciÃ³n y contra la vulneraciÃ³n de los derechos humanos.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hadja Lahbib, Member of the Commission. â Madam President, honourable Members, the debate today brought a number of concerns, but also a number of ideas to the table and underlined a shared ambition to build a stronger partnership with the LAC region, because we want to and because we have to.

In these perilous times, our partnership with Latin America and the Caribbean remains a steadfast pillar of global stability. Amid fleeting transactions and deals, our bond is grounded in trust and a shared commitment to longâterm cooperation. In a world that focuses on tariffs, we want more trade and more investment. Only cooperation and a joinedâup approach serve the ultimate goal, which is prosperity and stability in our regions. Together, we can reject isolationism and choose steadfast partnership over transient transactions, offering a powerful and influential joint voice advocating for peace, democracy and international law.

By deepening our cooperation and the triple transition â green, digital and social â we can forge a stronger, more enduring and sustainable partnership, reinforcing social inclusion and gender equality through initiatives like the biâregional pact on care will cement the societal benefits of our collaboration.

Security is more than ever a shared priority. With the proposed EUâLAC alliance for citizens' security, we can fortify our collaboration in fighting transnational organised crime.

Based on today's discussion, I am confident that the EU stands united in working for a summit sending a strong signal. The EU and the LAC are partners and ready to cooperate in these challenging times.

Â 
Â Â 
Â
